The executor\n     7→separately appends ``--file <path>`` for each entry in ``step.inputs``.\n     8→\"\"\"\n     9→\n    10→from __future__ import annotations\n    11→\n    12→from pathlib import Path\n    13→from typing import Literal\n    14→\n    15→from .models import AgentSpec\n    16→\n    17→_DEPTH_INSTRUCTIONS = {\n    18→    \"quick\": (\n    19→        \"Conduct a single focused debate round. Each perspective states its \"\n    20→        \"position on the key divergence points, then provide a convergence assessment.\"\n    21→    ),\n    22→    \"standard\": (\n    23→        \"Conduct claims.\\n\"\n    26→        \"Then provide a convergence assessment.\"\n    27→    ),\n    28→    \"deep\": (\n    29→        \"Conduct three debate rounds:\\n\"\n    30→        \"  Round 1: Each perspective states initial positions on divergence points.\\n\"\n    31→        \"  Round 2: Each perspective rebuts the other's key claims.\\n\"\n    32→        \"  Round 3: Final synthesis -- each perspective identifies concessions and \"\n    33→        \"remaining disagreements.\\n\"\n    34→        \"Then provide a convergence assessment.\"\n    35→    ),\n    36→}\n    37→\n    38→_OUTPUT_FORMAT_BLOCK = (\n    39→    \"\\n\\n<output_format>\\n\"\n    40→    \"CRITICAL: Your response MUST begin with YAML frontmatter (--- delimited block).\\n\"\n    41→    \"Do NOT include any text, preamble, or commentary before the opening ---.\\n\"\n    42→    'Do NOT say \"Here is...\", \"Sure!\", or any conversational text before the frontmatter.\\n'\n    43→    \"\\n\"\n    44→    \"Correct start:\\n\"\n    45→    \"---\\n\"\n    46→    \"key: value\\n\"\n    47→    \"---\\n\"\n    48→    \"\\n\"\n    49→    \"Incorrect start:\\n\"\n    50→    \"Here is the output:\\n\"\n    51→    \"---\\n\"\n    52→    \"key: value\\n\"\n    53→    \"---\\n\"\n    54→    \"</output_format>\"\n    55→)\n    56→\n    57→\n    58→def build_extract_prompt(\n    59→    spec_file: Path,\n    60→    retrospective_content: str | None = None,\n    61→) -> str:\n    62→    \"\"\"Prompt for step 'extract'.\n    63→\n    64→    Instructs Claude to read the provided specification and produce\n    65→    extraction.md with YAML frontmatter containing all 13 required\n    66→    protocol fields plus 8 structured body sections.\n    67→\n    68→    Parameters\n    69→    ----------\n    70→    spec_file:\n    71→        Path to the specification file being extracted.\n    72→    retrospective_content:\n    73→        Optional retrospective text from a prior release cycle.\n    74→        When provided, it is framed as advisory \"areas to watch\"\n    75→        context -- NOT as hard requirements (RSK-004 mitigation).\n    76→    \"\"\"\n    77→    base = (\n    78→        \"You are a requirements extraction specialist.\\n\\n\"\n    79→        \"Read the provided specification file and produce a requirements extraction document.\\n\\n\"\n    80→        \"Your output MUST begin with YAML frontmatter delimited by --- lines containing:\\n\"\n    81→        \"- spec_source: (string) the source specification filename\\n\"\n    82→        \"- generated: (string) ISO-8601 timestamp of extraction\\n\"\n    83→        \"- generator: (string) identifier of the extraction agent\\n\"\n    84→        \"- functional_requirements: (integer) count of identified functional requirements\\n\"\n    85→        \"- nonfunctional_requirements: (integer) count of identified non-functional requirements\\n\"\n    86→        \"- total_requirements: (integer) sum of functional + non-functional requirements\\n\"\n    87→        \"- complexity_score: (float 0.0-1.0) assessing overall complexity\\n\"\n    88→        \"- complexity_class: (string) one of: LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH\\n\"\n    89→        \"- domains_detected: (list) array of domain name strings, e.g. [backend, security, frontend]\\n\"\n    90→        \"- risks_identified: (integer) count of risks found in the specification\\n\"\n    91→        \"- dependencies_identified: (integer) count of external dependencies\\n\"\n    92→        \"- success_criteria_count: (integer) count of measurable success criteria\\n\"\n    93→        \"- extraction_mode: (string) one of: standard, chunked\\n\\n\"\n    94→        \"After the frontmatter, provide the following 8 structured sections:\\n\\n\"\n    95→        \"## Functional Requirements\\n\"\n    96→        \"Numbered list with FR-NNN IDs (e.g. FR-001, FR-002). \"\n    97→        \"Extract every functional requirement, even implicit ones.\\n\\n\"\n    98→        \"## Non-Functional Requirements\\n\"\n    99→        \"Numbered list with NFR-NNN IDs (e.g. NFR-001, NFR-002). \"\n   100→        \"Include performance, security, scalability, maintainability.\\n\\n\"\n   101→        \"## Complexity Assessment\\n\"\n   102→        \"Provide complexity_score and complexity_class with detailed scoring rationale.\\n\\n\"\n   103→        \"## Architectural Constraints\\n\"\n   104→        \"List all architectural constraints, technology mandates, and integration boundaries.\\n\\n\"\n   105→        \"## Risk Inventory\\n\"\n   106→        \"Numbered list of identified risks with severity (high/medium/low) and mitigation.\\n\\n\"\n   107→        \"## Dependency Inventory\\n\"\n   108→        \"List all external dependencies, libraries, services, and integration points.\\n\\n\"\n   109→        \"## Success Criteria\\n\"\n   110→        \"Measurable success criteria with acceptance thresholds.\\n\\n\"\n   111→        \"## Open Questions\\n\"\n   112→        \"Ambiguities, gaps, or items requiring stakeholder clarification.\\n\\n\"\n   113→        \"Be thorough and precise. Extract every requirement, even implicit ones.\"\n   114→    )\n   115→\n   116→    if retrospective_content:\n   117→        advisory = (\n   118→            \"\\n\\n## Advisory: Areas to Watch (from prior retrospective)\\n\\n\"\n   119→            \"The following retrospective content is provided as advisory context \"\n   120→            \"only. These are areas to watch during extraction -- they are NOT \"\n   121→            \"additional requirements and MUST NOT be treated as such. Use them \"\n   122→            \"to inform your risk assessment and open questions sections.\\n\\n\"\n   123→            f\"{retrospective_content}\"\n   124→        )\n   125→        base += advisory\n   126→\n   127→    return base + _OUTPUT_FORMAT_BLOCK\n   128→\n   129→\n   130→def build_generate_prompt(agent: AgentSpec, extraction_path: Path) -> str:\n   131→    \"\"\"Prompt for step 'generate-{agent.id}'.\n   132→\n   133→    Instructs Claude to read the extraction document and generate a\n   134→    complete project roadmap with the agent's persona as a role instruction.\n   135→    References expanded extraction fields for richer context.\n   136→    \"\"\"\n   137→    return (\n   138→        f\"You are a {agent.persona} specialist creating a project roadmap.\\n\\n\"\n   139→        \"Read the provided requirements extraction document and generate a comprehensive \"\n   140→        \"project roadmap.\\n\\n\"\n   141→        \"The extraction document contains YAML frontmatter with these fields you should \"\n   142→        \"reference for context:\\n\"\n   143→        \"- spec_source, generated, generator: provenance metadata\\n\"\n   144→        \"- external dependencies to plan around\\n\"\n   149→        \"- success_criteria_count: measurable criteria to validate against\\n\"\n   150→        \"- extraction_mode: extraction completeness indicator\\n\\n\"\n   151→        \"Your output MUST begin with YAML frontmatter delimited by --- lines containing:\\n\"\n   152→        f\"- spec_source: (the source specification filename)\\n\"\n   153→        \"- complexity_score: (float 0.0-1.0 from the extraction)\\n\"\n   154→        f\"- primary_persona: {agent.persona}\\n\\n\"\n   155→        \"After the frontmatter, provide a complete roadmap including:\\n\"\n   156→        \"1. Executive summary\\n\"\n   157→        \"2. Phased implementation plan with milestones\\n\"\n   158→        \"3. Risk assessment and mitigation strategies\\n\"\n   159→        \"4. Timeline estimates per phase\\n\\n\"\n   162→        f\"Apply your {agent.persona} perspective throughout: prioritize concerns, \"\n   163→        f\"risks, and recommendations that a {agent.persona} would emphasize.\\n\\n\"\n   164→        \"Use numbered and bulleted lists for actionable items. Be specific and concrete.\"\n   165→    ) + _OUTPUT_FORMAT_BLOCK\n   166→\n   167→\n   168→def build_diff_prompt(variant_a_path: Path, variant_b_path: Path) -> str:\n   169→    \"\"\"Prompt for step 'diff'.\n   170→\n   171→    Instructs Claude to compare two roadmap variants and produce\n   172→    diff-analysis.md with frontmatter fields total_diff_points,\n   173→    shared_assumptions_count.\n   174→    \"\"\"\n   175→    return (\n   176→        \"You are a comparative analysis specialist.\\n\\n\"\n   177→        \"Read the two provided roadmap variants and produce a structured diff analysis.\\n\\n\"\n   178→        \"Your output MUST begin with YAML frontmatter delimited by --- lines containing:\\n\"\n   179→        \"- total_diff_points: (integer count of identified divergence points)\\n\"\n   180→        \"- shared_assumptions_count: (integer count of shared assumptions)\\n\\n\"\n   181→        \"After the frontmatter, provide:\\n\"\n   182→        \"1. Shared assumptions and agreements between variants\\n\"\n   183→        \"2. Numbered list of divergence points, each with:\\n\"\n   184→        \"   - Description of the difference\\n\"\n   185→        \"   - Which variant takes which position\\n\"\n   186→        \"   - Potential impact of each approach\\n\"\n   187→        \"3. Areas where one variant is clearly stronger\\n\"\n   188→        \"4. Areas requiring debate to resolve\\n\\n\"\n   189→        \"Be objective. Present both positions fairly without bias.\"\n   190→    ) + _OUTPUT_FORMAT_BLOCK\n   191→\n   192→\n   193→def build_debate_prompt(\n   194→    diff_path: Path,\n   195→    variant_a_path: Path,\n   196→    variant_b_path: Path,\n   197→    depth: Literal[\"quick\", \"standard\", \"deep\"],\n   198→) -> str:\n   199→    \"\"\"Prompt for step 'debate'.\n   200→\n   201→    Depth controls the number of debate rounds embedded in the prompt.\n   202→    \"\"\"\n   203→    depth_instruction = _DEPTH_INSTRUCTIONS[depth]\n   204→    return (\n   205→        \"You are a structured debate facilitator.\\n\\n\"\n   206→        \"Read the provided diff analysis and both roadmap variants. \"\n   207→        \"Facilitate a structured adversarial debate between the two approaches.\\n\\n\"\n   208→        f\"Debate format:\\n{depth_instruction}\\n\\n\"\n   209→        \"Your output MUST begin with YAML frontmatter delimited by --- lines containing:\\n\"\n   210→        \"- convergence_score: (float 0.0-1.0 indicating how much agreement was reached)\\n\"\n   211→        \"- rounds_completed: (integer number of debate rounds conducted)\\n\\n\"\n   212→        \"After the frontmatter, provide the full debate transcript with:\\n\"\n   213→        \"- Each round clearly labeled\\n\"\n   214→        \"- Positions attributed to Variant A and Variant B\\n\"\n   215→        \"- A convergence assessment summarizing areas of agreement and remaining disputes\\n\\n\"\n   216→        \"Ensure each perspective argues its strongest case. Do not artificially force agreement.\"\n   217→    ) + _OUTPUT_FORMAT_BLOCK\n   218→\n   219→\n   220→def build_score_prompt(\n   221→    debate_path: Path,\n   222→    variant_a_path: Path,\n   223→    variant_b_path: Path,\n   224→) -> str:\n   225→    \"\"\"Prompt for step 'score'.\n   226→\n   227→    Instructs Claude to select a base variant and score both.\n   228→    \"\"\"\n   229→    return (\n   230→        \"You are an objective evaluation specialist.\\n\\n\"\n   231→        \"Read the debate transcript and both roadmap variants. \"\n   232→        \"Score each variant and select a base for the final merge.\\n\\n\"\n   233→        \"Your output MUST begin with YAML frontmatter delimited by --- lines containing:\\n\"\n   234→        \"- base_variant: (string: the identifier of the selected base variant)\\n\"\n   235→        \"- variant_scores: (string summary of scores, e.g. 'A:78 B:72')\\n\\n\"\n   236→        \"After the frontmatter, provide:\\n\"\n   237→        \"1. Extracts:\n    29→    - imports: All import statements (import X, from X import Y)\n    30→    - exports: Symbols listed in __all__, or top-level public definitions\n    31→    - references: Cross-file name references (function calls, attribute access)\n    32→\n    33→    Handles SyntaxError gracefully per R2: returns empty FileAnalysis with\n    34→    error metadata instead of raising.\n    35→\n    36→    Args:\n    37→        file_path: Path to the file being analyzed.\n    38→        content: Source code content of the file.\n    39→\n    40→    Returns:\n    41→        FileAnalysis with populated imports, exports, and references.\n    42→    \"\"\"\n    43→    content_hash = compute_content_hash(content)\n    44→    lines = content.splitlines()\n    45→\n    46→ file_path, exc)\n    50→        return FileAnalysis(\n    51→            file_path=file_path,\n    52→            content_hash=content_hash,\n    53→            imports=[],\n    54→            exports=[],\n    55→            references=[],\n    56→            metadata={\n    57→                \"line_count\": len(lines),\n    58→                \"size_bytes\": len(content),\n    59→                \"parse_error\": str(exc),\n    60→            },\n    61→        )\n    62→\n    63→    imports = _extract_imports(tree)\n    64→    exports = _extract_exports(tree)\n    65→    references = },\n    77→    )\n    78→\n    79→\n    80→def _extract_imports(tree: ast.Module) -> list[str]:\n    81→    \"\"\"Extract import statements from AST.\"\"\"\n    82→    imports: list[str] = []\n    83→\n    84→    for node in ast.walk(tree):\n    85→        if isinstance(node, ast.Import):\n    86→            for alias in node.names:\n    87→                imports.append(f\"import {alias.name}\")\n    88→        elif isinstance(node, ast.ImportFrom):\n    89→            module = node.module or \"\"\n    90→            for alias in node.names:\n    91→                imports.append(f\"from {module} import {alias.name}\")\n    92→\n    93→    return imports\n    94→\n    95→\n    96→def _extract_exports(tree: ast.Module) -> list[str]:\n    97→    \"\"\"Extract exported symbols from AST.\n    98→\n    99→    Looks for __all__ assignment first. If absent, collects top-level\n   100→    public function and class definitions.\n   101→    \"\"\"\n   102→    # Check for __all__\n   103→    for node in ast.iter_child_nodes(tree):\n   104→        if isinstance(node, ast.Assign):\n   105→            for target in node.targets:\n   106→                if isinstance(target, ast.Name) and target.id == \"__all__\":\n   107→                    if isinstance(node.value, (ast.List, ast.Tuple)):\n   108→                        return [\n   109→                            elt.value\n   110→                            for elt in node.value.elts\n   111→                            if isinstance(elt, ast.Constant) and isinstance(elt.value, str)\n   112→                        ]\n   113→\n   114→    # Fallback: top-level public definitions\n   115→    exports: list[str] = []\n   116→    for node in ast.iter_child_nodes(tree):\n   117→        if isinstance(node, (ast.FunctionDef, ast.AsyncFunctionDef)):\n   118→            if not node.name.startswith(\"_\"):\n   119→                exports.append(node.name)\n   120→        elif isinstance(node, ast.ClassDef):\n   121→            if not node.name.startswith(\"_\"):\n   122→                exports.append(node.name)\n   123→\n   124→    return exports\n   125→\n   126→\n   127→def _extract_references(tree: ast.Module) -> list[str]:\n   128→    \"\"\"Extract cross-file name references from AST.\n   129→\n   130→    Collects:\n   131→    - StepStatus\n    26→from ..pipeline.process import ClaudeProcess\n    27→from .gates import (\n    28→    CERTIFY_GATE,\n    29→    DEBATE_GATE,\n    30→    DIFF_GATE,\n    31→    EXTRACT_GATE,\n    32→    GENERATE_A_GATE,\n    33→    GENERATE_B_GATE,\n    34→    MERGE_GATE,\n    35→    SCORE_GATE,\n    36→    SPEC_FIDELITY_GATE,\n    37→    TEST_STRATEGY_GATE,\n    38→)\n    39→from .models import AgentSpec, RoadmapConfig\n    40→from .prompts import (\n    41→    build_debate_prompt,\n    42→    build_diff_prompt,\n    43→    build_extract_prompt,\n    44→    build_generate_prompt,\n    45→    build_merge_prompt,\n    46→    build_score_prompt,\n    47→    build_spec_fidelity_prompt,\n    48→    build_test_strategy_prompt,\n    49→)\n    50→from .certify_prompts import build_certification_prompt\n    51→\n    52→_log = logging.getLogger(\"superclaude.roadmap.executor\")\n    53→\n    54→# Linux kernel compile-time constant (arch/arm64, x86_64, etc.)\n    55→_MAX_ARG_STRLEN = 128 * 1024\n    56→# 2.3x safety factor; measured template peak ~3.4 KB\n    57→_PROMPT_TEMPLATE_OVERHEAD = 8 * 1024\n    58→# Derivation: MAX_ARG_STRLEN - PROMPT_TEMPLATE_OVERHEAD = 120 KB = 122,880 bytes\n    59→_EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT = _MAX_ARG_STRLEN - _PROMPT_TEMPLATE_OVERHEAD\n    60→assert _PROMPT_TEMPLATE_OVERHEAD >= 4096, (\n    61→    \"Kernel margin violated: _PROMPT_TEMPLATE_OVERHEAD must be >=4096 bytes \"\n    62→    \"to stay safely below MAX_ARG_STRLEN=128 KB; measured template peak ~3.4 KB\"\n    63→)\n    64→\n    65→\n    66→def _embed_inputs(input_paths: list[Path]) -> str:\n    67→    \"\"\"Read input files and return their contents as fenced code blocks.\n    68→\n    69→    Each file is wrapped in a fenced block with a ``# <path>`` header.\n    70→    Returns an empty string when *input_paths* is empty (no-op).\n    71→    \"\"\"\n    72→    if not input_paths:\n    73→        return \"\"\n    74→\n    75→    blocks: list[str] = []\n    76→    for p in input_paths:\n    77→        content = Path(p).read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    78→        blocks.append(f\"# {p}\\n```\\n{content}\\n```\")\n    79→    return \"\\n\\n\".join(blocks)\n    80→\n    81→\n    82→def _sanitize_output(output_file: Path) -> int:\n    83→    \"\"\"Strip conversational preamble and leading blank lines before YAML\n    84→    frontmatter in a step output file.\n    85→\n    86→    Reads the file, strips leading blank lines, finds the first ``^---``\n    87→    line, and removes everything before it.  Uses atomic write (write to\n    88→    ``.tmp`` then ``os.replace()``) to prevent partial file states.\n    89→\n    90→    Returns the byte count of the stripped content (0 when file already\n    91→    starts with ``---`` and has no preamble).\n    92→    \"\"\"\n    93→    import os\n    94→    import re\n    95→\n    96→    try:\n    97→        raw = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    98→    except FileNotFoundError:\n    99→        return 0\n   100→\n   101→    # Strip leading blank lines (LLMs sometimes emit \\n\\n before ---)\n   102→    content = raw.lstrip(\"\\n\\r\\t \")\n   103→\n   104→    # Already starts with frontmatter delimiter -- no preamble to strip\n   105→    if content.startswith(\"---\"):\n   106→        if content == raw:\n   107→            return 0\n   108→        # Leading whitespace only -- write back the stripped version\n   109→        preamble_bytes = len(raw.encode(\"utf-8\")) - len(content.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n   110→        tmp_file = output_file.with_suffix(output_file.suffix + \".tmp\")\n   111→        tmp_file.write_text(content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   112→        os.replace(tmp_file, output_file)\n   113→        _log.info(\n   114→            \"Stripped %d-byte leading whitespace from %s\",\n   115→            preamble_bytes,\n   116→            output_file,\n   117→        )\n   118→        return preamble_bytes\n   119→\n   120→    # Search for the first ^--- line (conversational preamble case)\n   121→    match = re.search(r\"^---[ \\t]*$\", content, re.MULTILINE)\n   122→    if match is None:\n   123→        # No frontmatter found at all -- leave file unchanged\n   124→        return 0\n   125→\n   126→    preamble = content[: match.start()]\n   127→    cleaned = content[match.start():]\n   128→    # Total bytes stripped = leading whitespace + conversational preamble\n   129→    preamble_bytes = len(raw.encode(\"utf-8\")) - len(cleaned.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n   130→\n   131→    # Atomic write: tmp file + os.replace\n   132→    tmp_file = output_file.with_suffix(output_file.suffix + \".tmp\")\n   133→    tmp_file.write_text(cleaned, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   134→    os.replace(tmp_file, output_file)\n   135→\n   136→    _log.info(\"Stripped %d-byte preamble from %s\", preamble_bytes, output_file)\n   137→    return preamble_bytes\n   138→\n   139→\n   140→def _inject_pipeline_diagnostics(\n   141→    output_file: Path,\n   142→    started_at: datetime,\n   143→    finished_at: datetime,\n   144→) -> None:\n   145→    \"\"\"Inject executor-populated pipeline_diagnostics into extraction frontmatter.\n   146→\n   147→    The LLM cannot reliably produce execution timing or environment metadata,\n   148→    so the executor injects these fields post-subprocess (FR-033).\n   149→    \"\"\"\n   150→    content = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").lstrip(\"\\n\\r\\t \")\n   151→    if not content.startswith(\"---\"):\n   152→        return\n   153→\n   154→    # Find end of frontmatter\n   155→    end_idx = content.find(\"\\n---\", 3)\n   156→    if end_idx == -1:\n   157→        return\n   158→\n   159→    # Idempotency: skip if already injected\n   160→    frontmatter = content[3:end_idx]\n   161→    if \"pipeline_diagnostics:\" in frontmatter:\n   162→        return\n   163→\n   164→    elapsed = (finished_at - started_at).total_seconds()\n   165→    diagnostics_line = (\n   166→        f\"pipeline_diagnostics: \"\n   167→        f\"{{elapsed_seconds: {elapsed:.1f}, \"\n   168→        f'started_at: \"{started_at.isoformat()}\", '\n   169→        f'finished_at: \"{finished_at.isoformat()}\"}}'\n   170→    )\n   171→\n   172→    # Insert before the closing ---\n   173→    new_content = content[:end_idx] + \"\\n\" + diagnostics_line + content[end_idx:]\n   174→    output_file.write_text(new_content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   175→\n   176→\n   177→def _inject_provenance_fields(\n   178→    output_file: Path,\n   179→    spec_source: str,\n   180→) -> None:\n   181→    \"\"\"Inject provenance fields into test-strategy frontmatter.\n   182→\n   183→    The test-strategy step's LLM output may omit provenance metadata\n   184→    (spec_source, generated, generator) since the prompt focuses on\n   185→    test strategy content. The executor injects these fields post-subprocess\n   186→    to satisfy TEST_STRATEGY_GATE required_frontmatter_fields.\n   187→    \"\"\"\n   188→    content = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").lstrip(\"\\n\\r\\t \")\n   189→    if not content.startswith(\"---\"):\n   190→        return\n   191→\n   192→    # Find end of frontmatter\n   193→    end_idx = content.find(\"\\n---\", 3)\n   194→    if end_idx == -1:\n   195→        return\n   196→\n   197→    # Idempotency: only inject fields that are missing\n   198→    frontmatter = content[3:end_idx]\n   199→\n   200→    fields_to_inject = []\n   201→    if \"spec_source:\" not in frontmatter:\n   202→        fields_to_inject.append(f\"spec_source: {spec_source}\")\n   203→    if \"generated:\" not in frontmatter:\n   204→        generated = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n   205→        fields_to_inject.append(f'generated: \"{generated}\"')\n   206→    if \"generator:\" not in frontmatter:\n   207→        fields_to_inject.append(\"generator: superclaude-roadmap-executor\")\n   208→\n   209→    if not fields_to_inject:\n   210→        return  # All fields already present\n   211→\n   212→    provenance_block = \"\\n\".join(fields_to_inject)\n   213→    new_content = content[:end_idx] + \"\\n\" + provenance_block + content[end_idx:]\n   214→    output_file.write_text(new_content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   215→\n   216→\n   217→def roadmap_run_step(\n   218→    step: Step,\n   219→    config: PipelineConfig,\n   220→    cancel_check: Callable[[], bool],\n   221→) -> StepResult:\n   222→    \"\"\"Execute a single roadmap step as a Claude subprocess.\n   223→\n   224→    Builds argv with context isolation, launches process, waits with\n   225→    timeout, and returns StepResult.\n   226→    \"\"\"\n   227→    started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   228→\n   229→    if config.dry_run:\n   230→        _log.info(\"[dry-run] Would execute step '%s'\", step.id)\n   231→        return StepResult(\n   232→            step=step,\n   233→            status=StepStatus.PASS,\n   234→            attempt=1,\n   235→            started_at=started_at,\n   236→            finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n   237→        )\n   238→\n   239→    # Inline embedding: read input files into the prompt instead of --file flags\n   240→    # --file is broken (cloud download mechanism, not local file injector) so\n   241→    # inline embedding is always used regardless of composed prompt size.\n   242→    embedded = _embed_inputs(step.inputs)\n   243→    if embedded:\n   244→        composed = step.prompt + \"\\n\\n\" + embedded\n   245→        if len(composed.encode(\"utf-8\")) > _EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT:\n   246→            # <= is intentional; _EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT = 120 KB is safely below MAX_ARG_STRLEN = 128 KB\n   247→            _log.warning(\n   248→                \"Step '%s': composed prompt exceeds %d bytes; embedding inline anyway\"\n   249→                \" (--file fallback is unavailable)\",\n   250→                step.id,\n   251→                _EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT,\n   252→            )\n   253→        effective_prompt = composed\n   254→        extra_args: list[str] = []\n   255→    else:\n   256→        effective_prompt = step.prompt\n   257→        extra_args = []\n   258→\n   259→    proc = ClaudeProcess(\n   260→        prompt=effective_prompt,\n   261→        output_file=step.output_file,\n   262→        error_file=step.output_file.with_suffix(\".err\"),\n   263→        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n   264→        model=step.model or config.model,\n   265→        permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n   266→        timeout_seconds=step.timeout_seconds,\n   267→        output_format=\"text\",\n   268→        extra_args=extra_args,\n   269→    )\n   270→\n   271→    proc.start()\n   272→\n   273→    # Poll for cancellation while waiting\n   274→    while proc._process is not None and proc._process.poll() is None:\n   275→        if cancel_check():\n   276→            proc.terminate()\n   277→            return StepResult(\n   278→                step=step,\n   279→                status=StepStatus.CANCELLED,\n   280→                attempt=1,\n   281→                gate_failure_reason=\"Cancelled by external signal\",\n   282→                started_at=started_at,\n   283→                finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n   284→            )\n   285→        time.sleep(1)\n   286→\n   287→    exit_code = proc.wait()\n   288→    finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   289→\n   290→    if exit_code == 124:\n   291→        return StepResult(\n   292→            step=step,\n   293→            status=StepStatus.TIMEOUT,\n   294→            attempt=1,\n   295→            gate_failure_reason=f\"Step '{step.id}' timed out after {step.timeout_seconds}s\",\n   296→            started_at=started_at,\n   297→            finished_at=finished_at,\n   298→        )\n   299→\n   300→    if exit_code != 0:\n   301→        return StepResult(\n   302→            step=step,\n   303→            status=StepStatus.FAIL,\n   304→            attempt=1,\n   305→            gate_failure_reason=f\"Step '{step.id}' exited with code {exit_code}\",\n   306→            started_at=started_at,\n   307→            finished_at=finished_at,\n   308→        )\n   309→\n   310→    # Sanitize output: strip conversational preamble before gate validation\n   311→    _sanitize_output(step.output_file)\n   312→\n   313→    # Inject executor-populated fields into extract step frontmatter (FR-033)\n   314→    if step.id == \"extract\" and step.output_file.exists():\n   315→        _inject_pipeline_diagnostics(step.output_file, started_at, finished_at)\n   316→\n   317→    # Inject provenance fields into test-strategy output\n   318→    if step.id == \"test-strategy\" and step.output_file.exists():\n   319→        spec_source = (\n   320→            config.spec_file.name if hasattr(config, \"spec_file\") else \"unknown\"\n   321→        )\n   322→        _inject_provenance_fields(step.output_file, spec_source)\n   323→\n   324→    # Process completed successfully; gate check happens in execute_pipeline\n   325→    return StepResult(\n   326→        step=step,\n   327→        status=StepStatus.PASS,\n   328→        attempt=1,\n   329→        started_at=started_at,\n   330→        finished_at=finished_at,\n   331→    )\n   332→\n   333→\n   334→def build_certify_step(\n   335→    config: RoadmapConfig,\n   336→    findings: list | None = None,\n   337→    context_sections: dict[str, str] | None = None,\n   338→) -> Step:\n   339→    \"\"\"Build a certify Step for execution via execute_pipeline().\n   340→\n   341→    The certify step runs as a standard Step (not ClaudeProcess directly)\n   342→    per spec section 2.5. It uses CERTIFY_GATE for output validation.\n   343→\n   344→    Parameters\n   345→    ----------\n   346→    config:\n   347→        RoadmapConfig with output_dir.\n   348→    findings:\n   349→        List of Finding objects to certify. If None, an empty prompt is built.\n   350→    context_sections:\n   351→        Pre-extracted context sections per finding location (NFR-011).\n   352→    \"\"\"\n   353→    from .models import Finding\n   354→\n   355→    out = config.output_dir\n   356→    certification_report = out / \"certification-report.md\"\n   357→\n   358→    prompt = build_certification_prompt(\n   359→        findings=findings or [],\n   360→        context_sections=context_sections or {},\n   361→    )\n   362→\n   363→    return Step(\n   364→        id=\"certify\",\n   365→        prompt=prompt,\n   366→        output_file=certification_report,\n   367→        gate=CERTIFY_GATE,\n   368→        timeout_seconds=300,\n   369→        inputs=[out / \"remediation-tasklist.md\"],\n   370→        retry_limit=1,\n   371→    )\n   372→\n   373→\n   374→def _build_steps(config: RoadmapConfig) -> list[Step | list[Step]]:\n   375→    \"\"\"Build the 9-step pipeline with parallel generate group.\n   376→\n   377→    Returns a list where each element is either a single Step (sequential)\n   378→    or a list[Step] (parallel group).\n   379→    \"\"\"\n   380→    out = config.output_dir\n   381→\n   382→    # Agent specs\n   383→    agent_a = config.agents[0]\n   384→    agent_b = config.agents[1] if len(config.agents) > 1 else config.agents[0]\n   385→\n   386→    # Output paths\n   387→    extraction = out / \"extraction.md\"\n   388→    roadmap_a = out / f\"roadmap-{agent_a.id}.md\"\n   389→    roadmap_b = out / f\"roadmap-{agent_b.id}.md\"\n   390→    diff_file = out / \"diff-analysis.md\"\n   391→    debate_file = out / \"debate-transcript.md\"\n   392→    score_file = out / \"base-selection.md\"\n   393→    merge_file = out / \"roadmap.md\"\n   394→    test_strat = out / \"test-strategy.md\"\n   395→    spec_fidelity_file = out / \"spec-fidelity.md\"\n   396→\n   397→    # Load retrospective content if configured (missing file handled gracefully)\n   398→    retrospective_content: str | None = None\n   399→    if config.retrospective_file is not None and config.retrospective_file.is_file():\n   400→        try:\n   401→            retrospective_content = config.retrospective_file.read_text(\n   402→                encoding=\"utf-8\"\n   403→            )\n   404→        except OSError:\n   405→            retrospective_content = None\n   406→\n   407→    steps: list[Step | list[Step]] = [\n   408→        # Step 1: Extract\n   409→        Step(\n   410→            id=\"extract\",\n   411→            prompt=build_extract_prompt(\n   412→                config.spec_file,\n   413→                retrospective_content=retrospective_content,\n   414→            ),\n   415→            output_file=extraction,\n   416→            gate=EXTRACT_GATE,\n   417→            timeout_seconds=300,\n   418→            inputs=[config.spec_file],\n   419→            retry_limit=1,\n   420→        ),\n   421→        # Steps 2a+2b: Generate (parallel)\n   422→        [\n   423→            Step(\n   424→                id=f\"generate-{agent_a.id}\",\n   425→                prompt=build_generate_prompt(agent_a, extraction),\n   426→                output_file=roadmap_a,\n   427→                gate=GENERATE_A_GATE,\n   428→                timeout_seconds=900,\n   429→                inputs=[extraction],\n   430→                retry_limit=1,\n   431→                model=agent_a.model,\n   432→            ),\n   433→            Step(\n   434→                id=f\"generate-{agent_b.id}\",\n   435→                prompt=build_generate_prompt(agent_b, extraction),\n   436→                output_file=roadmap_b,\n   437→                gate=GENERATE_B_GATE,\n   438→                timeout_seconds=900,\n   439→                inputs=[extraction],\n   440→                retry_limit=1,\n   441→                model=agent_b.model,\n   442→            ),\n   443→        ],\n   444→        # Step 3: Diff\n   445→        Step(\n   446→            id=\"diff\",\n   447→            prompt=build_diff_prompt(roadmap_a, roadmap_b),\n   448→            output_file=diff_file,\n   449→            gate=DIFF_GATE,\n   450→            timeout_seconds=300,\n   451→            inputs=[roadmap_a, roadmap_b],\n   452→            retry_limit=1,\n   453→        ),\n   454→        # Step 4: Debate\n   455→        Step(\n   456→            id=\"debate\",\n   457→            prompt=build_debate_prompt(diff_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b, config.depth),\n   458→            output_file=debate_file,\n   459→            gate=DEBATE_GATE,\n   460→            timeout_seconds=600,\n   461→            inputs=[diff_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b],\n   462→            retry_limit=1,\n   463→        ),\n   464→        # Step 5: Score\n   465→        Step(\n   466→            id=\"score\",\n   467→            prompt=build_score_prompt(debate_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b),\n   468→            output_file=score_file,\n   469→            gate=SCORE_GATE,\n   470→            timeout_seconds=300,\n   471→            inputs=[debate_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b],\n   472→            retry_limit=1,\n   473→        ),\n   474→        # Step 6: Merge\n   475→        Step(\n   476→            id=\"merge\",\n   477→            prompt=build_merge_prompt(score_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b, debate_file),\n   478→            output_file=merge_file,\n   479→            gate=MERGE_GATE,\n   480→            timeout_seconds=600,\n   481→            inputs=[score_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b, debate_file],\n   482→            retry_limit=1,\n   483→        ),\n   484→        # Step 7: Test Strategy\n   485→        Step(\n   486→            id=\"test-strategy\",\n   487→            prompt=build_test_strategy_prompt(merge_file, extraction),\n   488→            output_file=test_strat,\n   489→            gate=TEST_STRATEGY_GATE,\n   490→            timeout_seconds=300,\n   491→            inputs=[merge_file, extraction],\n   492→            retry_limit=1,\n   493→        ),\n   494→        # Step 8: Spec Fidelity (after test-strategy, FR-008 through FR-010)\n   495→        Step(\n   496→            id=\"spec-fidelity\",\n   497→            prompt=build_spec_fidelity_prompt(config.spec_file, merge_file),\n   498→            output_file=spec_fidelity_file,\n   499→            gate=SPEC_FIDELITY_GATE,\n   500→            timeout_seconds=600,\n   501→            inputs=[config.spec_file, merge_file],\n   502→            retry_limit=1,\n   503→        ),\n   504→    ]\n   505→\n   506→    return steps\n   507→\n   508→\n   509→def _format_halt_output(results: list[StepResult], config: RoadmapConfig) -> str:\n   510→    \"\"\"Format HALT diagnostic output per spec section 6.2.\"\"\"\n   511→    failed = [r for r in results if r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)]\n   512→    passed = [r for r in results if r.status == StepStatus.PASS]\n   513→    cancelled = [r for r in results if r.status == StepStatus.CANCELLED]\n   514→\n   515→    if not failed:\n   516→        return \"\"\n   517→\n   518→    fail = failed[-1]\n   519→    step = fail.step\n   520→\n   521→    # Calculate file details if output exists\n   522→    file_info = \"\"\n   523→    if step and step.output_file.exists():\n   524→        content = step.output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   525→        byte_count = len(content.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n   526→        line_count = len(content.splitlines())\n   527→        file_info = f\"  Output size: {byte_count} bytes ({line_count} lines)\\n\"\n   528→    elif step:\n   529→        file_info = \"  Output file: not created\\n\"\n   530→\n   531→    elapsed = f\"{fail.duration_seconds:.0f}s\"\n   532→\n   533→    lines = [\n   534→        f\"ERROR: Roadmap pipeline halted at step '{step.id}' (attempt {fail.attempt}/2)\",\n   535→        f\"  Gate failure: {fail.gate_failure_reason}\",\n   536→        f\"  Output file: {step.output_file}\",\n   537→        file_info.rstrip(),\n   538→        f\"  Step timeout: {step.timeout_seconds}s | Elapsed: {elapsed}\",\n   539→        \"\",\n   540→        f\"Completed steps: {', '.join(f'{r.step.id} (PASS, attempt {r.attempt})' for r in passed) or 'none'}\",\n   541→        f\"Failed step:     {step.id} ({fail.status.value}, attempt {fail.attempt})\",\n   542→    ]\n   543→\n   544→    # Collect skipped steps\n   545→    all_step_ids = _get_all_step_ids(config)\n   546→    executed_ids = {r.step.id for r in results}\n   547→    skipped_ids = [sid for sid in all_step_ids if sid not in executed_ids]\n   548→    if cancelled:\n   549→        skipped_ids = [r.step.id for r in cancelled] + skipped_ids\n   550→\n   551→    if skipped_ids:\n   552→        lines.append(f\"Skipped steps:   {', '.join(skipped_ids)}\")\n   553→\n   554→    lines.extend(\n   555→        [\n   556→            \"\",\n   557→            \"To retry from this step:\",\n   558→            f\"  superclaude roadmap run {config.spec_file} --resume\",\n   559→            \"\",\n   560→            \"To inspect the failing output:\",\n   561→            f\"  cat {step.output_file}\",\n   562→        ]\n   563→    )\n   564→\n   565→    return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n   566→\n   567→\n   568→def _get_all_step_ids(config: RoadmapConfig) -> list[str]:\n   569→    \"\"\"Get all step IDs in pipeline order.\"\"\"\n   570→    agent_a = config.agents[0]\n   571→    agent_b = config.agents[1] if len(config.agents) > 1 else config.agents[0]\n   572→    return [\n   573→        \"extract\",\n   574→        f\"generate-{agent_a.id}\",\n   575→        f\"generate-{agent_b.id}\",\n   576→        \"diff\",\n   577→        \"debate\",\n   578→        \"score\",\n   579→        \"merge\",\n   580→        \"test-strategy\",\n   581→        \"spec-fidelity\",\n   582→        \"remediate\",\n   583→        \"certify\",\n   584→    ]\n   585→\n   586→\n   587→def _print_terminal_halt(\n   588→    output_dir: Path,\n   589→    remaining_findings: list,\n   590→    attempt_count: int,\n   591→    spec_patch_budget_exhausted: bool = False,\n   592→    file=None,\n   593→) -> None:\n   594→    \"\"\"Print terminal halt message to stderr with finding details (SC-6).\n   595→\n   596→    Outputs:\n   597→    - Attempt count and remaining failing finding count\n   598→    - Per-finding details (ID, severity, description)\n   599→    - Manual-fix instructions including certification report path and resume command\n   600→\n   601→    Args:\n   602→        output_dir: Pipeline output directory (for certification report path)\n   603→        remaining_findings: List of Finding objects that failed remediation\n   604→        attempt_count: Number of remediation attempts made\n   605→        spec_patch_budget_exhausted: If True, note that spec-patch budget is also exhausted\n   606→        file: Output stream (defaults to sys.stderr)\n   607→    \"\"\"\n   608→    if file is None:\n   609→        file = sys.stderr\n   610→\n   611→    certify_path = output_dir / \"certify.md\"\n   612→\n   613→    lines = [\n   614→        \"\",\n   615→        \"=\" * 72,\n   616→        \"TERMINAL HALT: Remediation budget exhausted\",\n   617→        \"=\" * 72,\n   618→        f\"Attempt count: {attempt_count}\",\n   619→        f\"Remaining failing findings: {len(remaining_findings)}\",\n   620→        \"\",\n   621→    ]\n   622→\n   623→    if remaining_findings:\n   624→        lines.append(\"Failing findings:\")\n   625→        for finding in remaining_findings:\n   626→            severity = getattr(finding, \"severity\", \"UNKNOWN\")\n   627→            description = getattr(finding, \"description\", \"\")\n   628→            finding_id = getattr(finding, \"id\", \"UNKNOWN\")\n   629→            lines.append(f\"  - {finding_id} [{severity}]: {description}\")\n   630→        lines.append(\"\")\n   631→\n   632→    lines.extend(\n   633→        [\n   634→            \"Manual fix required:\",\n   635→            f\"  1. Review certification report: {certify_path}\",\n   636→            \"  2. Fix remaining findings manually\",\n   637→            \"  3. Resume pipeline with:\",\n   638→            f\"     superclaude roadmap run --resume\",\n   639→            \"\",\n   640→        ]\n   641→    )\n   642→\n   643→    if spec_patch_budget_exhausted:\n   644→        lines.extend(\n   645→            [\n   646→                \"Note: Both the remediation budget and the spec-patch cycle budget\",\n   647→                \"are exhausted. Full dual-budget recovery is deferred to v2.26.\",\n   648→                \"# TODO(v2.26): implement dual-budget-exhaustion recovery mechanism\",\n   649→                \"\",\n   650→            ]\n   651→        )\n   652→\n   653→    lines.append(\"=\" * 72)\n   654→\n   655→    print(\"\\n\".join(lines), file=file, flush=True)\n   656→\n   657→\n   658→def _check_annotate_deviations_freshness(\n   659→    output_dir: Path,\n   660→    roadmap_file: Path,\n   661→    gate_pass_state: dict[str, bool] | None = None,\n   662→) -> bool:\n   663→    \"\"\"Check if spec-deviations.md hash matches current roadmap.md (SC-8).\n   664→\n   665→    Reads roadmap_hash from spec-deviations.md frontmatter and compares\n   666→    against sha256(roadmap.md). Fail-closed for all error conditions.\n   667→\n   668→    On hash mismatch, resets spec-fidelity and deviation-analysis gate\n   669→    pass state to force re-run (FR-084).\n   670→\n   671→    Returns:\n   672→        True if hash matches (fresh), False for any failure or mismatch.\n   673→    \"\"\"\n   674→    spec_deviations = output_dir / \"spec-deviations.md\"\n   675→\n   676→    # Case 1: spec-deviations.md not found\n   677→    if not spec_deviations.exists():\n   678→        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: spec-deviations.md not found\")\n   679→        return False\n   680→\n   681→    # Case 2: Read error\n   682→    try:\n   683→        content = spec_deviations.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   684→    except OSError as e:\n   685→        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: read error for spec-deviations.md: %s\", e)\n   686→        return False\n   687→\n   688→    # Case 3: Empty file\n   689→    if not content.strip():\n   690→        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: spec-deviations.md is empty\")\n   691→        return False\n   692→\n   693→    # Case 4: Corrupt/missing frontmatter\n   694→    stripped = content.lstrip()\n   695→    if not stripped.startswith(\"---\"):\n   696→        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: spec-deviations.md has no frontmatter\")\n   697→        return False\n   698→\n   699→    rest = stripped[3:].lstrip(\"\\n\")\n   700→    end_idx = rest.find(\"\\n---\")\n   701→    if end_idx == -1:\n   702→        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: spec-deviations.md has corrupt frontmatter\")\n   703→        return False\n   704→\n   705→    # Parse roadmap_hash from frontmatter\n   706→    saved_hash: str | None = None\n   707→    for line in rest[:end_idx].splitlines():\n   708→        line = line.strip()\n   709→        if line.startswith(\"roadmap_hash:\"):\n   710→            saved_hash = line.split(\":\", 1)[1].strip()\n   711→            break\n   712→\n   713→    # Case 5: roadmap_hash field missing or empty\n   714→    if not saved_hash:\n   715→        _log.warning(\n   716→            \"Freshness check: roadmap_hash field missing or empty in spec-deviations.md\"\n   717→        )\n   718→        return False\n   719→\n   720→    # Case 6: roadmap.md not found\n   721→    if not roadmap_file.exists():\n   722→        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: roadmap.md not found at %s\", roadmap_file)\n   723→        return False\n   724→\n   725→    # Case 7: roadmap.md read error\n   726→    try:\n   727→        roadmap_content = roadmap_file.read_bytes()\n   728→    except OSError as e:\n   729→        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: read error for roadmap.md: %s\", e)\n   730→        return False\n   731→\n   732→    # Case 8: Hash comparison\n   733→    current_hash = hashlib.sha256(roadmap_content).hexdigest()\n   734→    if saved_hash != current_hash:\n   735→        _log.warning(\n   736→            \"Freshness check: hash mismatch -- saved=%s current=%s\",\n   737→            saved_hash[:12],\n   738→            current_hash[:12],\n   739→        )\n   740→        # FR-084: reset gate pass state for downstream steps\n   741→        if gate_pass_state is not None:\n   742→            for key in (\"spec-fidelity\", \"deviation-analysis\"):\n   743→                if key in gate_pass_state:\n   744→                    gate_pass_state[key] = False\n   745→        return False\n   746→\n   747→    return True\n   748→\n   749→\n   750→def _check_remediation_budget(\n   751→    output_dir: Path,\n   752→    max_attempts: int = 2,\n   753→    halt_fn: \"Callable[[Path, list, int], None] | None\" = None,\n   754→) -> bool:\n   755→    \"\"\"Check if remediation budget allows another attempt (SC-6).\n   756→\n   757→    Reads remediation_attempts from .roadmap-state.json.\n   758→    If attempts >= max_attempts, calls halt_fn (or _print_terminal_halt)\n   759→    and returns False.\n   760→\n   761→    Non-integer remediation_attempts is coerced to 0 with a WARNING log.\n   762→\n   763→    Args:\n   764→        output_dir: Pipeline output directory containing .roadmap-state.json\n   765→        max_attempts: Maximum allowed remediation attempts (default 2)\n   766→        halt_fn: Optional callable for terminal halt; defaults to _print_terminal_halt\n   767→\n   768→    Returns:\n   769→        True if budget allows another attempt, False if exhausted.\n   770→    \"\"\"\n   771→    state_file = output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n   772→    state = read_state(state_file)\n   773→\n   774→    attempts = 0\n   775→    if state is not None:\n   776→        raw = state.get(\"remediation_attempts\", 0)\n   777→        try:\n   778→            attempts = int(raw)\n   779→        except (ValueError, TypeError):\n   780→            _log.warning(\"Non-integer remediation_attempts %r coerced to 0\", raw)\n   781→            attempts = 0\n   782→\n   783→    if attempts >= max_attempts:\n   784→        if halt_fn is not None:\n   785→            halt_fn(output_dir, [], attempts + 1)\n   786→        else:\n   787→            _print_terminal_halt(\n   788→                output_dir=output_dir,\n   789→                remaining_findings=[],\n   790→                attempt_count=attempts + 1,\n   791→            )\n   792→        return False\n   793→\n   794→    return True\n   795→\n   796→\n   797→def _print_step_start(step: Step) -> None:\n   798→    \"\"\"Print progress output when a step starts.\"\"\"\n   799→    print(f\"[roadmap] Starting step: {step.id}\", flush=True)\n   800→\n   801→\n   802→def _print_step_complete(step: Step, result: StepResult) -> None:\n   803→    \"\"\"Print progress output when a step completes.\"\"\"\n   804→    duration = f\"{result.duration_seconds:.0f}s\"\n   805→    if result.status == StepStatus.PASS:\n   806→        print(\n   807→            f\"[roadmap] Step {step.id}  PASS (attempt {result.attempt}, {duration})\",\n   808→            flush=True,\n   809→        )\n   810→    else:\n   811→        print(\n   812→            f\"[roadmap] Step {step.id}  {result.status.value} (attempt {result.attempt}, {duration})\",\n   813→            flush=True,\n   814→        )\n   815→        if result.gate_failure_reason:\n   816→            print(f\"           Reason: {result.gate_failure_reason}\", flush=True)\n   817→\n   818→\n   819→def _dry_run_output(steps: list[Step | list[Step]]) -> None:\n   820→    \"\"\"Print step plan and gate criteria for --dry-run.\"\"\"\n   821→    step_num = 0\n   822→    for entry in steps:\n   823→        if isinstance(entry, list):\n   824→            for s in entry:\n   825→                step_num += 1\n   826→                _print_step_plan(step_num, s, parallel=True)\n   827→        else:\n   828→            step_num += 1\n   829→            _print_step_plan(step_num, entry)\n   830→\n   831→\n   832→def _print_step_plan(num: int, step: Step, parallel: bool = False) -> None:\n   833→    \"\"\"Print a single step's plan entry.\"\"\"\n   834→    par_label = \" (parallel)\" if parallel else \"\"\n   835→    print(f\"Step {num}{par_label}: {step.id}\")\n   836→    print(f\"  Output: {step.output_file}\")\n   837→    print(f\"  Timeout: {step.timeout_seconds}s\")\n   838→    if step.model:\n   839→        print(f\"  Model: {step.model}\")\n   840→    if step.gate:\n   841→        print(f\"  Gate tier: {step.gate.enforcement_tier}\")\n   842→        print(f\"  Gate min_lines:          print(f\"  Semantic checks: {', '.join(checks)}\")\n   850→    print()\n   851→\n   852→\n   853→def _save_state(\n   854→    config: RoadmapConfig,\n   855→    results: list[StepResult],\n   856→    remediate_metadata: dict | None = None,\n   857→    certify_metadata: dict | None = None,\n   858→) -> None:\n   859→    \"\"\"Write .roadmap-state.json to output_dir via atomic tmp + os.replace().\n   860→\n   861→    Preserves existing ``validation``, ``fidelity_status``, ``remediate``,\n   862→    and ``certify`` keys if present. Accepts optional remediate/certify\n   863→    metadata dicts for Phase 6 state finalization.\n   864→\n   865→    Defense-in-depth guards:\n   866→    - No-progress guard: skip write when no steps passed (prevents corruption\n   867→      from broken resumes that fail immediately).\n   868→    - Agent-mismatch guard: preserve original agents in state when no generate\n   869→      steps ran (prevents overwriting correct agent config with wrong agents).\n   870→    \"\"\"\n   871→    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n   872→\n   873→    # No-progress guard: if the results list is empty (no steps were even\n   874→    # attempted), do not write state. This prevents corruption from broken\n   875→    # resumes that produce zero results. Any non-empty results list indicates\n   876→    # the pipeline made progress and state should be recorded.\n   877→    step_results = [r for r in results if r.step]\n   878→    if not step_results:\n   879→        _log.info(\"State not saved: no step results in this run\")\n   880→        return\n   881→\n   882→    # Preserve existing keys across state rewrites\n   883→    existing = read_state(state_file)\n   884→\n   885→    # Agent-mismatch guard: if agents differ from state and no generate steps\n   886→    # ran, preserve original agents to prevent corruption\n   887→    if existing is not None:\n   888→        saved_agents = existing.get(\"agents\", [])\n   889→        current_agents = [\n   890→            {\"model\": a.model, \"persona\": a.persona} for a in config.agents\n   891→        ]\n   892→        if saved_agents and saved_agents != current_agents:\n   893→            generate_ran = any(\n   894→                r.step\n   895→                and r.step.id.startswith(\"generate-\")\n   896→                and r.status == StepStatus.PASS\n   897→                for r in results\n   898→            )\n   899→            if not generate_ran:\n   900→                _log.warning(\n   901→                    \"Agents differ from state file and no generate steps ran; \"\n   902→                    \"preserving original agents in state\"\n   903→                )\n   904→                return\n   905→\n   906→    existing_validation = existing.get(\"validation\") if existing else None\n   907→    existing_fidelity = existing.get(\"fidelity_status\") if existing else None\n   908→    existing_remediate = existing.get(\"remediate\") if existing else None\n   909→    existing_certify = existing.get(\"certify\") if existing else None\n   910→\n   911→    spec_hash = hashlib.sha256(config.spec_file.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n   912→\n   913→    state = {\n   914→        \"schema_version\": 1,\n   915→        \"spec_file\": str(config.spec_file),\n   916→        \"spec_hash\": spec_hash,\n   917→        \"agents\": [{\"model\": a.model, \"persona\": a.persona} for a in config.agents],\n   918→        \"depth\": config.depth,\n   919→        \"last_run\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n   920→        \"steps\": {\n   921→            r.step.id: {\n   922→                \"status\": r.status.value,\n   923→                \"attempt\": r.attempt,\n   924→                \"output_file\": str(r.step.output_file),\n   925→                \"started_at\": r.started_at.isoformat(),\n   926→                \"completed_at\": r.finished_at.isoformat(),\n   927→            }\n   928→            for r in results\n   929→            if r.step\n   930→        },\n   931→    }\n   932→\n   933→    # Merge with existing state (preserve steps not in this run)\n   934→    if existing:\n   935→        existing_steps = existing.get(\"steps\", {})\n   936→        for step_id, step_data in existing_steps.items():\n   937→            if step_id not in state[\"steps\"]:\n   938→                state[\"steps\"][step_id] = step_data\n   939→\n   940→    if existing_validation is not None:\n   941→        state[\"validation\"] = existing_validation\n   942→\n   943→    # Derive fidelity_status from spec-fidelity step result\n   944→    fidelity_result = next(\n   945→        (r for r in results if r.step and r.step.id == \"spec-fidelity\"),\n   946→        None,\n   947→    )\n   948→    if fidelity_result is not None:\n   949→        state[\"fidelity_status\"] = _derive_fidelity_status(fidelity_result)\n   950→    elif existing_fidelity is not None:\n   951→        state[\"fidelity_status\"] = existing_fidelity\n   952→\n   953→    # Remediate metadata (spec §3.1)\n   954→    if remediate_metadata is not None:\n   955→        state[\"remediate\"] = remediate_metadata\n   956→    elif existing_remediate is not None:\n   957→        state[\"remediate\"] = existing_remediate\n   958→\n   959→    # Certify metadata (spec §3.1)\n   960→    if certify_metadata is not None:\n   961→        state[\"certify\"] = certify_metadata\n   962→    elif existing_certify is not None:\n   963→        state[\"certify\"] = existing_certify\n   964→\n   965→    write_state(state, state_file)\n   966→\n   967→\n   968→def _derive_fidelity_status(result: StepResult) -> str:\n   969→    \"\"\"Derive fidelity_status enum from a spec-fidelity StepResult.\n   970→\n   971→    Returns one of: 'pass', 'fail', 'skipped', 'degraded'.\n   972→    \"\"\"\n   973→    if result.status == StepStatus.PASS:\n   974→        # Check if the output indicates degraded mode\n   975→        if result.step and result.step.output_file.exists():\n   976→            content = result.step.output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   977→            if \"validation_complete: false\" in content:\n   978→                return \"degraded\"\n   979→        return \"pass\"\n   980→    if result.status == StepStatus.SKIPPED:\n   981→        return \"skipped\"\n   982→    if result.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT):\n   983→        return \"fail\"\n   984→    return \"skipped\"\n   985→\n   986→\n   987→def generate_degraded_report(\n   988→    output_file: Path,\n   989→    failed_agent: str,\n   990→    failure_reason: str,\n   991→) -> None:\n   992→    \"\"\"Generate a degraded fidelity report when the agent fails.\n   993→\n   994→    Produces a report with validation_complete=false and\n   995→    fidelity_check_attempted=true so the SPEC_FIDELITY_GATE\n   996→    can distinguish degraded from clean passes (NFR-007).\n   997→\n   998→    The degraded report names the failed agent and reason in the body.\n   999→    \"\"\"\n  1000→    report = (\n  1001→        \"---\\n\"\n  1002→        \"high_severity_count: 0\\n\"\n  1003→        \"medium_severity_count: 0\\n\"\n  1004→        \"low_severity_count: 0\\n\"\n  1005→        \"total_deviations: 0\\n\"\n  1006→        \"validation_complete: false\\n\"\n  1007→        \"fidelity_check_attempted: true\\n\"\n  1008→        \"tasklist_ready: false\\n\"\n  1009→        \"---\\n\"\n  1010→        \"\\n\"\n  1011→        \"## Degraded Fidelity Report\\n\"\n  1012→        \"\\n\"\n  1013→        \"Spec-fidelity validation could not be completed.\\n\"\n  1014→ 1030→\n  1031→\n  1032→def build_remediate_metadata(\n  1033→    status: str,\n  1034→    scope: str,\n  1035→    findings_total: int,\n  1036→    findings_actionable: int,\n  1037→    findings_fixed:
