derive_pipeline_status(state: dict) -> str:\n  1085→    \"\"\"Derive the overall pipeline status from state transitions.\n  1086→\n  1087→    State transitions at each boundary:\n  1088→    - post-validate: validated | validated-with-issues\n  1089→    - post-remediate: remediated\n  1090→    - post-certify: certified | certified-with-caveats\n  1091→\n  1092→    Returns one of: 'pending', 'validated', 'validated-with-issues',\n  1093→    'remediated', 'certified', 'certified-with-caveats'.\n  1094→    \"\"\"\n  1095→    certify = state.get(\"certify\")\n  1096→    if certify is not None:\n  1097→        if certify.get(\"certified\", False):\n  1098→            return \"certified\"\n  1099→        return \"certified-with-caveats\"\n  1100→\n  1101→    remediate = state.get(\"remediate\")\n  1102→    if remediate is not None:\n  1103→        return \"remediated\"\n  1104→\n  1105→    validation = state.get(\"validation\")\n  1106→    if validation is not None:\n  1107→        if validation.get(\"status\") == \"pass\":\n  1108→            return \"validated\"\n  1109→        if validation.get(\"status\") == \"fail\":\n  1110→            return \"validated-with-issues\"\n  1111→\n  1112→    return \"pending\"\n  1113→\n  1114→\n  1115→def write_state(state: dict, path: Path) -> None:\n  1116→    \"\"\"Write state dict to path atomically via tmp file + os.replace().\"\"\"\n  1117→    import os as _os\n  1118→\n  1119→    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n  1120→    tmp = path.with_suffix(\".tmp\")\n  1121→    tmp.write_text(json.dumps(state, indent=2), encoding=\"utf-8\")\n  1122→    _os.replace(str(tmp), str(path))\n  1123→\n  1124→\n  1125→def read_state(path: Path) -> dict | None:\n  1126→    \"\"\"Read state from path with graceful recovery on missing/malformed files.\"\"\"\n  1127→    if not path.exists():\n  1128→        return None\n  1129→    try:\n  1130→        text = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n  1131→        if not text.strip():\n  1132→            return None\n  1133→        return json.loads(text)\n  1134→    except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n  1135→        return None\n  1136→\n  1137→\n  1138→def apply_decomposition_pass(deliverables: list[Deliverable]) -> list[Deliverable]:\n  1139→    \"\"\"Post-generation decomposition pass for the roadmap pipeline.\n  1140→\n  1141→    Runs after deliverable generation, before output formatting.\n  1142→    Splits behavioral deliverables into Implement/Verify pairs.\n  1143→\n  1144→    Idempotent: running twice produces identical results because\n  1145→    already-decomposed deliverables (IDs ending .a/.b) are skipped.\n  1146→\n  1147→    Preserves milestone-internal ordering: deliverables within each\n  1148→    milestone maintain their relative order after decomposition.\n  1149→    \"\"\"\n  1150→    return decompose_deliverables(deliverables)\n  1151→\n  1152→\n  1153→def _restore_from_state(\n  1154→    config: RoadmapConfig,\n  1155→    agents_explicit: bool,\n  1156→    depth_explicit: bool,\n  1157→) -> RoadmapConfig:\n  1158→    \"\"\"Restore agent/depth specs from .roadmap-state.json when not explicit.\n  1159→\n  1160→    On --resume, if the user did not explicitly pass --agents or --depth,\n  1161→    restore the values from the state file so the pipeline uses the same\n  1162→    configuration as the original run.\n  1163→    \"\"\"\n  1164→    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n  1165→    state = read_state(state_file)\n  1166→    if state is None:\n  1167→        if not agents_explicit or not depth_explicit:\n  1168→            print(\n  1169→                \"WARNING: --resume with no state file found. \"\n  1170→                \"Using defaults for unspecified options.\",\n  1171→                file=sys.stderr,\n  1172→                flush=True,\n  1173→            )\n  1174→        _log.info(\"No state file found; using CLI agents for fresh run\")\n  1175→        return config\n  1176→\n  1177→    # Restore agents from state if user did not explicitly pass --agents\n  1178→    if not agents_explicit:\n  1179→        saved_agents = state.get(\"agents\")\n  1180→        if saved_agents and isinstance(saved_agents, list):\n  1181→            try:\n  1182→                restored = [\n  1183→                    AgentSpec(model=a[\"model\"], persona=a[\"persona\"])\n  1184→                    for a in saved_agents\n  1185→                ]\n  1186→            except (KeyError, TypeError) as exc:\n  1187→                _log.warning(\n  1188→                    \"Malformed agents in state file (%s); using CLI agents\", exc\n  1189→                )\n  1190→                return config\n  1191→\n  1192→            if restored != config.agents:\n  1193→                agent_str = \", \".join(f\"{a.model}:{a.persona}\" for a in restored)\n  1194→                print(\n  1195→                    f\"[roadmap] Restoring agents from state file: {agent_str}\",\n  1196→                    flush=True,\n  1197→                )\n  1198→            config.agents = restored\n  1199→        else:\n  1200→            _log.warning(\"State file has no agents key; using CLI agents\")\n  1201→\n  1202→    # Restore depth from state if user did not explicitly pass --depth\n  1203→    if not depth_explicit:\n  1204→        saved_depth = state.get(\"depth\")\n  1205→        if saved_depth:\n  1206→            if saved_depth != config.depth:\n  1207→                print(\n  1208→                    f\"WARNING: --depth mismatch.\\n\"\n  1209→                    f\"  State file depth: {saved_depth}\\n\"\n  1210→                    f\"  Current depth:    {config.depth}\\n\"\n  1211→                    f\"  Using state file depth: {saved_depth}\\n\",\n  1212→                    file=sys.stderr,\n  1213→                    flush=True,\n  1214→                )\n  1215→            config.depth = saved_depth\n  1216→\n  1217→    return config\n  1218→\n  1219→\n  1220→def execute_roadmap(\n  1221→    config: RoadmapConfig,\n  1222→    resume: bool = False,\n  1223→    no_validate: bool = False,\n  1224→    auto_accept: bool = False,\n  1225→    agents_explicit: bool = True,\n  1226→    depth_explicit: bool = True,\n  1227→) -> None:\n  1228→    \"\"\"Execute the roadmap generation pipeline.\n  1229→\n  1230→    Builds the step list, handles --dry-run and --resume, then\n  1231→    delegates to execute_pipeline() with roadmap_run_step.\n  1232→\n  1233→    After all 9 steps pass, auto-invokes validation unless\n  1234→    --no-validate is set or --resume halted on a failed step.\n  1235→\n  1236→    Note: --no-validate does NOT skip the spec-fidelity step\n  1237→    (FR-010, AC-005). It only skips the post-pipeline validation\n  1238→    subsystem.\n  1239→\n  1240→    Args:\n  1241→        auto_accept: When True, the spec-patch resume cycle skips the\n  1242→            interactive prompt and proceeds automatically if evidence is\n  1243→            found. When False (default), the cycle prompts the user.\n  1244→            This is an internal parameter, not exposed on the CLI.\n  1245→        agents_explicit: Whether --agents was explicitly passed on the CLI.\n  1246→        depth_explicit: Whether --depth was explicitly passed on the CLI.\n  1247→    \"\"\"\n  1248→    config.output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n  1249→\n  1250→    # On --resume with defaulted agents/depth, restore from state file\n  1251→    if resume:\n  1252→        config = _restore_from_state(\n  1253→            config,\n  1254→            agents_explicit=agents_explicit,\n  1255→            depth_explicit=depth_explicit,\n  1256→        )\n  1257→\n  1258→    # Apply hardcoded defaults for any still-None fields (non-resume or no state)\n  1259→    if not config.agents:\n  1260→        config.agents = [AgentSpec(\"opus\", \"architect\"), AgentSpec(\"haiku\", \"architect\")]\n  1261→    if not config.depth:\n  1262→        config.depth = \"standard\"\n  1263→\n  1264→    # FR-2.24.1.11: Recursion guard — local variable, per-invocation\n  1265→    _spec_patch_cycle_count = 0\n  1266→\n  1267→    # FR-2.24.1.9 Condition 3: Capture spec hash at function entry\n  1268→    initial_spec_hash = hashlib.sha256(config.spec_file.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n  1269→\n  1270→    steps = _build_steps(config)\n  1271→\n  1272→    # --dry-run: print plan and exit\n  1273→    if config.dry_run:\n  1274→        _dry_run_output(steps)\n  1275→        return\n  1276→\n  1277→    # --resume: check which steps already pass their gates\n  1278→    if resume:\n  1279→        from ..pipeline.gates import gate_passed\n  1280→\n  1281→        steps = _apply_resume(steps, config, gate_passed)\n  1282→\n  1283→    # Execute pipeline\n  1284→    results = execute_pipeline(\n  1285→        steps=steps,\n  1286→        config=config,\n  1287→        run_step=roadmap_run_step,\n  1288→        on_step_start=_print_step_start,\n  1289→        on_step_complete=_print_step_complete,\n  1290→    )\n  1291→\n  1292→    # Save state\n  1293→    _save_state(config, results)\n  1294→\n  1295→    # Check for failures\n  1296→    failures = [r for r in results if r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)]\n  1297→    if failures:\n  1298→        # FR-2.24.1.9: Check if spec-fidelity failed and auto-resume is possible\n  1299→        spec_fidelity_failed = any(\n  1300→            r.step\n  1301→            and r.step.id == \"spec-fidelity\"\n  1302→            and r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)\n  1303→            for r in results\n  1304→        )\n  1305→\n  1306→        if spec_fidelity_failed:\n  1307→            resumed = _apply_resume_after_spec_patch(\n  1308→                config=config,\n  1309→                results=results,\n  1310→                auto_accept=auto_accept,\n  1311→                initial_spec_hash=initial_spec_hash,\n  1312→                cycle_count=_spec_patch_cycle_count,\n  1313→            )\n  1314→            if resumed:\n  1315→                _spec_patch_cycle_count += 1\n  1316→                # Cycle complete — resumed pipeline ran to completion or failure\n  1317→                # inside _apply_resume_after_spec_patch. If it returned True,\n  1318→                # we're done (either success or failure was handled internally).\n  1319→                return\n  1320→\n  1321→        halt_msg = _format_halt_output(results, config)\n  1322→        print(halt_msg, file=sys.stderr)\n  1323→        sys.exit(1)\n  1324→\n  1325→    print(f\"\\n[roadmap] Pipeline complete: {len(results)} steps passed\", flush=True)\n  1326→\n  1327→    # Auto-invoke validation after successful pipeline completion\n  1328→    if no_validate:\n  1329→        print(\"[roadmap] Validation skipped (--no-validate)\", flush=True)\n  1330→        _save_validation_status(config, \"skipped\")\n  1331→        return\n  1332→\n  1333→    # Check if validation already completed (--resume path)\n  1334→    if resume:\n  1335→        state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n  1336→        state = read_state(state_file)\n  1337→        if state and \"validation\" in state:\n  1338→            saved = state[\"validation\"]\n  1339→            if saved.get(\"status\") in (\"pass\", \"fail\"):\n  1340→                print(\n  1341→                    f\"[roadmap] Validation already completed ({saved['status']}), skipping\",\n  1342→                    flush=True,\n  1343→                )\n  1344→                return\n  1345→\n  1346→    _auto_invoke_validate(config)\n  1347→\n  1348→\n  1349→def _find_qualifying_deviation_files(\n  1350→    config: RoadmapConfig,\n  1351→    results: list[StepResult],\n  1352→) -> list:\n  1353→    \"\"\"Find deviation files written after spec-fidelity started.\n  1354→\n  1355→    Returns qualifying DeviationRecord objects, or empty list if\n  1356→    conditions are not met.\n  1357→\n  1358→    Implementation detail — not specified in the spec. Extracted for\n  1359→    testability of the three-condition detection gate.\n  1360→    \"\"\"\n  1361→    from .spec_patch import scan_accepted_deviation_records\n  1362→\n  1363→    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n  1364→    state = read_state(state_file)\n  1365→    if state is None:\n  1366→        return []\n  1367→\n  1368→    # Get spec-fidelity started_at timestamp\n  1369→    steps_state = state.get(\"steps\", {})\n  1370→    fidelity_state = steps_state.get(\"spec-fidelity\", {})\n  1371→    started_at_str = fidelity_state.get(\"started_at\")\n  1372→\n  1373→    # Fail-closed: if started_at is absent, condition not met\n  1374→    if not started_at_str:\n  1375→        return []\n  1376→\n  1377→    try:\n  1378→        started_at_ts = datetime.fromisoformat(started_at_str).timestamp()\n  1379→    except (ValueError, TypeError):\n  1380→        return []\n  1381→\n  1382→    # Scan all deviation records\n  1383→    all_records = scan_accepted_deviation_records(config.output_dir)\n  1384→    if not all_records:\n  1385→        return []\n  1386→\n  1387→    # Filter to records written AFTER spec-fidelity started.\n  1388→    # Strict > (not >=): a record with mtime == started_at was written before\n  1389→    # or exactly at step start, so it cannot be evidence of a subprocess fix.\n  1390→    # Caveat: HFS+ and some NFS mounts have 1-second mtime resolution, so\n  1391→    # records created within the same second as started_at may be missed.\n  1392→    qualifying = [r for r in all_records if r.mtime > started_at_ts]\n  1393→    return qualifying\n  1394→\n  1395→\n  1396→def _apply_resume_after_spec_patch(\n  1397→    config: RoadmapConfig,\n  1398→    results: list[StepResult],\n  1399→    auto_accept: bool,\n  1400→    initial_spec_hash: str,\n  1401→    cycle_count: int,\n  1402→) -> bool:\n  1403→    \"\"\"Attempt a single spec-patch auto-resume cycle after spec-fidelity FAIL.\n  1404→\n  1405→    Evaluates the three-condition detection gate (FR-2.24.1.9):\n  1406→      1. Recursion guard: cycle_count == 0\n  1407→      2. Qualifying deviation files exist with mtime > started_at\n  1408→      3. Spec file hash changed since run started (initial_spec_hash)\n  1409→\n  1410→    If all conditions pass, executes the six-step disk-reread sequence\n  1411→    (FR-2.24.1.10) and re-runs the pipeline via _apply_resume.\n  1412→\n  1413→    Single-writer assumption: no concurrent writer modifies\n  1414→    .roadmap-state.json between the reread and write steps.\n  1415→\n  1416→    Returns True if the cycle was attempted (regardless of outcome),\n  1417→    False if conditions were not met.\n  1418→    \"\"\"\n  1419→    # FR-2.24.1.11: Recursion guard\n  1420→    if cycle_count >= 1:\n  1421→        print(\n  1422→            \"[roadmap] Spec-patch cycle already exhausted \"\n  1423→            f\"(cycle_count={cycle_count}). Proceeding to normal failure.\",\n  1424→            flush=True,\n  1425→        )\n  1426→        return False\n  1427→\n  1428→    # FR-2.24.1.9 Condition 2: Qualifying deviation files\n  1429→    qualifying = _find_qualifying_deviation_files(config, results)\n  1430→    if not qualifying:\n  1431→        return False\n  1432→\n  1433→    # FR-2.24.1.9 Condition 3: Spec hash changed since run started\n  1434→    current_hash = hashlib.sha256(config.spec_file.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n  1435→    if current_hash == initial_spec_hash:\n  1436→        return False\n  1437→\n  1438→    # All three conditions met — enter the cycle\n  1439→    # FR-2.24.1.12: Cycle entry logging\n  1440→    print(\n  1441→        f\"[roadmap] Spec patched by subprocess. \"\n  1442→        f\"Found {len(qualifying)} accepted deviation record(s).\",\n  1443→        flush=True,\n  1444→    )\n  1445→    print(\n  1446→        \"[roadmap] Triggering spec-hash sync and resume (cycle 1/1).\",\n  1447→        flush=True,\n  1448→    )\n  1449→\n  1450→    # FR-2.24.1.10: Six-step disk-reread sequence\n  1451→    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n  1452→\n  1453→    # Step 1: Re-read state from disk\n  1454→    _fresh_state = read_state(state_file)  # noqa: F841\n  1455→\n  1456→    # Step 2: Recompute spec hash\n  1457→    new_hash = hashlib.sha256(config.spec_file.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n  1458→\n  1459→    # Step 3: Atomic write of new hash\n  1460→    try:\n  1461→        # Read again for fresh copy, update only spec_hash, write atomically\n  1462→        fresh_for_write = read_state(state_file) or {}\n  1463→        fresh_for_write[\"spec_hash\"] = new_hash\n  1464→        write_state(fresh_for_write, state_file)\n  1465→    except OSError as exc:\n  1466→        print(\n  1467→            f\"[roadmap] ERROR: Failed to update spec_hash during \"\n  1468→            f\"auto-resume cycle: {exc}. Falling through to normal failure.\",\n  1469→            file=sys.stderr,\n  1470→            flush=True,\n  1471→        )\n  1472→        return True  # Cycle was attempted but failed — don't retry\n  1473→\n  1474→    # Step 4: Re-read state from disk AGAIN (this is what _apply_resume gets)\n  1475→    post_write_state = read_state(state_file)\n  1476→    if post_write_state is None:\n  1477→        print(\n  1478→            \"[roadmap] ERROR: Could not re-read state after write. \"\n  1479→            \"Falling through to normal failure.\",\n  1480→            file=sys.stderr,\n  1481→            flush=True,\n  1482→        )\n  1483→        return True\n  1484→\n  1485→    # Step 5: Rebuild steps\n  1486→    steps = _build_steps(config)\n  1487→\n  1488→    # Step 6: Apply resume with post-write state\n  1489→    from ..pipeline.gates import gate_passed\n  1490→\n  1491→    steps = _apply_resume(steps, config, gate_passed)\n  1492→\n  1493→    # Re-execute pipeline from the resumed point\n  1494→    resumed_results = execute_pipeline(\n  1495→        steps=steps,\n  1496→        config=config,\n  1497→        run_step=roadmap_run_step,\n  1498→        on_step_start=_print_step_start,\n  1499→        on_step_complete=_print_step_complete,\n  1500→    )\n  1501→\n  1502→    # Save state from resumed run\n  1503→    _save_state(config, resumed_results)\n  1504→\n  1505→    # FR-2.24.1.12: Cycle completion logging\n  1506→    print(\"[roadmap] Spec-patch resume cycle complete.\", flush=True)\n  1507→\n  1508→    # Check if resumed pipeline also failed\n  1509→    resumed_failures = [\n  1510→        r for r in resumed_results if r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)\n  1511→    ]\n  1512→    if resumed_failures:\n  1513→        # FR-2.24.1.13: Normal failure on cycle exhaustion\n  1514→        # Use second-run results only\n  1515→        halt_msg = _format_halt_output(resumed_results, config)\n  1516→        print(halt_msg, file=sys.stderr)\n  1517→        sys.exit(1)\n  1518→\n  1519→    # Resumed pipeline succeeded\n  1520→    print(\n  1521→        f\"\\n[roadmap] Pipeline complete: {len(resumed_results)} steps passed\",\n  1522→        flush=True,\n  1523→    )\n  1524→    return True\n  1525→\n  1526→\n  1527→def _save_validation_status(\n  1528→    config: RoadmapConfig,\n  1529→    status: str,\n  1530→) -> None:\n  1531→    \"\"\"Update .roadmap-state.json with validation status.\n  1532→\n  1533→    Adds or updates the ``validation`` key without modifying existing state.\n  1534→\n  1535→    Parameters\n  1536→    ----------\n  1537→    config:\n  1538→        RoadmapConfig with output_dir.\n  1539→    status:\n  1540→        One of \"pass\", \"fail\", or \"skipped\".\n  1541→    \"\"\"\n  1542→    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n  1543→    state = read_state(state_file)\n  1544→    if state is None:\n  1545→        state = {}\n  1546→    state[\"validation\"] = {\n  1547→        \"status\": status,\n  1548→        \"timestamp\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n  1549→    }\n  1550→    write_state(state, state_file)\n  1551→\n  1552→\n  1553→def _auto_invoke_validate(config: RoadmapConfig) -> None:\n  1554→    \"\"\"Auto-invoke validation after a successful roadmap pipeline run.\n  1555→\n  1556→    Inherits --model, --max-turns, --debug from the parent roadmap config.\n  1557→    Default agent count for roadmap-run invocation is 2 (dual-agent for rigor).\n  1558→    \"\"\"\n  1559→    from .models import AgentSpec, ValidateConfig\n  1560→    from .validate_executor import execute_validate\n  1561→\n  1562→    # Default to 2 agents for roadmap-run auto-invocation (dual-agent for rigor per OQ-1)\n  1563→    validate_agents = config.agents[:2] if len(config.agents) >= 2 else config.agents\n  1564→\n  1565→    validate_config = ValidateConfig(\n  1566→        output_dir=config.output_dir,\n  1567→        agents=validate_agents,\n  1568→        work_dir=config.output_dir,\n  1569→        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n  1570→        model=config.model,\n  1571→        debug=config.debug,\n  1572→    )\n  1573→\n  1574→    print(\"\\n[roadmap] Auto-invoking validation...\", flush=True)\n  1575→\n  1576→    try:\n  1577→        counts = execute_validate(validate_config)\n  1578→        blocking = counts.get(\"blocking_count\", 0)\n  1579→        warning = counts.get(\"warning_count\", 0)\n  1580→        info = counts.get(\"info_count\", 0)\n  1581→        print(\n  1582→            f\"[validate] Complete: {blocking} blocking, {warning} warning, {info} info\",\n  1583→            flush=True,\n  1584→        )\n  1585→        validation_status = \"fail\" if blocking > 0 else \"pass\"\n  1586→        _save_validation_status(config, validation_status)\n  1587→    except FileNotFoundError as exc:\n  1588→        _log.error(\"Validation skipped: %s\", exc)\n  1589→        print(f\"[validate] Skipped: {exc}\", file=sys.stderr, flush=True)\n  1590→        _save_validation_status(config, \"skipped\")\n  1591→\n  1592→\n  1593→def check_remediate_resume(\n  1594→    config: RoadmapConfig,\n  1595→    gate_fn: Callable,\n  1596→) -> bool:\n  1597→    \"\"\"Check if the remediate step can be skipped on --resume.\n  1598→\n  1599→    Returns True (skip) when:\n  1600→    1. remediation-tasklist.md exists\n  1601→    2. Passes REMEDIATE_GATE\n  1602→    3. source_report_hash matches current validation report SHA-256\n  1603→\n  1604→    Returns False (re-run needed) otherwise.\n  1605→    \"\"\"\n  1606→    from .gates import REMEDIATE_GATE\n  1607→\n  1608→    tasklist_file = config.output_dir / \"remediation-tasklist.md\"\n  1609→    if not tasklist_file.exists():\n  1610→        return False\n  1611→\n  1612→    passed, _reason = gate_fn(tasklist_file, REMEDIATE_GATE)\n  1613→    if not passed:\n  1614→        return False\n  1615→\n  1616→    # Hash check: verify tasklist was generated from current validation report\n  1617→    if not _check_tasklist_hash_current(tasklist_file, config.output_dir):\n  1618→        print(\n  1619→            \"[roadmap] Remediation tasklist stale (hash mismatch), will re-run remediate\",\n  1620→            flush=True,\n  1621→        )\n  1622→        return False\n  1623→\n  1624→    return True\n  1625→\n  1626→\n  1627→def check_certify_resume(\n  1628→    config: RoadmapConfig,\n  1629→    gate_fn: Callable,\n  1630→) -> bool:\n  1631→    \"\"\"Check if the certify step can be skipped on --resume.\n  1632→\n  1633→    Returns True (skip) when:\n  1634→    1. certification-report.md exists\n  1635→    2. Passes CERTIFY_GATE\n  1636→\n  1637→    Returns False (re-run needed) otherwise.\n  1638→    \"\"\"\n  1639→    from .gates import CERTIFY_GATE\n  1640→\n  1641→    report_file = config.output_dir / \"certification-report.md\"\n  1642→    if not report_file.exists():\n  1643→        return False\n  1644→\n  1645→    passed, _reason = gate_fn(report_file, CERTIFY_GATE)\n  1646→    return passed\n  1647→\n  1648→\n  1649→def _check_tasklist_hash_current(\n  1650→    tasklist_file: Path,\n  1651→    output_dir: Path,\n  1652→) -> bool:\n  1653→    \"\"\"Check if remediation tasklist's source_report_hash matches current report.\n  1654→\n  1655→    Reads the YAML frontmatter source_report_hash and compares against\n  1656→    SHA-256 of the validation report file. Returns False on mismatch\n  1657→    (fail closed).\n  1658→    \"\"\"\n  1659→    from .gates import _parse_frontmatter\n  1660→\n  1661→    content = tasklist_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n  1662→    fm = _parse_frontmatter(content)\n  1663→    if fm is None:\n  1664→        return False\n  1665→\n  1666→    saved_hash = fm.get(\"source_report_hash\", \"\")\n  1667→    if not saved_hash:\n  1668→        return False\n  1669→\n  1670→    # Find the validation report (source_report field)\n  1671→    source_report = fm.get(\"source_report\", \"\")\n  1672→    if source_report:\n  1673→        report_path = Path(source_report)\n  1674→        if not report_path.is_absolute():\n  1675→            report_path = output_dir / report_path\n  1676→    else:\n  1677→        # Default to reflect-merged.md or merged-validation-report.md\n  1678→        report_path = output_dir / \"reflect-merged.md\"\n  1679→        if not report_path.exists():\n  1680→            report_path = output_dir / \"merged-validation-report.md\"\n  1681→\n  1682→    if not report_path.exists():\n  1683→        return False\n  1684→\n  1685→    current_hash = hashlib.sha256(report_path.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n  1686→    return saved_hash == current_hash\n  1687→\n  1688→\n  1689→def _step_needs_rerun(\n  1690→    step: Step,\n  1691→    gate_fn: Callable,\n  1692→    dirty_outputs: set[Path],\n  1693→    force_extract: bool,\n  1694→    state_paths: dict[str, Path],\n  1695→) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n  1696→    \"\"\"Determine if a single step needs re-running.\n  1697→\n  1698→    A step needs re-run if:\n  1699→    1. force_extract and step is 'extract', OR\n  1700→    2. Any of its input files are in the dirty set, OR\n  1701→    3. Its own gate check fails (using state-recorded paths when available).\n  1702→\n  1703→    Returns:\n  1704→        (needs_rerun: bool, reason: str)\n  1705→    \"\"\"\n  1706→    if force_extract and step.id == \"extract\":\n  1707→        return True, \"spec file changed; forcing extract rerun\"\n  1708→\n  1709→    # If any input was re-generated, must re-run\n  1710→    if any(inp in dirty_outputs for inp in (step.inputs or [])):\n  1711→        deps = [str(inp) for inp in (step.inputs or []) if inp in dirty_outputs]\n  1712→        return True, f\"input dependency regenerated: {deps}\"\n  1713→\n  1714→    # Defense-in-depth: use state-recorded path for gate check\n  1715→    check_path = state_paths.get(step.id, step.output_file)\n  1716→    if check_path != step.output_file:\n  1717→        _log.info(\n  1718→            \"Resume: step '%s' using state-recorded path %s \"\n  1719→            \"(config-derived: %s)\",\n  1720→            step.id,\n  1721→            check_path,\n  1722→            step.output_file,\n  1723→        )\n  1724→\n  1725→    # Log the failure reason with diagnostic hint\n  1731→        if reason and \"File not found\" in reason:\n  1732→            print(\n  1733→                f\"[roadmap] {step.id}: output missing ({check_path.name})\\n\"\n  1734→                f\"  Hint: Were different --agents used in the original run?\",\n  1735→                file=sys.stderr,\n  1736→                flush=True,\n  1737→            )\n  1738→        else:\n  1739→            print(f\"[roadmap] {step.id}: gate failed -- {reason}\", flush=True)\n  1740→        return True, f\"gate failed: {reason}\"\n  1741→\n  1742→    # No gate defined -- must run\n  1743→    return True, \"no gate defined\"\n  1744→\n  1745→\n  1746→def _apply_resume(\n  1747→    steps: list[Step | list[Step]],\n  1748→    config: RoadmapConfig,\n  1749→    gate_fn: Callable,\n  1750→) -> list[Step | list[Step]]:\n  1751→    \"\"\"Apply --resume logic: skip steps whose outputs pass gates.\n  1752→\n  1753→    Uses dependency tracking: if a step's inputs include the output of a\n  1754→    step that will be re-run, it must also re-run regardless of gate status.\n  1755→\n  1756→    Includes state-driven path resolution and parallel group semantics.\n  1757→    \"\"\"\n  1758→    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n  1759→    state = read_state(state_file)\n  1760→    force_extract = False\n  1761→\n  1762→    # State-driven path resolution: build lookup step_id -> recorded output_file\n  1763→    state_paths: dict[str, Path] = {}\n  1764→    if state is not None:\n  1765→        saved_hash = state.get(\"spec_hash\", \"\")\n  1766→        current_hash = hashlib.sha256(config.spec_file.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n  1767→        if saved_hash and saved_hash != current_hash:\n  1768→            print(\n  1769→                f\"WARNING: spec-file has changed since last run.\\n\"\n  1770→                f\"  Last hash: {saved_hash[:12]}...\\n\"\n  1771→                f\"  Current:   {current_hash[:12]}...\\n\"\n  1772→                f\"Forcing re-run of extract step.\",\n  1773→                file=sys.stderr,\n  1774→                flush=True,\n  1775→            )\n  1776→            force_extract = True\n  1777→\n  1778→        # files that will be regenerated\n  1787→\n  1788→    for entry in steps:\n  1789→        if isinstance(entry, list):\n  1790→            # Parallel group: check each step, re-run entire group if any needs it\n  1791→            group_needs_rerun = False\n  1792→            rerun_reasons: list[str] = []\n  1793→\n  1794→            for s in entry:\n  1795→                needs, reason = _step_needs_rerun(\n  1796→                    s, gate_fn, dirty_outputs, force_extract, state_paths,\n  1797→                )\n  1798→                if needs:\n  1799→                    group_needs_rerun = True\n  1800→                    rerun_reasons.append(f\"{s.id}: {reason}\")\n  1801→\n  1802→            if group_needs_rerun:\n  1803→                _log.info(\n  1804→                    \"Parallel group [%s] marked for rerun: %s\",\n  1805→                    \", \".join(s.id for s in entry),\n  1806→                    \"; \".join(rerun_reasons),\n  1807→                )\n  1808→                result.append(entry)\n  1809→                # files, skipped count).\n   151→    \"\"\"\n   152→    included: list[Path] = []\n   153→    skipped = 0\n   154→\n   155→    for py_file in sorted(source_dir.rglob(\"*.py\")):\n   156→        name = 194→\n   195→\n   196→def _extract_injectable_params(\n   197→    tree: ast.Module, file_path: str\n   198→) -> list[tuple[str, str, int]]:\n   199→    \"\"\"Extract Optional[Callable] constructor parameters.\n   200→\n   201→    Returns list of (class_name.param_name, param_name, line_number).\n   202→    \"\"\"\n   203→    injectables: list[tuple[str, str, int]] = []\n   204→\n   205→    for \"\"\"Check if an annotation represents Optional[Callable] or Callable | None.\"\"\"\n   234→    # Optional[Callable] -> Subscript(Name('Optional'), Name('Callable'))\n   235→    if isinstance(annotation, ast.Subscript):\n   236→        if isinstance(annotation.value, ast.Name) and annotation.value.id == \"Optional\":\n   237→            slice_val = annotation.slice\n   238→            if isinstance(slice_val, ast.Name) and slice_val.id == \"Callable\":\n   239→                return True\n   240→            # Optional[Callable[...]]\n   241→            if isinstance(slice_val, ast.Subscript):\n   242→                if isinstance(slice_val.value, ast.Name) and slice_val.value.id == \"Callable\":\n   243→                    return True\n   244→        # Also check Attribute form: typing.Optional\n   245→        if isinstance(annotation.value, ast.Attribute):\n   246→            if annotation.value.attr == \"Optional\":\n   247→                slice_val = annotation.slice\n   248→                if isinstance(slice_val, ast.Name) and slice_val.id == \"Callable\":\n   249→                    return True\n   250→\n   251→    # Callable | None -> BinOp(Name('Callable'), BitOr, Constant(None))\n   252→    if isinstance(annotation, ast.BinOp) and isinstance(annotation.op, ast.BitOr):\n   253→        left, right = annotation.left, annotation.right\n   254→        if (isinstance(left, ast.Name) and left.id == \"Callable\" and\n   255→                isinstance(right, ast.Constant) and right.value is None):\n   256→            return True\n   257→        if (isinstance(right, ast.Name) and right.id == \"Callable\" and\n   258→                isinstance(left, ast.Constant) and left.value is None):\n   259→            return True\n   260→\n   261→    return False\n   262→\n   263→\n   264→def _has_none_default(func_def: ast.FunctionDef, target_arg: ast.arg) -> bool:\n   265→    \"\"\"Check if the given arg has a default value of None.\"\"\"\n   266→    args = func_def.args\n   267→    # defaults apply to the last N positional args\n   268→    all_args = args.args\n   269→    defaults = args.defaults\n   270→\n   271→    if not defaults:\n   272→        return False\n   273→\n   274→    # Map defaults to args (right-aligned)\n   275→    offset = len(all_args) - len(defaults)\n   276→    for i, arg in enumerate(all_args):\n   277→        if arg is target_arg:\n   278→            default_idx = i - offset\n   279→            if 0 <= default_idx < len(defaults):\n   280→                default = defaults[default_idx]\n   281→                return isinstance(default, ast.Constant) and default.value is None\n   282→    return False\n   283→\n   284→\n   285→def _find_wiring_call_sites(\n   286→    param_name: str,\n   287→    class_name: str,\n   288→    trees: dict[Path, ast.Module],\n   289→) -> bool:\n   290→    \"\"\"Search for call sites that explicitly provide the parameter by keyword.\"\"\"\n   291→    for tree in trees.values():\n   292→        for node in ast.walk(tree):\n   293→            if isinstance(node, ast.Call):\n   294→                # Check keyword arguments\n   295→                for kw in node.keywords:\n   296→                    if kw.arg == param_name:\n   297→                        # Verify it's calling the right class\n   298→                        if _is_class_call(node, class_name):\n   299→                            return True\n   300→    return False\n   301→\n   302→\n   303→def _is_class_call(call: ast.Call, class_name: str) -> bool:\n   304→    \"\"\"Check if a Call node is calling the specified class.\"\"\"\n   305→    func = call.func\n   306→    if isinstance(func, ast.Name) and func.id == class_name:\n   307→        return True\n   308→    if isinstance(func, ast.Attribute) and func.attr == class_name:\n   309→        return True\n   310→    return False\n   311→\n   312→\n   313→def analyze_unwired_callables(\n   314→    config: WiringConfig,\n   315→    source_dir: Path,\n   316→) -> list[WiringFinding]:\n   317→    \"\"\"Detect injectable callables (Optional[Callable]) that are never wired.\n   318→\n   319→    Algorithm (section 5.2.1):\n   320→    1. Parse all Python files, extract Optional[Callable] constructor params\n   321→    2. For each injectable, search all files for keyword call sites\n   322→    3. If zero providers, emit unwired_callable finding\n   323→    4. Apply whitelist suppression\n   324→\n   325→    Args:\n   326→        config: Wiring analysis configuration.\n   327→        source_dir: Root directory to scan.\n   328→\n   329→    Returns:\n   330→        List of WiringFinding with finding_type=\"unwired_callable\".\n   331→    \"\"\"\n   332→    files, _ = _collect_python_files(source_dir, config.exclude_patterns)\n   333→    trees: dict[Path, ast.Module] = {}\n   334→\n   335→    for f in files:\n   336→        tree = _safe_parse(f)\n   337→        if tree is not None:\n   338→            trees[f] = tree\n   339→\n   340→    findings: list[WiringFinding] = []\n   341→\n   342→    for file_path, tree in trees.items():\n   343→        injectables = _extract_injectable_params(tree, str(file_path))\n   344→        for symbol, param_name, lineno in injectables:\n   345→            class_name = symbol.split(\".\")[0]\n   346→            if not _find_wiring_call_sites(param_name, class_name, trees):\n   347→                findings.append(\n   348→                    WiringFinding(\n   349→                        finding_type=\"unwired_callable\",\n   350→                        file_path=str(file_path),\n   351→                        symbol_name=symbol,\n   352→                        line_number=lineno,\n   353→                        detail=(\n   354→                            f\"Parameter '{param_name}' typed Optional[Callable] \"\n   355→                            f\"with default None is never wired by any call site\"\n   356→                        ),\n   357→                        severity=\"critical\",\n   358→                    )\n   359→                )\n   360→\n   361→    # Apply whitelist\n   362→    whitelist = load_whitelist(config.whitelist_path, config.rollout_mode)\n   363→    return _apply_whitelist(findings, whitelist)\n   364→\n   365→\n   366→# --- G-002: Orphan Module Analysis (section 5.2.2) ---\n   367→\n   368→\n   369→def _find_provider_dirs(source_dir: Path, provider_names: frozenset[str]) -> list[Path]:\n   370→    \"\"\"Find directories matching provider_dir_names convention.\"\"\"\n   371→    providers: list[Path] = []\n   372→    for d in sorted(source_dir.rglob(\"*\")):\n   373→        if d.is_dir() and d.name in provider_names:\n   374→            providers.append(d)\n   375→    return providers\n   376→\n   377→\n   378→def _extract_imports(tree: ast.Module) -> set[str]:\n   379→    \"\"\"Extract all imported module/symbol names from an AST.\"\"\"\n   380→    imports: set[str] = set()\n   381→    for node in ast.walk(tree):\n   382→        if isinstance(node, ast.Import):\n   383→            for alias in node.names:\n   384→                imports.add(alias.name)\n   385→        elif isinstance(node, ast.ImportFrom):\n   386→            if node.module:\n   387→                imports.add(node.module)\n   388→                for alias in node.names:\n   389→                    imports.add(f\"{node.module}.{alias.name}\")\n   390→    return imports\n   391→\n   392→\n   393→def analyze_orphan_modules(\n   394→    config: WiringConfig,\n   395→    source_dir: Path,\n   396→) -> list[WiringFinding]:\n   397→    \"\"\"Detect Python modules in provider directories with zero inbound imports.\n   398→\n   399→    Algorithm (section 5.2.2):\n   400→    1. Find provider directories by config.provider_dir_names convention\n   401→    2. For each Python file in provider dirs, check if any file outside\n   402→       the provider dir imports it\n   403→    3. If zero importers, emit orphan_module finding\n   404→    4. Apply exclude_patterns and whitelist suppression\n   405→\n   406→    Args:\n   407→        config: Wiring analysis configuration.\n   408→        source_dir: Root directory to scan.\n   409→\n   410→    Returns:\n   411→        List of WiringFinding with finding_type=\"orphan_module\".\n   412→    \"\"\"\n   413→    provider_dirs = _find_provider_dirs(source_dir, config.provider_dir_names)\n   414→    if not provider_dirs:\n   415→        return []\n   416→\n   417→    # Collect all Python files and parse them for imports\n   418→    all_files, _ = _collect_python_files(source_dir, [])\n   419→    all_imports: set[str] = set()\n   420→    trees: dict[Path, ast.Module] = {}\n   421→\n   422→    for f in all_files:\n   423→        tree = _safe_parse(f)\n   424→        if tree is not None:\n   425→            trees[f] = tree\n   426→\n   427→    # Gather imports from files OUTSIDE provider directories\n   428→    for f, tree in trees.items():\n   429→        is_in_provider = any(\n   430→            f.resolve().is_relative_to(pd.resolve()) for pd in provider_dirs\n   431→        )\n   432→        if not is_in_provider:\n   433→            all_imports.update(_extract_imports(tree))\n   434→\n   435→    findings: list[WiringFinding] = []\n   436→\n   437→    for provider_dir in provider_dirs:\n   438→        for py_file in sorted(provider_dir.glob(\"*.py\")):\n   439→            # Apply exclude patterns\n   440→            if any(fnmatch(py_file.name, pat) for pat in config.exclude_patterns):\n   441→                continue\n   442→\n   443→            # Derive the module path components\n   444→            try:\n   445→                rel = py_file.relative_to(source_dir)\n   446→            except ValueError:\n   447→                continue\n   448→\n   449→            # Build possible import names\n   450→            module_stem = py_file.stem\n   451→            module_dotpath = str(rel.with_suffix(\"\")).replace(\"/\", \".\").replace(\"\\\\\", \".\")\n   452→\n   453→            # Check if any external file imports this module\n   454→            is_imported = False\n   455→            for imp in all_imports:\n   456→                if module_dotpath in imp or module_stem in imp:\n   457→                    is_imported = True\n   458→                    break\n   459→\n   460→            if not is_imported:\n   461→                findings.append(\n   462→                    WiringFinding(\n   463→                        finding_type=\"orphan_module\",\n   464→                        file_path=str(py_file),\n   465→                        symbol_name=module_dotpath,\n   466→                        line_number=1,\n   467→                        detail=(\n   468→                            f\"Module '{module_dotpath}' in provider directory \"\n   469→                            f\"'{provider_dir.name}' has zero inbound imports\"\n   470→                        ),\n   471→                        severity=\"major\",\n   472→                    )\n   473→                )\n   474→\n   475→    # Apply whitelist\n   476→    whitelist = load_whitelist(config.whitelist_path, config.rollout_mode)\n   477→    return _apply_whitelist(findings, whitelist)\n   478→\n   479→\n   480→# --- G-003: Registry Analysis (section 5.2.3) ---\n   481→\n   482→\n   483→def _matches_registry_pattern(\n   484→    name: str, patterns: tuple[re.Pattern[str], ...]\n   485→) -> bool:\n   486→    \"\"\"Check if a variable name matches any registry detection pattern.\"\"\"\n   487→    return any(p.match(name) for p in patterns)\n   488→\n   489→\n   490→def _resolve_name_in_scope(\n   491→    name: str, tree: ast.Module, all_imports: set[str]\n   492→) -> bool:\n   493→    \"\"\"Check if a name is resolvable: defined in module or importable.\"\"\"\n   494→    # Check module-level definitions\n   495→    for node in ast.iter_child_nodes(tree):\n   496→        if isinstance(node, (ast.FunctionDef, ast.AsyncFunctionDef)):\n   497→            if node.name == name:\n   498→                return True\n   499→        elif isinstance(node, ast.ClassDef):\n   500→            if node.name == name:\n   501→                return True\n   502→        elif isinstance(node, ast.Assign):\n   503→            for target in node.targets:\n   504→                if isinstance(target, ast.Name) and target.id == name:\n   505→                    return True\n   506→\n   507→    # Check if importable\n   508→    if name in all_imports:\n   509→        return True\n   510→\n   511→    return False\n   512→\n   513→\n   514→def analyze_registries(\n   515→    config: WiringConfig,\n   516→    source_dir: Path,\n   517→) -> list[WiringFinding]:\n   518→    \"\"\"Detect broken dispatch registry entries referencing nonexistent callables.\n   519→\n   520→    Algorithm (section 5.2.3):\n   521→    1. Scan files for module-level dict assignments matching registry patterns\n   522→    2. For each dict entry, verify the value references an importable callable\n   523→    3. Unresolved references emit unwired_registry findings\n   524→\n   525→    Args:\n   526→        config: Wiring analysis configuration.\n   527→        source_dir: Root directory to scan.\n   528→\n   529→    Returns:\n   530→        List of WiringFinding with finding_type=\"unwired_registry\".\n   531→    \"\"\"\n   532→    files, _ = _collect_python_files(source_dir, config.exclude_patterns)\n   533→    trees: dict[Path, ast.Module] = {}\n   534→    all_imports_by_file: dict[Path, set[str]] = {}\n   535→\n   536→    for f in files:\n   537→        tree = _safe_parse(f)\n   538→        if tree is not None:\n   539→            trees[f] = tree\n   540→            all_imports_by_file[f] = _extract_imports(tree)\n   541→\n   542→    findings: list[WiringFinding] = []\n   543→\n   544→    for file_path, tree in trees.items():\n   545→        file_imports = all_imports_by_file.get(file_path, set())\n   546→\n   547→        for node in ast.iter_child_nodes(tree):\n   548→            if not isinstance(node, ast.Assign):\n   549→                continue\n   550→\n   551→            for target in node.targets:\n   552→                if not isinstance(target, ast.Name):\n   553→                    continue\n   554→                if not _matches_registry_pattern(target.id, config.registry_patterns):\n   555→                    continue\n   556→\n   557→                # This is a registry assignment — check if value is a dict\n   558→                if not isinstance(node.value, ast.Dict):\n   559→                    continue\n   560→\n   561→                for key_node, val_node in zip(node.value.keys, node.value.values):\n   562→                    if val_node is None:\n   563→                        continue\n   564→\n   565→                    # Explicit None is allowed\n   566→                    if isinstance(val_node, ast.Constant) and val_node.value is None:\n   567→                        continue\n   568→\n   569→                    # Name reference — must be resolvable\n   570→                    if isinstance(val_node, ast.Name):\n   571→                        if not _resolve_name_in_scope(\n   572→                            val_node.id, tree, file_imports\n   573→                        ):\n   574→                            key_repr = (\n   575→                                ast.literal_eval(key_node)\n   576→                                if isinstance(key_node, ast.Constant)\n   577→                                else \"<dynamic>\"\n   578→                            )\n   579→                            findings.append(\n   580→                                WiringFinding(\n   581→                                    finding_type=\"unwired_registry\",\n   582→                                    file_path=str(file_path),\n   583→                                    symbol_name=f\"{target.id}[{key_repr!r}]\",\n   584→                                    line_number=val_node.lineno,\n   585→                                    detail=(\n   586→                                        f\"Registry '{target.id}' entry \"\n   587→                                        f\"'{key_repr}' references unresolvable \"\n   588→                                        f\"callable '{val_node.id}'\"\n   589→                                    ),\n   590→                                    severity=\"critical\",\n   591→                                )\n   592→                            )\n   593→\n   594→                    # String reference — try to resolve as dotted path\n   595→                    elif isinstance(val_node, ast.Constant) and isinstance(\n   596→                        val_node.value, str\n   597→                    ):\n   598→                        ref = val_node.value\n   599→                        parts = ref.rsplit(\".\", 1)\n   600→                        func_name = parts[-1] if len(parts) > 1 else ref\n   601→                        if not _resolve_name_in_scope(\n   602→                            func_name, tree, file_imports\n   603→                        ):\n   604→                            key_repr = (\n   605→                                ast.literal_eval(key_node)\n   606→                                if isinstance(key_node, ast.Constant)\n   607→                                else \"<dynamic>\"\n   608→                            )\n   609→                            findings.append(\n   610→                                WiringFinding(\n   611→                                    finding_type=\"unwired_registry\",\n   612→                                    file_path=str(file_path),\n   613→                                    symbol_name=f\"{target.id}[{key_repr!r}]\",\n   614→                                    line_number=val_node.lineno,\n   615→                                    detail=(\n   616→                                        f\"Registry '{target.id}' entry \"\n   617→                                        f\"'{key_repr}' references unresolvable \"\n   618→                                        f\"string path '{ref}'\"\n   619→                                    ),\n   620→                                    severity=\"critical\",\n   621→                                )\n   622→                            )\n   623→\n   624→    # Apply whitelist\n   625→    whitelist = load_whitelist(config.whitelist_path, config.rollout_mode)\n   626→    return _apply_whitelist(findings, whitelist)\n   627→\n   628→\n   629→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   630→# Top-level entry point\n   631→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   632→\n   633→\n   634→def run_wiring_analysis(\n   635→    config: WiringConfig,\n   636→    source_dir: Path,\n   637→) -> WiringReport:\n   638→    \"\"\"Run all three wiring analyzers and produce a WiringReport.\n   639→\n   640→    Args:\n   641→        config: Wiring analysis configuration.\n   642→        source_dir: Root directory to scan.\n   643→\n   644→    Returns:\n   645→        WiringReport aggregating findings from all analyzers.\n   646→    \"\"\"\n   647→    start = time.monotonic()\n   648→\n   649→    _, skipped = _collect_python_files(source_dir, config.exclude_patterns)\n   650→    files, _ = _collect_python_files(source_dir, config.exclude_patterns)\n   651→    files_analyzed = len(files)\n   652→\n   653→    unwired = analyze_unwired_callables(config, source_dir)\n   654→    orphans = analyze_orphan_modules(config, source_dir)\n   655→    registries = analyze_registries(config, source_dir)\n   656→\n   657→    duration = time.monotonic() - start\n   658→\n   659→    return WiringReport(\n   660→        target_dir=str(source_dir),\n   661→        files_analyzed=files_analyzed,\n   662→        files_skipped=skipped,\n   663→        unwired_callables=unwired,\n   664→        orphan_modules=orphans,\n   665→        unwired_registries=registries,\n   666→        scan_duration_seconds=round(duration, 4),\n   667→        rollout_mode=config.rollout_mode,\n   668→    )\n   669→\n   670→\n   671→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   672→# Report emission (section 5.4)\n   673→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   674→\n   675→\n   676→def emit_report(report: WiringReport, output_path: Path) -> Path:\n   677→    \"\"\"Write a wiring verification report with YAML frontmatter and 7 Markdown sections.\n   678→\n   679→    Frontmatter contains 16 fields per section 5.6. String fields are serialized\n   680→    with yaml.safe_dump() to prevent YAML injection (section 5.4).\n   681→\n   682→    Args:\n   683→        report: Aggregated wiring analysis report.\n   684→        output_path: Destination file path.\n   685→\n   686→    Returns:\n   687→        The output_path after writing.\n   688→    \"\"\"\n   689→    # Compute severity counts from unsuppressed findings\n   690→    unsuppressed = report.unsuppressed_findings\n   691→    critical_count = sum(1 for f in unsuppressed if f.severity == \"critical\")\n   692→    major_count = sum(1 for f in unsuppressed if f.severity == \"major\")\n   693→    info_count = sum(1 for f in unsuppressed if f.severity == \"info\")\n   694→    suppressed_count = sum(1 for f in report.all_findings if f.suppressed)\n   695→\n   696→    # blocking_findings depends on rollout_mode\n   697→    blocking = report.blocking_count()\n   698→\n   699→    # Build frontmatter dict (16 fields per section 5.6)\n   700→    fm: dict = {\n   701→        \"gate\": \"wiring-verification\",\n   702→        \"target_dir\": report.target_dir,\n   703→        \"files_analyzed\": report.files_analyzed,\n   704→        \"files_skipped\": report.files_skipped,\n   705→        \"rollout_mode\": report.rollout_mode,\n   706→        \"analysis_complete\": True,\n   707→        \"audit_artifacts_used\": 0,\n   708→        \"unwired_callable_count\": len([\n   709→            f for f in report.unwired_callables if not f.suppressed\n   710→        ]),\n   711→        \"orphan_module_count\": len([\n   712→            f for f in report.orphan_modules if not f.suppressed\n   713→        ]),\n   714→        \"unwired_registry_count\": len([\n   715→            f for f in report.unwired_registries if not f.suppressed\n   716→        ]),\n   717→        \"critical_count\": critical_count,\n   718→        \"major_count\": major_count,\n   719→        \"info_count\": info_count,\n   720→        \"total_findings\": len(unsuppressed),\n   721→        \"blocking_findings\": blocking,\n   722→        \"whitelist_entries_applied\": suppressed_count,\n   723→    }\n   724→\n   725→    # Serialize frontmatter with yaml.safe_dump for string safety\n   726→    fm_yaml = yaml.safe_dump(fm, default_flow_style=False, sort_keys=False)\n   727→\n   728→    # Build Markdown body with 7 sections (section 5.4.1)\n   729→    sections: list[str] = []\n   730→\n   731→    # 1. Summary\n   732→    sections.append(\"## Summary\\n\")\n   733→    sections.append(\n   734→        f\"- **Target**: {report.target_dir}\\n\"\n   735→        f\"- **Files analyzed**: {report.files_analyzed}\\n\"\n   736→        f\"- **Files skipped**: {report.files_skipped}\\n\"\n   737→        f\"- **Rollout mode**: {report.rollout_mode}\\n\"\n   738→        f\"- **Total findings**: {len(unsuppressed)}\\n\"\n   739→        f\"- **Blocking findings**: {blocking}\\n\"\n   740→        f\"- **Scan duration**: {report.scan_duration_seconds:.4f}s\\n\"\n   741→    )\n   742→\n   743→    # 2. Unwired Optional Callable Injections\n   744→    sections.append(\"## Unwired Optional Callable Injections\\n\")\n   745→    uc = [f for f in report.unwired_callables if not f.suppressed]\n   746→    if uc:\n   747→        for f in uc:\n   748→            sections.append(\n   749→                f\"- **{f.symbol_name}** ({f.file_path}:{f.line_number}) \"\n   750→                f\"[{f.severity}]: {f.detail}\\n\"\n   751→            )\n   752→    else:\n   753→        sections.append(\"No unsuppressed unwired callable findings.\\n\")\n   754→\n   755→    # 3. Orphan Modules / Symbols\n   756→    sections.append(\"## Orphan Modules / Symbols\\n\")\n   757→    om = [f for f in report.orphan_modules if not f.suppressed]\n   758→    if om:\n   759→        for f in om:\n   760→            sections.append(\n   761→                f\"- **{f.symbol_name}** ({f.file_path}:{f.line_number}) \"\n   762→                f\"[{f.severity}]: {f.detail}\\n\"\n   763→            )\n   764→    else:\n   765→        sections.append(\"No unsuppressed orphan module findings.\\n\")\n   766→\n   767→    # 4. Unregistered Dispatch Entries\n   768→    sections.append(\"## Unregistered Dispatch Entries\\n\")\n   769→    ur = [f for f in report.unwired_registries if not f.suppressed]\n   770→    if ur:\n   771→        for f in ur:\n   772→            sections.append(\n   773→                f\"- **{f.symbol_name}** ({f.file_path}:{f.line_number}) \"\n   774→                f\"[{f.severity}]: {f.detail}\\n\"\n   775→            )\n   776→    else:\n   777→        sections.append(\"No unsuppressed registry findings.\\n\")\n   778→\n   779→    # 5. Suppressions and Dynamic Retention\n   780→    sections.append(\"## Suppressions and Dynamic Retention\\n\")\n   781→    suppressed_findings = [f for f in report.all_findings if f.suppressed]\n   782→    if suppressed_findings:\n   783→        for f in suppressed_findings:\n   784→            sections.append(\n   785→                f\"- **{f.symbol_name}** ({f.finding_type}): suppressed\\n\"\n   786→            )\n   787→    else:\n   788→        sections.append(\"No suppressions applied.\\n\")\n   789→    sections.append(f\"- Audit artifacts used: 0\\n\")\n   790→\n   791→    # 6. Recommended Remediation\n   792→    sections.append(\"## Recommended Remediation\\n\")\n   793→    if uc:\n   794→        sections.append(\n   795→            \"- **Unwired callables**: Wire Optional[Callable] parameters \"\n   796→            \"at call sites or add to whitelist if intentionally unused.\\n\"\n   797→        )\n   798→    if om:\n   799→        sections.append(\n   800→            \"- **Orphan modules**: Import orphan modules from consumer code \"\n   801→            \"or remove if no longer needed.\\n\"\n   802→        )\n   803→    if ur:\n   804→        sections.append(\n   805→            \"- **Broken registries**: Fix registry entries to reference \"\n   806→            \"resolvable callables or remove stale entries.\\n\"\n   807→        )\n   808→    if not uc and not om and not ur:\n   809→        sections.append(\"No remediation needed — all checks pass.\\n\")\n   810→\n   811→    # 7. Evidence and Limitations\n   812→    sections.append(\"## Evidence and Limitations\\n\")\n   813→    sections.append(\n   814→        \"- Analysis uses AST-based static analysis; dynamic imports and \"\n   815→        \"runtime wiring are not detected.\\n\"\n   816→        \"- Alias resolution is limited to direct name references.\\n\"\n   817→        \"- Registry detection uses pattern matching on variable names; \"\n   818→        \"non-conventional naming may be missed.\\n\"\n   819→    )\n   820→\n   821→    # Assemble full document\n   822→    body = \"\\n\".join(sections)\n   823→    content = f\"---\\n{fm_yaml}---\\n\\n{body}\"\n   824→\n   825→    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n   826→    output_path.write_text(content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   827→    return output_path\n   828→\n   829→\n   830→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   831→# Frontmatter extraction (section 5.5, NFR-007 compliant)\n   832→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   833→\n   834→# Duplicated from pipeline/gates.py:77 — NOT imported to preserve NFR-007 layering.\n   835→_FRONTMATTER_RE = re.compile(\n   836→    r\"^---[ \\t]*\\n((?:[ \\t]*\\w[\\w\\s]*:.*\\n)+)---[ \\t]*$\",\n   837→    re.MULTILINE,\n   838→)\n   839→\n   840→\n   841→def _extract_frontmatter_values(content: str) -> dict[str, str]:\n   842→    \"\"\"Extract frontmatter key-value pairs from report content.\n   843→\n   844→    Uses _FRONTMATTER_RE (duplicated from pipeline/gates.py to preserve\n   845→    NFR-007 layering) to extract YAML frontmatter key-value pairs.\n   846→\n   847→    Returns a dict mapping each key to its raw string value.\n   848→    Returns empty dict for content without valid frontmatter.\n   849→    \"\"\"\n   850→    match = _FRONTMATTER_RE.search(content)\n   851→    if match is None:\n   852→        return {}\n   853→\n   854→    result: dict[str, str] = {}\n   855→    for line in match.group(1).splitlines():\n   856→        line = line.strip()\n   857→        if \":\" in line:\n   858→            key, _, value = line.partition(\":\")\n   859→            key = key.strip()\n   860→            value = value.strip()\n   861→            if key:\n   862→                result[key] = value\n   863→    return result\n   864→\n   865→\n   866→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   867→# Semantic check functions (section 5.6)\n   868→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   869→\n   870→\n   871→def _analysis_complete_true(content: str) -> bool:\n   872→    \"\"\"Semantic check 1: analysis_complete must be true.\"\"\"\n   873→    fm = _extract_frontmatter_values(content)\n   874→    return fm.get(\"analysis_complete\", \"\").lower() == \"true\"\n   875→\n   876→\n   877→def _recognized_rollout_mode(content: str) -> bool:\n   878→    \"\"\"Semantic check 2: rollout_mode must be shadow, soft, or full.\"\"\"\n   879→    fm = _extract_frontmatter_values(content)\n   880→    return fm.get(\"rollout_mode\", \"\") in (\"shadow\", \"soft\", \"full\")\n   881→\n   882→\n   883→def _finding_counts_consistent(content: str) -> bool:\n   884→    \"\"\"Semantic check 3: total_findings == sum of category counts.\"\"\"\n   885→    fm = _extract_frontmatter_values(content)\n   886→    try:\n   887→        total = int(fm.get(\"total_findings\", \"-1\"))\n   888→        uc = int(fm.get(\"unwired_callable_count\", \"-1\"))\n   889→        om = int(fm.get(\"orphan_module_count\", \"-1\"))\n   890→        ur = int(fm.get(\"unwired_registry_count\", \"-1\"))\n   891→    except ValueError:\n   892→        return False\n   893→    return total == uc + om + ur\n   894→\n   895→\n   896→def _severity_summary_consistent(content: str) -> bool:\n   897→    \"\"\"Semantic check 4: critical + major + info == total_findings.\"\"\"\n   898→    fm = _extract_frontmatter_values(content)\n   899→    try:\n   900→        total = int(fm.get(\"total_findings\", \"-1\"))\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_9OupdsYuN7Ld3fVyqAdJOuxT","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"\"\"Pipeline gate validation -- pure Python, no subprocess, no LLM invocation.\n     2→\n     3→Validates step outputs against GateCriteria with tier-proportional checks:\n     4→  EXEMPT  -> always passes\n     5→  LIGHT   -> file exists + non-empty\n     6→  STANDARD -> + min lines + YAML frontmatter fields\n     7→  STRICT  -> + semantic checks (if defined)\n     8→\n     9→NFR-003: No subprocess import. EXEMPT: always passes\n    29→    if tier == \"EXEMPT\":\n    30→        return True, None\n    31→\n    32→    # LIGHT, STANDARD, STRICT: file must exist\n    33→    if not output_file.exists():\n    34→        return False, f\"File not found: {output_file}\"\n    35→\n    36→    # LIGHT, STANDARD, STRICT: file must be non-empty\n    37→    content = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    38→    if len(content.strip()) == 0:\n    39→        return False, f\"File empty (0 bytes): {output_file}\"\n    40→\n    41→    # LIGHT stops here\n    42→    if tier == \"LIGHT\":\n    43→        return True, None\n    44→\n    45→    # STANDARD, STRICT: minimum line count\n    46→    lines = content.splitlines()\n    47→    if len(lines) < criteria.min_lines:\n    48→        return False, (\n    49→            f\"Below minimum line count: {len(lines)} < {criteria.min_lines} \"\n    50→            f\"in {output_file}\"\n    51→        )\n    52→\n    53→    # STANDARD, STRICT: YAML frontmatter fields\n    54→    if criteria.required_frontmatter_fields:\n    55→        ok, reason = _check_frontmatter(\n    56→            content, criteria.required_frontmatter_fields, output_file\n    57→        )\n    58→        if not ok:\n    59→            return False, reason\n    60→\n    61→    # STANDARD stops here\n    62→    if tier == \"STANDARD\":\n    63→        return True, None\n    64→\n    65→    # STRICT: semantic checks\n    66→    if criteria.semantic_checks:\n    67→        for check in criteria.semantic_checks:\n    68→            if not check.check_fn(content):\n    69→                return (\n    70→                    False,\n    71→                    f\"Semantic check '{check.name}' failed: {check.failure_message}\",\n    72→                )\n    73→\n    74→    return True, None\n    75→\n    76→\n    77→_FRONTMATTER_RE = re.compile(\n    78→    r\"^---[ \\t]*\\n((?:[ \\t]*\\w[\\w\\s]*:.*\\n)+)---[ \\t]*$\",\n    79→    re.MULTILINE,\n    80→)\n    81→\n    82→\n    83→def _check_frontmatter(\n    84→    content: str, required_fields: list[str], output_file: Path\n    85→) -> tuple[bool, str | None]:\n    86→    \"\"\"Extract and validate YAML frontmatter fields.\n    87→\n    88→    Uses regex with re.MULTILINE to discover frontmatter anywhere in the\n    89→    document (not just byte 0), allowing conversational preamble before the\n    90→    ``---`` delimiters.  Horizontal rules (``---`` with no ``key: value``\n    91→    content) are rejected because the pattern requires at least one\n    92→    ``key: value`` line between delimiters.\n    93→    \"\"\"\n    94→    match = _FRONTMATTER_RE.search(content)\n    95→    if match is None:\n    96→        return False, f\"YAML frontmatter not found in {output_file}\"\n    97→\n    98→    frontmatter_text = match.group(1)\n    99→\n   100→    # Extract keys from key: value lines\n   101→    found_keys: set[str] = set()\n   102→    for line in frontmatter_text.splitlines():\n   103→        line = line.strip()\n   104→        if \":\" in line:\n   105→            key = line.split(\":\", 1)[0].strip()\n   106→            if key:\n   107→                found_keys.add(key)\n   108→\n   109→    for field in required_fields:\n   110→        if field not in found_keys:\n   111→            return (\n   112→                False,\n   113→                f\"Missing required frontmatter field '{field}' in {output_file}\",\n   114→            )\n   115→\n   116→    return True, None\n   117→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01Lct2REZ4DvB4XkM68gPVGv","session_id":"95da2ed5-221d-45a5-8aaa-b31414811865","uuid":"04c2e843-5eae-42e1-b2ba-0f2d7a071fd0","timestamp":"2026-03-19T04:50:48.095Z"}
