Uses `_DEPTH_INSTRUCTIONS[depth]`\n   - Frontmatter:\n     - `convergence_score`\n     - `rounds_completed`\n\n5. `build_score_prompt(debate_path: Path, variant_a_path: Path, variant_b_path: Path) -> str`\n   - Selects base variant and scores both.\n   - Frontmatter:\n     - `base_variant`\n     - `variant_scores`\n\n6. `build_merge_prompt(base_selection_path: Path, variant_a_path: Path, variant_b_path: Path, debate_path: Path) -> str`\n   - Produces merged roadmap.\n   - Frontmatter:\n     - `spec_source`\n     - `complexity_score`\n     - `adversarial: true`\n   - Also instructs:\n     - no heading gaps\n     - resolved internal cross-references\n     - no duplicate H2/H3 heading text\n\n7. `build_spec_fidelity_prompt(spec_file: Path, roadmap_path: Path) -> str`\n   - Compares spec vs roadmap.\n   - Embeds explicit HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW severity definitions.\n   - Explicit comparison dimensions:\n     - signatures\n     - data models\n     - gates\n     - CLI options\n     - NFRs\n   - Frontmatter:\n     - `high_severity_count`\n     - `medium_severity_count`\n     - `low_severity_count`\n     - `total_deviations`\n     - `validation_complete`\n     - `tasklist_ready`\n   - Body requires numbered deviation entries with:\n     - ID\n     - severity\n     - deviation\n     - spec quote\n     - roadmap quote\n     - impact\n     - recommended correction\n\n8. `build_test_strategy_prompt(roadmap_path: Path, extraction_path: Path) -> str`\n   - Produces roadmap test strategy.\n   - Frontmatter:\n     - `complexity_class`\n     - `validation_philosophy`\n     - `validation_milestones`\n     - `work_milestones`\n     - `interleave_ratio`\n     - `major_issue_policy`\n   - Hardcodes semantic mapping:\n     - LOW → `1:3`\n     - MEDIUM → `1:2`\n     - HIGH → `1:1`\n   - Requires issue classification table and milestone/test plan sections.\n\n### Notes\n- `spec_file`, `extraction_path`, etc. are accepted as parameters for semantic clarity, but these prompt builders mostly do not interpolate the actual path values into the text.\n- All prompt builders append `_OUTPUT_FORMAT_BLOCK`.\n\n## 2. `executor.py` full contents/structure\n\nFile:\n- `/config/workspace/IronClaude/src/superclaude/cli/roadmap/executor.py`\n\n### Purpose\nRoadmap pipeline executor. Builds steps, runs Claude subprocesses, handles resume/dry-run/state/validation/spec-patch cycle.\n\n### Imports\nKey imports:\n- stdlib: `hashlib`, `json`, `logging`, `sys`, `time`, `datetime`, `Path`, `Callable`\n- pipeline:\n  - `decompose_deliverables`\n  - `execute_pipeline`\n  - `Deliverable`, `PipelineConfig`, `Step`, `StepResult`, `StepStatus`\n  - `ClaudeProcess`\n- roadmap:\n  - gates constants\n  - `AgentSpec`, `RoadmapConfig`\n  - prompt builders\n  - `build_certification_prompt`\n\n### Module constants\n- `_MAX_ARG_STRLEN = 128 * 1024`\n- `_PROMPT_TEMPLATE_OVERHEAD = 8 * 1024`\n- `_EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT = _MAX_ARG_STRLEN - _PROMPT_TEMPLATE_OVERHEAD`\n\nThese support inline prompt embedding instead of `--file`.\n\n---\n\n### Helper functions before the main execution path\n\n#### `_embed_inputs(input_paths: list[Path]) -> str`\n- Reads each input file and appends content as fenced code blocks:\n  - header format: `# <path>`\n  - content fenced with triple backticks\n- Returns concatenated string or empty string.\n\n#### `_sanitize_output(output_file: Path) -> int`\n- Cleans output before gate validation.\n- Strips:\n  - leading whitespace\n  - conversational preamble before first `---`\n- Writes sanitized content back atomically via `.tmp` + `os.replace`.\n- Returns byte count removed.\n\n#### `_inject_pipeline_diagnostics(output_file: Path, started_at: datetime, finished_at: datetime) -> None`\n- Injects `pipeline_diagnostics` into extraction frontmatter if missing:\n  - `elapsed_seconds`\n  - `started_at`\n  - `finished_at`\n\n#### `_inject_provenance_fields(output_file: Path, spec_source: str) -> None`\n- Injects missing frontmatter fields into test-strategy output:\n  - `spec_source`\n  - `generated`\n  - `generator`\n\n---\n\n### `roadmap_run_step`\n\nSignature:\n```python\ndef roadmap_run_step(\n    step: Step,\n    config: PipelineConfig,\n    cancel_check: Callable[[], bool],\n) -> StepResult:\n```\n\nBehavior:\n- Records `started_at`\n- If `config.dry_run`, returns PASS immediately\n- Embeds `step.inputs` inline into prompt using `_embed_inputs`\n- Warns if composed prompt exceeds embed limit, but still embeds inline\n- Creates `ClaudeProcess` with:\n  - `prompt`\n  - `output_file`\n  - `.err` file\n  - `max_turns`\n  - `model`\n  - `permission_flag`\n  - `timeout_seconds`\n  - `output_format=\"text\"`\n- Starts process\n- Polls once per second for cancellation\n- If cancelled:\n  - terminates process\n  - returns `StepStatus.CANCELLED`\n- On exit:\n  - `124` => `TIMEOUT`\n  - nonzero => `FAIL`\n  - zero => sanitize output, inject metadata where needed, return `PASS`\n\nImportant post-processing:\n- for `extract`: `_inject_pipeline_diagnostics`\n- for `test-strategy`: `_inject_provenance_fields`\n\n---\n\n### `build_certify_step`\nBuilds a `Step` for certification:\n- id: `\"certify\"`\n- output: `certification-report.md`\n- gate: `CERTIFY_GATE`\n- input: `remediation-tasklist.md`\n\n---\n\n### `_build_steps`\n\nSignature:\n```python\ndef _build_steps(config: RoadmapConfig) -> list[Step | list[Step]]:\n```\n\nThis is the key roadmap pipeline builder.\n\n#### Current pipeline shape\nIt currently builds **8 executable pipeline entries**, where one entry is a parallel group:\n\n1. `extract`\n2. parallel group:\n   - `generate-{agent_a.id}`\n   - `generate-{agent_b.id}`\n3. `diff`\n4. `debate`\n5. `score`\n6. `merge`\n7. `test-strategy`\n8. `spec-fidelity`\n\n#### Notably absent from `_build_steps`\nAlthough `_get_all_step_ids` includes them, `_build_steps` does **not** create:\n- `remediate`\n- `certify`\n\n#### Per-step outputs\n- extraction: `extraction.md`\n- generate A: `roadmap-{agent_a.id}.md`\n- generate B: `roadmap-{agent_b.id}.md`\n- diff: `diff-analysis.md`\n- debate: `debate-transcript.md`\n- score: `base-selection.md`\n- merge: `roadmap.md`\n- test-strategy: `test-strategy.md`\n- spec-fidelity: `spec-fidelity.md`\n\n#### Inputs by step\n- extract: `[config.spec_file]`\n- generate-* : `[extraction]`\n- diff: `[roadmap_a, roadmap_b]`\n- debate: `[diff_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b]`\n- score: `[debate_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b]`\n- merge: `[score_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b, debate_file]`\n- test-strategy: `[merge_file, extraction]`\n- spec-fidelity: `[config.spec_file, merge_file]`\n\n#### Other behavior\n- Reads optional retrospective file into `retrospective_content`\n- Uses `build_extract_prompt(... retrospective_content=...)`\n- Uses per-agent models on generate steps\n\n---\n\n### `_get_all_step_ids`\n\nSignature:\n```python\ndef _get_all_step_ids(config: RoadmapConfig) -> list[str]:\n```\n\nReturns:\n```python\n[\n    \"extract\",\n    f\"generate-{agent_a.id}\",\n    f\"generate-{agent_b.id}\",\n    \"diff\",\n    \"debate\",\n    \"score\",\n    \"merge\",\n    \"test-strategy\",\n    \"spec-fidelity\",\n    \"remediate\",\n    \"certify\",\n]\n```\n\nImportant mismatch:\n- `_get_all_step_ids` includes `remediate` and `certify`\n- `_build_steps` does not build them\n\nThis likely matters for resume/halt reporting.\n\n---\n\n### Other notable executor functions\n\n#### Halt/progress/state helpers\n- `_format_halt_output`\n- `_print_terminal_halt`\n- `_print_step_start`\n- `_print_step_complete`\n- `_dry_run_output`\n- `_print_step_plan`\n\n#### Freshness/budget logic\n- `_check_annotate_deviations_freshness`\n- `_check_remediation_budget`\n\n#### State handling\n- `_save_state`\n- `_derive_fidelity_status`\n- `write_state`\n- `read_state`\n- `derive_pipeline_status`\n\n#### Degraded report\n- `generate_degraded_report`\n  - writes a fallback fidelity report with:\n    - `validation_complete: false`\n    - `fidelity_check_attempted: true`\n    - `tasklist_ready: false`\n\n#### Metadata builders\n- `build_remediate_metadata`\n- `build_certify_metadata`\n\n#### Resume handling\n- `_restore_from_state`\n- `check_remediate_resume`\n- `check_certify_resume`\n- `_check_tasklist_hash_current`\n- `_step_needs_rerun`\n- `_apply_resume`\n\n#### Validation\n- `_save_validation_status`\n- `_auto_invoke_validate`\n\n#### Spec-patch cycle\n- `_find_qualifying_deviation_files`\n- `_apply_resume_after_spec_patch`\n\n#### Public entrypoint\n- `execute_roadmap(...)`\n\n---\n\n### Important structural observation\n`execute_roadmap()` is written as if roadmap has broader lifecycle support including:\n- spec fidelity\n- remediation\n- certification\n- validation resume state\n\nBut `_build_steps()` currently builds only through `spec-fidelity`.\n\n## 3. `gates.py` full contents/structure\n\nFile:\n- `/config/workspace/IronClaude/src/superclaude/cli/roadmap/gates.py`\n\n### Purpose\nDefines pure-data gate criteria and pure semantic check functions for roadmap outputs.\n\nImports:\n- `GateCriteria`\n- `SemanticCheck`\nfrom:\n- `/config/workspace/IronClaude/src/superclaude/cli/pipeline/models.py`\n\n### Structure\nThe file has:\n1. pure semantic helper/check functions\n2. gate constants\n3. `ALL_GATES`\n\n### Key helper functions\nNot exhaustive, but the major ones are:\n\n- `_no_heading_gaps`\n- `_cross_refs_resolve`\n- `_no_duplicate_headings`\n- `_frontmatter_values_non_empty`\n- `_has_actionable_content`\n- `_strip_yaml_quotes`\n- `_parse_frontmatter`\n- `_high_severity_count_zero`\n- `_has_per_finding_table`\n- `_all_actionable_have_status`\n- `_tasklist_ready_consistent`\n- `_convergence_score_valid`\n\nDeviation-analysis-specific:\n- `_no_ambiguous_deviations`\n- `_certified_is_true`\n- `_validation_complete_true`\n- `_routing_ids_valid`\n- `_slip_count_matches_routing`\n- `_routing_consistent_with_slip_count`\n- `_pre_approved_not_in_fix_roadmap`\n- `_total_analyzed_consistent`\n- `_total_annotated_consistent`\n\nExtraction/test-strategy semantic helpers:\n- `_complexity_class_valid`\n- `_extraction_mode_valid`\n- `_interleave_ratio_consistent`\n- `_milestone_counts_positive`\n- `_validation_philosophy_correct`\n- `_major_issue_policy_correct`\n\n### Gate constants\nDefined gates:\n\n- `EXTRACT_GATE`\n- `GENERATE_A_GATE`\n- `GENERATE_B_GATE`\n- `DIFF_GATE`\n- `DEBATE_GATE`\n- `SCORE_GATE`\n- `MERGE_GATE`\n- `TEST_STRATEGY_GATE`\n- `SPEC_FIDELITY_GATE`\n- `REMEDIATE_GATE`\n- `CERTIFY_GATE`\n- `DEVIATION_ANALYSIS_GATE`\n\n### `ALL_GATES`\nCurrent value:\n```python\nALL_GATES = [\n    (\"extract\", EXTRACT_GATE),\n    (\"generate-A\", GENERATE_A_GATE),\n    (\"generate-B\", GENERATE_B_GATE),\n    (\"diff\", DIFF_GATE),\n    (\"debate\", DEBATE_GATE),\n    (\"score\", SCORE_GATE),\n    (\"merge\", MERGE_GATE),\n    (\"test-strategy\", TEST_STRATEGY_GATE),\n    (\"spec-fidelity\", SPEC_FIDELITY_GATE),\n    (\"deviation-analysis\", DEVIATION_ANALYSIS_GATE),\n    (\"remediate\", REMEDIATE_GATE),\n    (\"certify\", CERTIFY_GATE),\n]\n```\n\n### Gate behavior summary\n- `EXTRACT_GATE`: STRICT, 13 required frontmatter fields, min_lines=50\n- `GENERATE_*`: STRICT, min_lines=100, requires actionable content\n- `DIFF_GATE`: STANDARD\n- `DEBATE_GATE`: STRICT with convergence score validation\n- `SCORE_GATE`: STANDARD\n- `MERGE_GATE`: STRICT with heading/cross-ref/dup-heading checks\n- `TEST_STRATEGY_GATE`: STRICT with complexity/interleave/milestone/philosophy/policy checks\n- `SPEC_FIDELITY_GATE`: STRICT requiring `high_severity_count == 0` and `tasklist_ready` consistency\n- `REMEDIATE_GATE`: STRICT ensuring all actionable findings terminalized\n- `CERTIFY_GATE`: STRICT requiring non-empty frontmatter, per-finding table, `certified == true`\n- `DEVIATION_ANALYSIS_GATE`: STRICT with routing/count consistency checks\n\n## 4. `wiring_gate.py` full contents/structure\n\nFile:\n- `/config/workspace/IronClaude/src/superclaude/cli/audit/wiring_gate.py`\n\n### Purpose\nImplements static wiring audit:\n- data models\n- analyzers\n- report emitter\n- report frontmatter parser\n- semantic checks\n- `WIRING_GATE`\n\n### Data models\n\n#### `WiringFinding`\nFields:\n- `finding_type: Literal[\"unwired_callable\", \"orphan_module\", \"unwired_registry\"]`\n- `file_path: str`\n- `symbol_name: str`\n- `line_number: int`\n- `detail: str`\n- `severity: Literal[\"critical\", \"major\", \"info\"] = \"critical\"`\n- `suppressed: bool = False`\n\n#### `WiringReport`\nFields:\n- `target_dir`\n- `files_analyzed`\n- `files_skipped`\n- `unwired_callables`\n- `orphan_modules`\n- `unwired_registries`\n- `scan_duration_seconds`\n- `rollout_mode`\n\nComputed properties/methods:\n- `all_findings`\n- `total_findings`\n- `unsuppressed_findings`\n- `clean`\n- `blocking_count(mode: str | None = None) -> int`\n\nBlocking semantics:\n- shadow: 0\n- soft: unsuppressed critical only\n- full: unsuppressed critical + major\n\n---\n\n### Analyzer helpers\n- `_collect_python_files`\n- `_safe_parse`\n- `_apply_whitelist`\n\n#### G-001 unwired callables\nHelpers:\n- `_extract_injectable_params`\n- `_is_optional_callable`\n- `_has_none_default`\n- `_find_wiring_call_sites`\n- `_is_class_call`\n\nMain function:\n```python\ndef analyze_unwired_callables(\n    config: WiringConfig,\n    source_dir: Path,\n) -> list[WiringFinding]:\n```\n\nWhat it detects:\n- `Optional[Callable]` / `Callable | None` constructor parameters with default `None`\n- If no keyword-wired call sites are found, emits critical `unwired_callable` finding\n\n#### G-002 orphan modules\nHelpers:\n- `_find_provider_dirs`\n- `_extract_imports`\n\nMain function:\n```python\ndef analyze_orphan_modules(\n    config: WiringConfig,\n    source_dir: Path,\n) -> list[WiringFinding]:\n```\n\nWhat it detects:\n- modules under provider directories (`steps`, `handlers`, etc.) with zero inbound imports from outside provider dirs\n- emits major `orphan_module` findings\n\n#### G-003 unwired registries\nHelpers:\n- `_matches_registry_pattern`\n- `_resolve_name_in_scope`\n\nMain function:\n```python\ndef analyze_registries(\n    config: WiringConfig,\n    source_dir: Path,\n) -> list[WiringFinding]:\n```\n\nWhat it detects:\n- dict assignments whose variable names match registry patterns\n- unresolved callable references in registry entries\n- emits critical `unwired_registry` findings\n\n---\n\n### `run_wiring_analysis`\n\nSignature:\n```python\ndef run_wiring_analysis(\n    config: WiringConfig,\n    source_dir: Path,\n) -> WiringReport:\n```\n\nBehavior:\n- counts skipped and included Python files\n- runs:\n  - `analyze_unwired_callables`\n  - `analyze_orphan_modules`\n  - `analyze_registries`\n- returns `WiringReport`\n\n---\n\n### `emit_report`\n\nSignature:\n```python\ndef emit_report(report: WiringReport, output_path: Path) -> Path:\n```\n\nBehavior:\n- computes unsuppressed severity counts\n- computes `blocking_findings = report.blocking_count()`\n- writes YAML frontmatter with 16 fields:\n  - `gate`\n  - `target_dir`\n  - `files_analyzed`\n  - `files_skipped`\n  - `rollout_mode`\n  - `analysis_complete`\n  - `audit_artifacts_used`\n  - `unwired_callable_count`\n  - `orphan_module_count`\n  - `unwired_registry_count`\n  - `critical_count`\n  - `major_count`\n  - `info_count`\n  - `total_findings`\n  - `blocking_findings`\n  - `whitelist_entries_applied`\n- body has 7 sections:\n  1. Summary\n  2. Unwired Optional Callable Injections\n  3. Orphan Modules / Symbols\n  4. Unregistered Dispatch Entries\n  5. Suppressions and Dynamic Retention\n  6. Recommended Remediation\n  7. Evidence and Limitations\n\nWrites file and returns `output_path`.\n\n---\n\n### Frontmatter parsing\n- `_FRONTMATTER_RE`\n- `_extract_frontmatter_values(content: str) -> dict[str, str]`\n\nThis is intentionally duplicated from pipeline logic to preserve layering.\n\n---\n\n### Semantic checks\n- `_analysis_complete_true`\n- `_recognized_rollout_mode`\n- `_finding_counts_consistent`\n- `_severity_summary_consistent`\n- `_zero_blocking_findings_for_mode`\n\n---\n\n### `WIRING_GATE`\nDefined as `GateCriteria(...)` with:\n- 16 required frontmatter fields\n- `min_lines=10`\n- `enforcement_tier=\"STRICT\"`\n- 5 semantic checks listed above\n\nRequired fields are:\n- `gate`\n- `target_dir`\n- `files_analyzed`\n- `rollout_mode`\n- `analysis_complete`\n- `unwired_callable_count`\n- `orphan_module_count`\n- `unwired_registry_count`\n- `critical_count`\n- `major_count`\n- `info_count`\n- `total_findings`\n- `blocking_findings`\n- `whitelist_entries_applied`\n- `files_skipped`\n- `audit_artifacts_used`\n\n---\n\n### `blocking_for_mode`\nSignature:\n```python\ndef blocking_for_mode(report: WiringReport) -> bool:\n```\nReturns:\n```python\nreturn report.blocking_count() > 0\n```\n\n## 5. `wiring_analyzer.py` existence and exports\n\nFile exists:\n- `/config/workspace/IronClaude/src/superclaude/cli/audit/wiring_analyzer.py`\n\n### Purpose\nAST-based file analyzer for ToolOrchestrator integration.\n\n### Top-level export\n- `ast_analyze_file(file_path: str, content: str) -> FileAnalysis`\n\n### Other internal helpers\n- `_extract_imports`\n- `_extract_exports`\n- `_extract_references`\n- `_call_target_name`\n- `_attribute_chain`\n\n### Imported types/functions\nFrom `superclaude.cli.audit.tool_orchestrator`:\n- `FileAnalysis`\n- `compute_content_hash`\n\n### Behavior summary\n- Parses Python source with `ast.parse`\n- Returns `FileAnalysis`\n- Populates:\n  - imports\n  - exports\n  - references\n  - metadata\n- On `SyntaxError`, returns empty analysis with parse error in metadata instead of raising\n\n### Export shape\nThere is no explicit `__all__`; practically exported top-level names include:\n- `ast_analyze_file`\n\n## 6. `wiring_config.py` existence and exports\n\nFile exists:\n- `/config/workspace/IronClaude/src/superclaude/cli/audit/wiring_config.py`\n\n### Top-level exports\n- `DEFAULT_REGISTRY_PATTERNS`\n- `WiringConfigError`\n- `WhitelistEntry`\n- `WiringConfig`\n- `load_whitelist(path: Path | None, rollout_mode: Literal[\"shadow\", \"soft\", \"full\"] = \"shadow\") -> list[WhitelistEntry]`\n\n### `WiringConfig`\nDataclass fields:\n- `provider_dir_names: frozenset[str] = {\"steps\", \"handlers\", \"validators\", \"checks\"}`\n- `exclude_patterns: list[str] = [\"test_*.py\", \"conftest.py\", \"__init__.py\"]`\n- `registry_patterns: tuple[re.Pattern[str], ...] = DEFAULT_REGISTRY_PATTERNS`\n- `whitelist_path: Path | None = None`\n- `rollout_mode: Literal[\"shadow\", \"soft\", \"full\"] = \"shadow\"`\n\n### `WhitelistEntry`\nDataclass fields:\n- `symbol: str`\n- `reason: str`\n- `finding_type: str = \"unwired_callable\"`\n\n### `load_whitelist`\nLoads YAML whitelist sections:\n- `unwired_callables`\n- `orphan_modules`\n- `unwired_registries`\n\nMaps them to finding types:\n- `unwired_callable`\n- `orphan_module`\n- `unwired_registry`\n\nBehavior:\n- shadow mode: warns and skips malformed content\n- soft/full: raises `WiringConfigError`\n\n## 7. `tests/audit/` existing test files\n\nDirectory exists. It imports:\n- `Path`\n- `Literal`\n- `AgentSpec` from `.models`\n\n### Module-level constants\n1. `_DEPTH_INSTRUCTIONS`\n   - Dict keyed by `\"quick\" | \"standard\" | \"deep\"`\n   - Used by `build_debate_prompt`\n   - Encodes debate-round instructions:\n     - quick: 1 round\n     - standard: 2 rounds\n     - deep: 3 rounds with final synthesis\n\n2. `_OUTPUT_FORMAT_BLOCK`\n   - Shared suffix appended to all prompts\n   - Enforces YAML frontmatter at the very start\n   - Explicitly forbids conversational preamble like “Here is...”\n\n### Prompt builder functions in `prompts.py`\nThese are the existing prompt builders:\n\n1. `build_extract_prompt(spec_file: Path, retrospective_content: str | None = None) -> str`\n   - Produces extraction prompt.\n   - Requires YAML frontmatter fields:\n     - `spec_source`\n     - `generated`\n     - `generator`\n     - `functional_requirements`\n     - `nonfunctional_requirements`\n     - `total_requirements`\n     - `complexity_score`\n     - `complexity_class`\n     - `domains_detected`\n     - `risks_identified`\n     - `dependencies_identified`\n     - `success_criteria_count`\n     - `extraction_mode`\n   - Requires 8 body sections:\n     - Functional Requirements\n     - Non-Functional Requirements\n     - Complexity Assessment\n     - Architectural Constraints\n     - Risk Inventory\n     - Dependency Inventory\n     - Success Criteria\n     - Open Questions\n   - If `retrospective_content` is provided, adds an advisory section saying it is not a requirement source.\n\n2. `build_generate_prompt(agent: AgentSpec, extraction_path: Path) -> str`\n   - Generates persona-specific roadmap prompt.\n   - Uses `agent.persona`.\n   - Requires frontmatter:\n     - `spec_source`\n     - `complexity_score`\n     - `primary_persona`\n   - Requires body:\n     - Executive summary\n     - Phased implementation plan\n     - Risk assessment\n     - Resource requirements\n     - Success criteria\n     - Timeline estimates\n\n3. `build_diff_prompt(variant_a_path: Path, variant_b_path: Path) -> str`\n   - Compares two roadmap variants.\n   - Frontmatter:\n     - `total_diff_points`\n     - `shared_assumptions_count`\n   - Body:\n     - shared assumptions\n     - divergence points\n     - stronger areas\n     - debate-needed areas\n\n4. `build_debate_prompt(diff_path: Path, variant_a_path: Path, variant_b_path: Path, depth: Literal[\"quick\", \"standard\", \"deep\"]) -> str`\n   - Structured adversarial debate prompt.\n   - Uses `_DEPTH_INSTRUCTIONS[depth]`\n   - Frontmatter:\n     - `convergence_score`\n     - `rounds_completed`\n\n5. `build_score_prompt(debate_path: Path, variant_a_path: Path, variant_b_path: Path) -> str`\n   - Selects base variant and scores both.\n   - Frontmatter:\n     - `base_variant`\n     - `variant_scores`\n\n6. `build_merge_prompt(base_selection_path: Path, variant_a_path: Path, variant_b_path: Path, debate_path: Path) -> str`\n   - Produces merged roadmap.\n   - Frontmatter:\n     - `spec_source`\n     - `complexity_score`\n     - `adversarial: true`\n   - Also instructs:\n     - no heading gaps\n     - resolved internal cross-references\n     - no duplicate H2/H3 heading text\n\n7. `build_spec_fidelity_prompt(spec_file: Path, roadmap_path: Path) -> str`\n   - Compares spec vs roadmap.\n   - Embeds explicit HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW severity definitions.\n   - Explicit comparison dimensions:\n     - signatures\n     - data models\n     - gates\n     - CLI options\n     - NFRs\n   - Frontmatter:\n     - `high_severity_count`\n     - `medium_severity_count`\n     - `low_severity_count`\n     - `total_deviations`\n     - `validation_complete`\n     - `tasklist_ready`\n   - Body requires numbered deviation entries with:\n     - ID\n     - severity\n     - deviation\n     - spec quote\n     - roadmap quote\n     - impact\n     - recommended correction\n\n8. `build_test_strategy_prompt(roadmap_path: Path, extraction_path: Path) -> str`\n   - Produces roadmap test strategy.\n   - Frontmatter:\n     - `complexity_class`\n     - `validation_philosophy`\n     - `validation_milestones`\n     - `work_milestones`\n     - `interleave_ratio`\n     - `major_issue_policy`\n   - Hardcodes semantic mapping:\n     - LOW → `1:3`\n     - MEDIUM → `1:2`\n     - HIGH → `1:1`\n   - Requires issue classification table and milestone/test plan sections.\n\n### Notes\n- `spec_file`, `extraction_path`, etc. are accepted as parameters for semantic clarity, but these prompt builders mostly do not interpolate the actual path values into the text.\n- All prompt builders append `_OUTPUT_FORMAT_BLOCK`.\n\n## 2. `executor.py` full contents/structure\n\nFile:\n- `/config/workspace/IronClaude/src/superclaude/cli/roadmap/executor.py`\n\n### Purpose\nRoadmap pipeline executor. Builds steps, runs Claude subprocesses, handles resume/dry-run/state/validation/spec-patch cycle.\n\n### Imports\nKey imports:\n- stdlib: `hashlib`, `json`, `logging`, `sys`, `time`, `datetime`, `Path`, `Callable`\n- pipeline:\n  - `decompose_deliverables`\n  - `execute_pipeline`\n  - `Deliverable`, `PipelineConfig`, `Step`, `StepResult`, `StepStatus`\n  - `ClaudeProcess`\n- roadmap:\n  - gates constants\n  - `AgentSpec`, `RoadmapConfig`\n  - prompt builders\n  - `build_certification_prompt`\n\n### Module constants\n- `_MAX_ARG_STRLEN = 128 * 1024`\n- `_PROMPT_TEMPLATE_OVERHEAD = 8 * 1024`\n- `_EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT = _MAX_ARG_STRLEN - _PROMPT_TEMPLATE_OVERHEAD`\n\nThese support inline prompt embedding instead of `--file`.\n\n---\n\n### Helper functions before the main execution path\n\n#### `_embed_inputs(input_paths: list[Path]) -> str`\n- Reads each input file and appends content as fenced code blocks:\n  - header format: `# <path>`\n  - content fenced with triple backticks\n- Returns concatenated string or empty string.\n\n#### `_sanitize_output(output_file: Path) -> int`\n- Cleans output before gate validation.\n- Strips:\n  - leading whitespace\n  - conversational preamble before first `---`\n- Writes sanitized content back atomically via `.tmp` + `os.replace`.\n- Returns byte count removed.\n\n#### `_inject_pipeline_diagnostics(output_file: Path, started_at: datetime, finished_at: datetime) -> None`\n- Injects `pipeline_diagnostics` into extraction frontmatter if missing:\n  - `elapsed_seconds`\n  - `started_at`\n  - `finished_at`\n\n#### `_inject_provenance_fields(output_file: Path, spec_source: str) -> None`\n- Injects missing frontmatter fields into test-strategy output:\n  - `spec_source`\n  - `generated`\n  - `generator`\n\n---\n\n### `roadmap_run_step`\n\nSignature:\n```python\ndef roadmap_run_step(\n    step: Step,\n    config: PipelineConfig,\n    cancel_check: Callable[[], bool],\n) -> StepResult:\n```\n\nBehavior:\n- Records `started_at`\n- If `config.dry_run`, returns PASS immediately\n- Embeds `step.inputs` inline into prompt using `_embed_inputs`\n- Warns if composed prompt exceeds embed limit, but still embeds inline\n- Creates `ClaudeProcess` with:\n  - `prompt`\n  - `output_file`\n  - `.err` file\n  - `max_turns`\n  - `model`\n  - `permission_flag`\n  - `timeout_seconds`\n  - `output_format=\"text\"`\n- Starts process\n- Polls once per second for cancellation\n- If cancelled:\n  - terminates process\n  - returns `StepStatus.CANCELLED`\n- On exit:\n  - `124` => `TIMEOUT`\n  - nonzero => `FAIL`\n  - zero => sanitize output, inject metadata where needed, return `PASS`\n\nImportant post-processing:\n- for `extract`: `_inject_pipeline_diagnostics`\n- for `test-strategy`: `_inject_provenance_fields`\n\n---\n\n### `build_certify_step`\nBuilds a `Step` for certification:\n- id: `\"certify\"`\n- output: `certification-report.md`\n- gate: `CERTIFY_GATE`\n- input: `remediation-tasklist.md`\n\n---\n\n### `_build_steps`\n\nSignature:\n```python\ndef _build_steps(config: RoadmapConfig) -> list[Step | list[Step]]:\n```\n\nThis is the key roadmap pipeline builder.\n\n#### Current pipeline shape\nIt currently builds **8 executable pipeline entries**, where one entry is a parallel group:\n\n1. `extract`\n2. parallel group:\n   - `generate-{agent_a.id}`\n   - `generate-{agent_b.id}`\n3. `diff`\n4. `debate`\n5. `score`\n6. `merge`\n7. `test-strategy`\n8. `spec-fidelity`\n\n#### Notably absent from `_build_steps`\nAlthough `_get_all_step_ids` includes them, `_build_steps` does **not** create:\n- `remediate`\n- `certify`\n\n#### Per-step outputs\n- extraction: `extraction.md`\n- generate A: `roadmap-{agent_a.id}.md`\n- generate B: `roadmap-{agent_b.id}.md`\n- diff: `diff-analysis.md`\n- debate: `debate-transcript.md`\n- score: `base-selection.md`\n- merge: `roadmap.md`\n- test-strategy: `test-strategy.md`\n- spec-fidelity: `spec-fidelity.md`\n\n#### Inputs by step\n- extract: `[config.spec_file]`\n- generate-* : `[extraction]`\n- diff: `[roadmap_a, roadmap_b]`\n- debate: `[diff_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b]`\n- score: `[debate_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b]`\n- merge: `[score_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b, debate_file]`\n- test-strategy: `[merge_file, extraction]`\n- spec-fidelity: `[config.spec_file, merge_file]`\n\n#### Other behavior\n- Reads optional retrospective file into `retrospective_content`\n- Uses `build_extract_prompt(... retrospective_content=...)`\n- Uses per-agent models on generate steps\n\n---\n\n### `_get_all_step_ids`\n\nSignature:\n```python\ndef _get_all_step_ids(config: RoadmapConfig) -> list[str]:\n```\n\nReturns:\n```python\n[\n    \"extract\",\n    f\"generate-{agent_a.id}\",\n    f\"generate-{agent_b.id}\",\n    \"diff\",\n    \"debate\",\n    \"score\",\n    \"merge\",\n    \"test-strategy\",\n    \"spec-fidelity\",\n    \"remediate\",\n    \"certify\",\n]\n```\n\nImportant mismatch:\n- `_get_all_step_ids` includes `remediate` and `certify`\n- `_build_steps` does not build them\n\nThis likely matters for resume/halt reporting.\n\n---\n\n### Other notable executor functions\n\n#### Halt/progress/state helpers\n- `_format_halt_output`\n- `_print_terminal_halt`\n- `_print_step_start`\n- `_print_step_complete`\n- `execute_roadmap(...)`\n\n---\n\n### Important structural observation\n`execute_roadmap()` is written as if roadmap has broader lifecycle support including:\n- spec fidelity\n- remediation\n- certification\n- validation resume state\n\nBut `_build_steps()` currently builds only through `spec-fidelity`.\n\n## 3. `gates.py` full contents/structure\n\nFile:\n- contents/structure\n\nFile:\n- `/config/workspace/IronClaude/src/superclaude/cli/audit/wiring_gate.py`\n\n### Purpose\nImplements static wiring audit:\n- data models\n- analyzers\n- report emitter\n- report frontmatter parser\n- semantic checks\n- `WIRING_GATE`\n\n### Data models\n\n#### `WiringFinding`\nFields:\n- `finding_type: Literal[\"unwired_callable\", \"orphan_module\", \"unwired_registry\"]`\n- `file_path: str`\n- `symbol_name: str`\n- `line_number: int`\n- `detail: str`\n- `severity: Literal[\"critical\", \"major\", \"info\"] = \"critical\"`\n- `suppressed: bool = False`\n\n#### `WiringReport`\nFields:\n- `target_dir`\n- `files_analyzed`\n- `files_skipped`\n- `unwired_callables`\n- `orphan_modules`\n- `unwired_registries`\n- `scan_duration_seconds`\n- `rollout_mode`\n\nComputed properties/methods:\n- `all_findings`\n- `total_findings`\n- `unsuppressed_findings`\n- `clean`\n- `blocking_count(mode: str | None = None) -> int`\n\nBlocking semantics:\n- shadow: 0\n- soft: unsuppressed critical only\n- full: unsuppressed critical + major\n\n---\n\n### Analyzer helpers\n- `_collect_python_files`\n- `_safe_parse`\n- `_apply_whitelist`\n\n#### G-001 unwired callables\nHelpers:\n- `_extract_injectable_params`\n- `_is_optional_callable`\n- `_has_none_default`\n- `_find_wiring_call_sites`\n- `_is_class_call`\n\nMain function:\n```python\ndef analyze_unwired_callables(\n    config: WiringConfig,\n    source_dir: Path,\n) -> list[WiringFinding]:\n```\n\nWhat it detects:\n- `Optional[Callable]` / `Callable | None` constructor parameters with default `None`\n- If no keyword-wired call sites are found, emits critical `unwired_callable` finding\n\n#### G-002 orphan modules\nHelpers:\n- `_find_provider_dirs`\n- `_extract_imports`\n\nMain function:\n```python\ndef analyze_orphan_modules(\n    config: WiringConfig,\n    source_dir: Path,\n) -> list[WiringFinding]:\n```\n\nWhat it detects:\n- modules under provider directories (`steps`, `handlers`, etc.) with zero inbound imports from outside provider dirs\n- emits major `orphan_module` findings\n\n#### G-003 unwired registries\nHelpers:\n- `_matches_registry_pattern`\n- `_resolve_name_in_scope`\n\nMain function:\n```python\ndef analyze_registries(\n    config: WiringConfig,\n    source_dir: Path,\n) -> list[WiringFinding]:\n```\n\nWhat it detects:\n- dict assignments whose variable names match registry patterns\n- unresolved callable references in registry entries\n- emits critical `unwired_registry` findings\n\n---\n\n### `run_wiring_analysis`\n\nSignature:\n```python\ndef run_wiring_analysis(\n    config: WiringConfig,\n    source_dir: Path,\n) -> WiringReport:\n```\n\nBehavior:\n- counts skipped and included Python files\n- runs:\n  - `analyze_unwired_callables`\n  - `analyze_orphan_modules`\n  - `analyze_registries`\n- returns `WiringReport`\n\n---\n\n### `emit_report`\n\nSignature:\n```python\ndef emit_report(report: WiringReport, output_path: Path) -> Path:\n```\n\nBehavior:\n- computes unsuppressed severity counts\n- computes `blocking_findings = report.blocking_count()`\n- writes YAML frontmatter with 16 fields:\n  - `gate`\n  - `target_dir`\n  - `files_analyzed`\n  - `files_skipped`\n  - `rollout_mode`\n  - `analysis_complete`\n  - `audit_artifacts_used`\n  - `unwired_callable_count`\n  - `orphan_module_count`\n  - `unwired_registry_count`\n  - `critical_count`\n  - `major_count`\n  - `info_count`\n  - `total_findings`\n  - `blocking_findings`\n  - `whitelist_entries_applied`\n- body has 7 sections:\n  1. Summary\n  2. Unwired Optional Callable Injections\n  3. Orphan Modules / Symbols\n  4. Unregistered Dispatch Entries\n  5. Suppressions and Dynamic Retention\n  6. Recommended Remediation\n  7. Evidence and Limitations\n\nWrites file and returns `output_path`.\n\n---\n\n### Frontmatter parsing\n- `_FRONTMATTER_RE`\n- `_extract_frontmatter_values(content: str) -> dict[str, str]`\n\nThis is intentionally duplicated from pipeline logic to preserve layering.\n\n---\n\n### Semantic checks\n- `_analysis_complete_true`\n- `_recognized_rollout_mode`\n- `_finding_counts_consistent`\n- `_severity_summary_consistent`\n- `_zero_blocking_findings_for_mode`\n\n---\n\n### `WIRING_GATE`\nDefined as `GateCriteria(...)` with:\n- 16 required frontmatter fields\n- `min_lines=10`\n- `enforcement_tier=\"STRICT\"`\n- 5 semantic checks listed above\n\nRequired fields are:\n- `gate`\n- `target_dir`\n- `files_analyzed`\n- `rollout_mode`\n- `analysis_complete`\n- `unwired_callable_count`\n- `orphan_module_count`\n- `unwired_registry_count`\n- `critical_count`\n- `major_count`\n- `info_count`\n- `total_findings`\n- `blocking_findings`\n- `whitelist_entries_applied`\n- `files_skipped`\n- `audit_artifacts_used`\n\n---\n\n### `blocking_for_mode`\nSignature:\n```python\ndef blocking_for_mode(report: WiringReport) -> bool:\n```\nReturns:\n```python\nreturn report.blocking_count() > 0\n```\n\n## 5. `wiring_analyzer.py` existence and exports\n\nFile exists:\n- `/config/workspace/IronClaude/src/superclaude/cli/audit/wiring_analyzer.py`\n\n### Purpose\nAST-based file analyzer for ToolOrchestrator integration.\n\n### Top-level export\n- `ast_analyze_file(file_path: str, content: str) -> FileAnalysis`\n\n### Other internal helpers\n- `_extract_imports`\n- `_extract_exports`\n- `_extract_references`\n- `_call_target_name`\n- `_attribute_chain`\n\n### Imported types/functions\nFrom `superclaude.cli.audit.tool_orchestrator`:\n- `FileAnalysis`\n- `compute_content_hash`\n\n### Behavior summary\n- Parses Python source with `ast.parse`\n- Returns `FileAnalysis`\n- Populates:\n  - assessment.\"\n    ),\n    \"deep\": (\n        \"Conduct three debate rounds:\\n\"\n        \"  Round 1: Each perspective states initial positions on divergence points.\\n\"\n        \"  Round 2: provide a convergence assessment.\"\n    ),\n}\n\n_OUTPUT_FORMAT_BLOCK = (\n    \"\\n\\n<output_format>\\n\"\n    \"CRITICAL: Your response MUST begin with YAML frontmatter (--- delimited block).\\n\"\n    \"Do NOT include any text, preamble, or commentary before the opening ---.\\n\"\n    'Do NOT say \"Here is...\", \"Sure!\", or any conversational text before the frontmatter.\\n'\n    \"\\n\"\n    \"Correct start:\\n\"\n    \"---\\n\"\n    \"key: value\\n\"\n    \"---\\n\"\n    \"\\n\"\n    \"Incorrect start:\\n\"\n    \"Here is the output:\\n\"\n    \"---\\n\"\n    \"key: value\\n\"\n    \"---\\n\"\n    \"</output_format>\"\n)\n\n\ndef build_extract_prompt(\n    spec_file: Path,\n    retrospective_content: str | None = None,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Prompt for step 'extract'.\n\n    Instructs Claude to read the provided specification and produce\n    extraction.md with YAML frontmatter containing all 13 required\n    protocol fields plus 8 structured body sections.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    spec_file:\n        Path to the specification file being extracted.\n    retrospective_content:\n        Optional retrospective text from a prior release cycle.\n        When provided, it is framed as advisory \"areas to watch\"\n        context -- NOT as hard requirements (RSK-004 mitigation).\n    \"\"\"\n    base = (\n        \"You are a requirements extraction specialist.\\n\\n\"\n        \"Read the provided specification file and produce a requirements extraction document.\\n\\n\"\n        \"Your output MUST begin with YAML frontmatter delimited by --- lines containing:\\n\"\n        \"- spec_source: (string) the source specification filename\\n\"\n        \"- generated: (string) ISO-8601 timestamp of extraction\\n\"\n        \"- generator: (string) identifier of the extraction agent\\n\"\n        \"- functional_requirements: (integer) count of identified functional requirements\\n\"\n        \"- nonfunctional_requirements: (integer) count of identified non-functional requirements\\n\"\n        \"- total_requirements: (integer) sum of functional + non-functional requirements\\n\"\n        \"- complexity_score: (float 0.0-1.0) assessing overall complexity\\n\"\n        \"- complexity_class: (string) one of: LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH\\n\"\n        \"- domains_detected: (list) array of domain name strings, e.g. [backend, security, frontend]\\n\"\n        \"- risks_identified: (integer) count of risks found in the specification\\n\"\n        \"- dependencies_identified: (integer) count of external dependencies\\n\"\n        \"- success_criteria_count: (integer) count of measurable success criteria\\n\"\n        \"- extraction_mode: (string) one of: standard, chunked\\n\\n\"\n        \"After the frontmatter, provide the following 8 structured sections:\\n\\n\"\n        \"## Functional Requirements\\n\"\n        \"Numbered list with FR-NNN IDs (e.g. FR-001, FR-002). \"\n        \"Extract every functional requirement, even implicit ones.\\n\\n\"\n        \"## Non-Functional Requirements\\n\"\n        \"Numbered list with NFR-NNN IDs (e.g. NFR-001, NFR-002). \"\n        \"Include performance, security, scalability, maintainability.\\n\\n\"\n        \"## Complexity Assessment\\n\"\n        \"Provide complexity_score and complexity_class with detailed scoring rationale.\\n\\n\"\n        \"## Architectural Constraints\\n\"\n        \"List all architectural constraints, technology mandates, and integration boundaries.\\n\\n\"\n        \"## Risk Inventory\\n\"\n        \"Numbered list of identified risks with severity (high/medium/low) and mitigation.\\n\\n\"\n        \"## Dependency Inventory\\n\"\n        \"List all external dependencies, libraries, services, and integration points.\\n\\n\"\n        \"## Success Criteria\\n\"\n        \"Measurable success criteria with acceptance thresholds.\\n\\n\"\n        \"## Open Questions\\n\"\n        \"Ambiguities, gaps, or items requiring stakeholder clarification.\\n\\n\"\n        \"Be thorough and precise. Extract every requirement, even implicit ones.\"\n    )\n\n    if retrospective_content:\n        advisory = (\n            \"\\n\\n## Advisory: Areas to Watch (from prior retrospective)\\n\\n\"\n            \"The following retrospective content is provided as advisory context \"\n            \"only. These are areas to watch during extraction -- they are NOT \"\n            \"additional requirements and MUST NOT be treated as such. Use them \"\n            \"to inform your risk assessment and open questions sections.\\n\\n\"\n            f\"{retrospective_content}\"\n        )\n        base += advisory\n\n    return base + _OUTPUT_FORMAT_BLOCK\n\n\ndef build_generate_prompt(agent: AgentSpec, extraction_path: Path) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Prompt for step 'generate-{agent.id}'.\n\n    Instructs Claude to read the extraction document and generate a\n    complete project roadmap with the agent's persona as a role instruction.\n    References expanded extraction fields for richer context.\n    \"\"\"\n    return (\n        f\"You are a {agent.persona} specialist creating a project roadmap.\\n\\n\"\n        \"Read the provided requirements extraction document and generate a comprehensive \"\n        \"project roadmap.\\n\\n\"\n        \"The extraction document contains YAML frontmatter with these fields you should \"\n        \"reference for context:\\n\"\n        \"- spec_source, generated, generator: provenance metadata\\n\"\n        \"- functional_requirements, nonfunctional_requirements, total_requirements: scope counts\\n\"\n        \"- complexity_score, complexity_class: complexity assessment\\n\"\n        \"- domains_detected: list of technical domain names to address\\n\"\n        \"- risks_identified: number of risks to mitigate in the roadmap\\n\"\n        \"- dependencies_identified: external dependencies to plan around\\n\"\n        \"- success_criteria_count: measurable criteria to validate against\\n\"\n        \"- extraction_mode: extraction completeness indicator\\n\\n\"\n        \"Your output MUST begin with YAML frontmatter delimited by --- lines containing:\\n\"\n        f\"- spec_source: (the source specification filename)\\n\"\n        \"- complexity_score: (float 0.0-1.0 from the extraction)\\n\"\n        f\"- primary_persona: {agent.persona}\\n\\n\"\n        \"After the frontmatter, provide a complete roadmap including:\\n\"\n        \"1. Executive summary\\n\"\n        \"2. Phased implementation plan with milestones\\n\"\n        \"3. Risk assessment and mitigation strategies\\n\"\n        \"4. Resource requirements and dependencies\\n\"\n        \"5. Success criteria and validation approach\\n\"\n        \"6. Timeline estimates per phase\\n\\n\"\n        f\"Apply your {agent.persona} perspective throughout: prioritize concerns, \"\n        f\"risks, and recommendations that a {agent.persona} would emphasize.\\n\\n\"\n        \"Use numbered and bulleted lists for actionable items. Be specific and concrete.\"\n    ) + _OUTPUT_FORMAT_BLOCK\n\n\ndef build_diff_prompt(variant_a_path: Path, variant_b_path: Path) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Prompt for step 'diff'.\n\n    Instructs Claude to compare two roadmap variants and produce\n    diff-analysis.md with frontmatter fields total_diff_points,\n    shared_assumptions_count.\n    \"\"\"\n    return (\n        \"You are a comparative analysis specialist.\\n\\n\"\n        \"Read the two provided roadmap variants and produce a structured diff analysis.\\n\\n\"\n        \"Your output MUST begin with YAML frontmatter delimited by --- lines containing:\\n\"\n        \"- total_diff_points: (integer count of identified divergence points)\\n\"\n        \"- shared_assumptions_count: (integer count of shared assumptions)\\n\\n\"\n        \"After the frontmatter, provide:\\n\"\n        \"1. Shared assumptions and agreements between variants\\n\"\n        \"2. Areas where one variant is clearly stronger\\n\"\n        \"4. Areas requiring debate to resolve\\n\\n\"\n        \"Be objective. Present both positions fairly without bias.\"\n    ) + _OUTPUT_FORMAT_BLOCK\n\n\ndef build_debate_prompt(\n    diff_path: Path,\n    variant_a_path: Path,\n    variant_b_path: Path,\n    depth: Literal[\"quick\", \"standard\", \"deep\"],\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Prompt for step 'debate'.\n\n    Depth controls the number of debate rounds embedded in the prompt.\n    \"\"\"\n    depth_instruction = _DEPTH_INSTRUCTIONS[depth]\n    return (\n        \"You are a structured debate facilitator.\\n\\n\"\n        \"Read the provided diff analysis and both roadmap variants. \"\n        \"Facilitate a structured adversarial debate between the two approaches.\\n\\n\"\n        f\"Debate format:\\n{depth_instruction}\\n\\n\"\n        \"Your output MUST begin with YAML frontmatter delimited by --- lines containing:\\n\"\n        \"- convergence_score: (float 0.0-1.0 indicating how much agreement was reached)\\n\"\n        \"- rounds_completed: (integer number of debate rounds conducted)\\n\\n\"\n        \"After the frontmatter, provide the full debate transcript with:\\n\"\n        \"- Each round clearly labeled\\n\"\n        \"- Positions attributed to Variant A and Variant B\\n\"\n        \"- A convergence assessment summarizing areas of agreement and remaining disputes\\n\\n\"\n        \"Ensure each perspective argues its strongest case. Do not artificially force agreement.\"\n    ) + _OUTPUT_FORMAT_BLOCK\n\n\ndef build_score_prompt(\n    debate_path: Path,\n    variant_a_path: Path,\n    variant_b_path: Path,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Prompt for step 'score'.\n\n    Instructs Claude to select a base variant and score both.\n    \"\"\"\n    return (\n        \"You are an objective evaluation specialist.\\n\\n\"\n        \"Read the debate transcript and both roadmap variants. \"\n        \"Score each variant and select a base for the final merge.\\n\\n\"\n        \"Your output MUST begin with YAML frontmatter delimited by --- lines containing:\\n\"\n        \"- base_variant: (string: the identifier of the selected base variant)\\n\"\n        \"- variant_scores: (string summary of scores, e.g. 'A:78 B:72')\\n\\n\"\n        \"After the frontmatter, provide:\\n\"\n        \"1. The roadmap cannot be used as-is without \"\n        \"risking incorrect implementation. Examples:\\n\"\n        \"- A functional requirement (FR-NNN) is entirely missing from the roadmap\\n\"\n        \"- A non-functional requirement (NFR-NNN) is contradicted by the roadmap\\n\"\n        \"- A success criterion (SC-NNN) has no corresponding validation in the roadmap\\n\"\n        \"- An architectural constraint is violated by the roadmap's proposed approach\\n\\n\"\n        \"**MEDIUM**: The roadmap addresses the requirement but with insufficient \"\n        \"detail, ambiguous language, or minor misalignment that could lead to \"\n        \"implementation issues. Examples:\\n\"\n        \"- Different heading structure or section ordering\\n\"\n        \"- Terminology variations that don't change meaning\\n\"\n        \"- Missing cross-references that don't affect understanding\\n\\n\"\n        \"## Comparison Dimensions\\n\\n\"\n        \"Compare across ALL of these dimensions:\\n\"\n        \"1. **Signatures**: Function/method/API signatures specified vs. roadmapped\\n\"\n        \"2. **Data Models**: Data structures, schemas, field definitions\\n\"\n        \"3. **Gates**: Quality gates, validation checkpoints, acceptance criteria\\n\"\n        \"4. **CLI Options**: Command-line flags, arguments, configuration options\\n\"\n        \"5. **NFRs**: Performance targets, security requirements, scalability constraints\\n\\n\"\n        \"## Output Requirements\\n\\n\"\n        \"Your output MUST begin with YAML frontmatter delimited by --- lines containing:\\n\"\n        \"- high_severity_count: (integer) number of HIGH severity deviations\\n\"\n        \"- medium_severity_count: (integer) number of MEDIUM severity deviations\\n\"\n        \"- low_severity_count: (integer) number of LOW severity deviations\\n\"\n        \"- total_deviations: (integer) total number of deviations found\\n\"\n        \"- validation_complete: (boolean) true if analysis completed fully\\n\"\n        \"- tasklist_ready: (boolean) true ONLY if high_severity_count is 0 AND \"\n        \"validation_complete is true\\n\\n\"\n        \"After the frontmatter, provide:\\n\\n\"\n        \"## Deviation Report\\n\\n\"\n        \"For each deviation, provide a numbered entry with:\\n\"\n        \"- **ID**: DEV-NNN (zero-padded 3-digit)\\n\"\n        \"- **Severity**: HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW\\n\"\n        \"- **Deviation**: Concise description of what differs\\n\"\n        \"- **Spec Quote**: Verbatim quote from the specification\\n\"\n        \"- **Roadmap Quote**: Verbatim quote from the roadmap, or '[MISSING]' if absent\\n\"\n        \"- _embed_inputs(input_paths: list[Path]) -> str:\n    67→    \"\"\"Read input files and return their contents as fenced code blocks.\n    68→\n    69→    Each file is wrapped in a fenced block with a ``# <path>`` header.\n    70→    Returns an empty string when *input_paths* is empty (no-op).\n    71→    \"\"\"\n    72→    if not input_paths:\n    73→        return \"\"\n    74→\n    75→    blocks: list[str] = []\n    76→    for p in input_paths:\n    77→        content = Path(p).read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    78→        blocks.append(f\"# {p}\\n```\\n{content}\\n```\")\n    79→    return \"\\n\\n\".join(blocks)\n    80→\n    81→\n    82→def _sanitize_output(output_file: Path) -> int:\n    83→    \"\"\"Strip conversational preamble and leading blank lines before YAML\n    84→    frontmatter in a step output file.\n    85→\n    86→    Reads the file, strips leading blank lines, finds the first ``^---``\n    87→    line, and removes everything before it.  Uses atomic write (write to\n    88→    ``.tmp`` then ``os.replace()``) to prevent partial file states.\n    89→\n    90→    Returns the byte count of the stripped content (0 when file already\n    91→    starts with ``---`` and has no preamble).\n    92→    \"\"\"\n    93→    import os\n    94→    import re\n    95→\n    96→    try:\n    97→        raw = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    98→    except FileNotFoundError:\n    99→        return 0\n   100→\n   101→    # Strip leading blank lines (LLMs sometimes emit \\n\\n before ---)\n   102→    content = raw.lstrip(\"\\n\\r\\t \")\n   103→\n   104→    # Already starts with frontmatter delimiter -- no preamble to strip\n   105→    if content.startswith(\"---\"):\n   106→        if content == raw:\n   107→            return 0\n   108→        # Leading whitespace only -- write back the stripped version\n   109→        preamble_bytes = len(raw.encode(\"utf-8\")) - len(content.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n   110→        tmp_file = output_file.with_suffix(output_file.suffix + \".tmp\")\n   111→        tmp_file.write_text(content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   112→        os.replace(tmp_file, output_file)\n   113→        _log.info(\n   114→            \"Stripped %d-byte leading whitespace from %s\",\n   115→            preamble_bytes,\n   116→            output_file,\n   117→        )\n   118→        return preamble_bytes\n   119→\n   120→    # Search for the first ^--- line (conversational preamble case)\n   121→    match = re.search(r\"^---[ \\t]*$\", content, re.MULTILINE)\n   122→    if match is None:\n   123→        # No frontmatter found at all -- leave file unchanged\n   124→        return 0\n   125→\n   126→    preamble = content[: match.start()]\n   127→    cleaned = content[match.start():]\n   128→    # Total bytes stripped = leading whitespace + conversational preamble\n   129→    preamble_bytes = len(raw.encode(\"utf-8\")) - len(cleaned.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n   130→\n   131→    # Atomic write: tmp file + os.replace\n   132→    tmp_file = output_file.with_suffix(output_file.suffix + \".tmp\")\n   133→    tmp_file.write_text(cleaned, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   134→    os.replace(tmp_file, output_file)\n   135→\n   136→    _log.info(\"Stripped %d-byte preamble from %s\", preamble_bytes, output_file)\n   137→    return preamble_bytes\n   138→\n   139→\n   140→def _inject_pipeline_diagnostics(\n   141→    output_file: Path,\n   142→    started_at: datetime,\n   143→    finished_at: datetime,\n   144→) -> None:\n   145→    \"\"\"Inject executor-populated pipeline_diagnostics into extraction frontmatter.\n   146→\n   147→    The LLM cannot reliably produce execution timing or environment metadata,\n   148→    so the executor injects these fields post-subprocess (FR-033).\n   149→    \"\"\"\n   150→    content = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").lstrip(\"\\n\\r\\t \")\n   151→    if not content.startswith(\"---\"):\n   152→        return\n   153→\n   154→    # Find end of frontmatter\n   155→    end_idx = content.find(\"\\n---\", 3)\n   156→    if end_idx == -1:\n   157→        return\n   158→\n   159→    # Idempotency: skip if already injected\n   160→    frontmatter = content[3:end_idx]\n   161→    if \"pipeline_diagnostics:\" in frontmatter:\n   162→        return\n   163→\n   164→    elapsed = (finished_at - started_at).total_seconds()\n   165→    diagnostics_line = (\n   166→        f\"pipeline_diagnostics: \"\n   167→        f\"{{elapsed_seconds: {elapsed:.1f}, \"\n   168→        f'started_at: \"{started_at.isoformat()}\", '\n   169→        f'finished_at: \"{finished_at.isoformat()}\"}}'\n   170→    )\n   171→\n   172→    # Insert before the closing ---\n   173→    new_content = content[:end_idx] + \"\\n\" + diagnostics_line + content[end_idx:]\n   174→    output_file.write_text(new_content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   175→\n   176→\n   177→def _inject_provenance_fields(\n   178→    output_file: Path,\n   179→    spec_source: str,\n   180→) -> None:\n   181→    \"\"\"Inject provenance fields into test-strategy frontmatter.\n   182→\n   183→    The test-strategy step's LLM output may omit provenance metadata\n   184→    (spec_source, generated, generator) since the prompt focuses on\n   185→    test strategy content. The executor injects these fields post-subprocess\n   186→    to satisfy TEST_STRATEGY_GATE required_frontmatter_fields.\n   187→    \"\"\"\n   188→    content = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").lstrip(\"\\n\\r\\t \")\n   189→    if not content.startswith(\"---\"):\n   190→        return\n   191→\n   192→    # Find end of frontmatter\n   193→    end_idx = content.find(\"\\n---\", 3)\n   194→    if end_idx == -1:\n   195→        return\n   196→\n   197→    # Idempotency: only inject fields that are missing\n   198→    frontmatter = content[3:end_idx]\n   199→\n   200→    fields_to_inject = []\n   201→    if \"spec_source:\" not in frontmatter:\n   202→        fields_to_inject.append(f\"spec_source: {spec_source}\")\n   203→    if \"generated:\" not in frontmatter:\n   204→        generated = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n   205→        fields_to_inject.append(f'generated: \"{generated}\"')\n   206→    if \"generator:\" not in frontmatter:\n   207→        fields_to_inject.append(\"generator: superclaude-roadmap-executor\")\n   208→\n   209→    if not fields_to_inject:\n   210→        return  # All fields already present\n   211→\n   212→    provenance_block = \"\\n\".join(fields_to_inject)\n   213→    new_content = content[:end_idx] + \"\\n\" + provenance_block + content[end_idx:]\n   214→    output_file.write_text(new_content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   215→\n   216→\n   217→def roadmap_run_step(\n   218→    step: Step,\n   219→    config: PipelineConfig,\n   220→    cancel_check: Callable[[], bool],\n   221→) -> StepResult:\n   222→    \"\"\"Execute a single roadmap step as a Claude subprocess.\n   223→\n   224→    Builds argv with context isolation, launches process, waits with\n   225→    timeout, and returns StepResult.\n   226→    \"\"\"\n   227→    started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   228→\n   229→    if config.dry_run:\n   230→        _log.info(\"[dry-run] Would execute step '%s'\", step.id)\n   231→        return StepResult(\n   232→            step=step,\n   233→            status=StepStatus.PASS,\n   234→            attempt=1,\n   235→            started_at=started_at,\n   236→            finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n   237→        )\n   238→\n   239→    # Inline embedding: read input files into the prompt instead of --file flags\n   240→    # --file is broken (cloud download mechanism, not local file injector) so\n   241→    # inline embedding is always used regardless of composed prompt size.\n   242→    embedded = _embed_inputs(step.inputs)\n   243→    if embedded:\n   244→        composed = step.prompt + \"\\n\\n\" + embedded\n   245→        if len(composed.encode(\"utf-8\")) > _EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT:\n   246→            # <= is intentional; _EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT = 120 KB is safely below MAX_ARG_STRLEN = 128 KB\n   247→            _log.warning(\n   248→                \"Step '%s': composed prompt exceeds %d bytes; embedding inline anyway\"\n   249→                \" (--file fallback is unavailable)\",\n   250→                step.id,\n   251→                _EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT,\n   252→            )\n   253→        effective_prompt = composed\n   254→        extra_args: list[str] = []\n   255→    else:\n   256→        effective_prompt = step.prompt\n   257→        extra_args = []\n   258→\n   259→    proc = ClaudeProcess(\n   260→        prompt=effective_prompt,\n   261→        output_file=step.output_file,\n   262→        error_file=step.output_file.with_suffix(\".err\"),\n   263→        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n   264→        model=step.model or config.model,\n   265→        permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n   266→        timeout_seconds=step.timeout_seconds,\n   267→        output_format=\"text\",\n   268→        extra_args=extra_args,\n   269→    )\n   270→\n   271→    proc.start()\n   272→\n   273→    # Poll for cancellation while waiting\n   274→    while proc._process is not None and proc._process.poll() is None:\n   275→        if cancel_check():\n   276→            proc.terminate()\n   277→            return StepResult(\n   278→                step=step,\n   279→                status=StepStatus.CANCELLED,\n   280→                attempt=1,\n   281→                gate_failure_reason=\"Cancelled by external signal\",\n   282→                started_at=started_at,\n   283→                finished_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),\n   284→            )\n   285→        time.sleep(1)\n   286→\n   287→    exit_code = proc.wait()\n   288→    finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n   289→\n   290→    if exit_code == 124:\n   291→        return StepResult(\n   292→            step=step,\n   293→            status=StepStatus.TIMEOUT,\n   294→            attempt=1,\n   295→            gate_failure_reason=f\"Step '{step.id}' timed out after {step.timeout_seconds}s\",\n   296→            started_at=started_at,\n   297→            finished_at=finished_at,\n   298→        )\n   299→\n   300→    if exit_code != 0:\n   301→        return StepResult(\n   302→            step=step,\n   303→            status=StepStatus.FAIL,\n   304→            attempt=1,\n   305→            gate_failure_reason=f\"Step '{step.id}' exited with code {exit_code}\",\n   306→            started_at=started_at,\n   307→            finished_at=finished_at,\n   308→        )\n   309→\n   310→    # Sanitize output: strip conversational preamble before gate validation\n   311→    _sanitize_output(step.output_file)\n   312→\n   313→    # Inject executor-populated fields into extract step frontmatter (FR-033)\n   314→    if step.id == \"extract\" and step.output_file.exists():\n   315→        _inject_pipeline_diagnostics(step.output_file, started_at, finished_at)\n   316→\n   317→    # Inject provenance fields into test-strategy output\n   318→    if step.id == \"test-strategy\" and step.output_file.exists():\n   319→        spec_source = (\n   320→            config.spec_file.name if hasattr(config, \"spec_file\") else \"unknown\"\n   321→        )\n   322→        _inject_provenance_fields(step.output_file, spec_source)\n   323→\n   324→    # Process completed successfully; gate check happens in execute_pipeline\n   325→    return StepResult(\n   326→        step=step,\n   327→        status=StepStatus.PASS,\n   328→        attempt=1,\n   329→        started_at=started_at,\n   330→        finished_at=finished_at,\n   331→    )\n   332→\n   333→\n   334→def build_certify_step(\n   335→    config: RoadmapConfig,\n   336→    findings: list | None = None,\n   337→    context_sections: dict[str, str] | None = None,\n   338→) -> Step:\n   339→    \"\"\"Build a certify Step for execution via execute_pipeline().\n   340→\n   341→    The certify step runs as a standard Step (not ClaudeProcess directly)\n   342→    per spec section 2.5. It uses CERTIFY_GATE for output validation.\n   343→\n   344→    Parameters\n   345→    ----------\n   346→    config:\n   347→        RoadmapConfig with output_dir.\n   348→    findings:\n   349→        List of Finding objects to certify. If None, an empty prompt is built.\n   350→    context_sections:\n   351→        Pre-extracted context sections per finding location (NFR-011).\n   352→    \"\"\"\n   353→    from .models import Finding\n   354→\n   355→    out = config.output_dir\n   356→    certification_report = out / \"certification-report.md\"\n   357→\n   358→    prompt = build_certification_prompt(\n   359→        findings=findings or [],\n   360→        context_sections=context_sections or {},\n   361→    )\n   362→\n   363→    return Step(\n   364→        id=\"certify\",\n   365→        prompt=prompt,\n   366→        output_file=certification_report,\n   367→        gate=CERTIFY_GATE,\n   368→        timeout_seconds=300,\n   369→        inputs=[out / \"remediation-tasklist.md\"],\n   370→        retry_limit=1,\n   371→    )\n   372→\n   373→\n   374→def _build_steps(config: RoadmapConfig) -> list[Step | list[Step]]:\n   375→    \"\"\"Build the 9-step pipeline with parallel generate group.\n   376→\n   377→    Returns a list where each element is either a single Step (sequential)\n   378→    or a list[Step] (parallel group).\n   379→    \"\"\"\n   380→    out = config.output_dir\n   381→\n   382→    # Agent specs\n   383→    agent_a = config.agents[0]\n   384→    agent_b = config.agents[1] if len(config.agents) > 1 else config.agents[0]\n   385→\n   386→    # Output paths\n   387→    extraction = out / \"extraction.md\"\n   388→    roadmap_a = out / f\"roadmap-{agent_a.id}.md\"\n   389→    roadmap_b = out / f\"roadmap-{agent_b.id}.md\"\n   390→    diff_file = out / \"diff-analysis.md\"\n   391→    debate_file = out / \"debate-transcript.md\"\n   392→    score_file = out / \"base-selection.md\"\n   393→    merge_file = out / \"roadmap.md\"\n   394→    test_strat = out / \"test-strategy.md\"\n   395→    spec_fidelity_file = out / \"spec-fidelity.md\"\n   396→\n   397→    # Load retrospective content if configured (missing file handled gracefully)\n   398→    retrospective_content: str | None = None\n   399→    if config.retrospective_file is not None and config.retrospective_file.is_file():\n   400→        try:\n   401→            retrospective_content = config.retrospective_file.read_text(\n   402→                encoding=\"utf-8\"\n   403→            )\n   404→        except OSError:\n   405→            retrospective_content = None\n   406→\n   407→    steps: list[Step | list[Step]] = [\n   408→        # Step 1: Extract\n   409→        Step(\n   410→            id=\"extract\",\n   411→            prompt=build_extract_prompt(\n   412→                config.spec_file,\n   413→                retrospective_content=retrospective_content,\n   414→            ),\n   415→            output_file=extraction,\n   416→            gate=EXTRACT_GATE,\n   417→            timeout_seconds=300,\n   418→            inputs=[config.spec_file],\n   419→            retry_limit=1,\n   420→        ),\n   421→        # Steps 2a+2b: Generate (parallel)\n   422→        [\n   423→            Step(\n   424→                id=f\"generate-{agent_a.id}\",\n   425→                prompt=build_generate_prompt(agent_a, extraction),\n   426→                output_file=roadmap_a,\n   427→                gate=GENERATE_A_GATE,\n   428→                timeout_seconds=900,\n   429→                inputs=[extraction],\n   430→                retry_limit=1,\n   431→                model=agent_a.model,\n   432→            ),\n   433→            Step(\n   434→                id=f\"generate-{agent_b.id}\",\n   435→                prompt=build_generate_prompt(agent_b, extraction),\n   436→                output_file=roadmap_b,\n   437→                gate=GENERATE_B_GATE,\n   438→                timeout_seconds=900,\n   439→                inputs=[extraction],\n   440→                retry_limit=1,\n   441→                model=agent_b.model,\n   442→            ),\n   443→        ],\n   444→        # Step 3: Diff\n   445→        Step(\n   446→            id=\"diff\",\n   447→            prompt=build_diff_prompt(roadmap_a, roadmap_b),\n   448→            output_file=diff_file,\n   449→            gate=DIFF_GATE,\n   450→            timeout_seconds=300,\n   451→            inputs=[roadmap_a, roadmap_b],\n   452→            retry_limit=1,\n   453→        ),\n   454→        # Step 4: Debate\n   455→        Step(\n   456→            id=\"debate\",\n   457→            prompt=build_debate_prompt(diff_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b, config.depth),\n   458→            output_file=debate_file,\n   459→            gate=DEBATE_GATE,\n   460→            timeout_seconds=600,\n   461→            inputs=[diff_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b],\n   462→            retry_limit=1,\n   463→        ),\n   464→        # Step 5: Score\n   465→        Step(\n   466→            id=\"score\",\n   467→            prompt=build_score_prompt(debate_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b),\n   468→            output_file=score_file,\n   469→            gate=SCORE_GATE,\n   470→            timeout_seconds=300,\n   471→            inputs=[debate_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b],\n   472→            retry_limit=1,\n   473→        ),\n   474→        #
