output_file=merge_file,\n   479→            gate=MERGE_GATE,\n   480→            timeout_seconds=600,\n   481→            inputs=[score_file, roadmap_a, roadmap_b, debate_file],\n   482→            retry_limit=1,\n   483→        ),\n   484→        # Step 7: Test Strategy\n   485→        Step(\n   486→            id=\"test-strategy\",\n   487→            prompt=build_test_strategy_prompt(merge_file, extraction),\n   488→            output_file=test_strat,\n   489→            gate=TEST_STRATEGY_GATE,\n   490→            timeout_seconds=300,\n   491→            inputs=[merge_file, extraction],\n   492→            retry_limit=1,\n   493→        ),\n   494→        # Step 8: Spec Fidelity (after test-strategy, FR-008 through FR-010)\n   495→        Step(\n   496→            id=\"spec-fidelity\",\n   497→            prompt=build_spec_fidelity_prompt(config.spec_file, merge_file),\n   498→            output_file=spec_fidelity_file,\n   499→            gate=SPEC_FIDELITY_GATE,\n   500→            timeout_seconds=600,\n   501→            inputs=[config.spec_file, merge_file],\n   502→            retry_limit=1,\n   503→        ),\n   504→    ]\n   505→\n   506→    return steps\n   507→\n   508→\n   509→def _format_halt_output(results: list[StepResult], config: RoadmapConfig) -> str:\n   510→    \"\"\"Format HALT diagnostic output per spec section 6.2.\"\"\"\n   511→    failed = [r for r in results if r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)]\n   512→    passed = [r for r in results if r.status == StepStatus.PASS]\n   513→    cancelled = [r for r in results if r.status == StepStatus.CANCELLED]\n   514→\n   515→    if not failed:\n   516→        return \"\"\n   517→\n   518→    fail = failed[-1]\n   519→    step = fail.step\n   520→\n   521→    # Calculate file details if output exists\n   522→    file_info = \"\"\n   523→    if step and step.output_file.exists():\n   524→        content = step.output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   525→        byte_count = len(content.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n   526→        line_count = len(content.splitlines())\n   527→        file_info = f\"  Output size: {byte_count} bytes ({line_count} lines)\\n\"\n   528→    elif step:\n   529→        file_info = \"  Output file: not created\\n\"\n   530→\n   531→    elapsed = f\"{fail.duration_seconds:.0f}s\"\n   532→\n   533→    lines = [\n   534→        f\"ERROR: Roadmap pipeline halted at step '{step.id}' (attempt {fail.attempt}/2)\",\n   535→        f\"  Gate failure: {fail.gate_failure_reason}\",\n   536→        f\"  Output file: {step.output_file}\",\n   537→        file_info.rstrip(),\n   538→        f\"  Step timeout: {step.timeout_seconds}s | Elapsed: {elapsed}\",\n   539→        \"\",\n   540→        f\"Completed steps: {', '.join(f'{r.step.id} (PASS, attempt {r.attempt})' for r in passed) or 'none'}\",\n   541→        f\"Failed step:     {step.id} ({fail.status.value}, attempt {fail.attempt})\",\n   542→    ]\n   543→\n   544→    # Collect skipped steps\n   545→    all_step_ids = _get_all_step_ids(config)\n   546→    executed_ids = {r.step.id for r in results}\n   547→    skipped_ids = [sid for sid in all_step_ids if sid not in executed_ids]\n   548→    if cancelled:\n   549→        skipped_ids = [r.step.id for r in cancelled] + skipped_ids\n   550→\n   551→    if skipped_ids:\n   552→        lines.append(f\"Skipped steps:   {', '.join(skipped_ids)}\")\n   553→\n   554→    lines.extend(\n   555→        [\n   556→            \"\",\n   557→            \"To retry from this step:\",\n   558→            f\"  superclaude roadmap run {config.spec_file} --resume\",\n   559→            \"\",\n   560→            \"To inspect the failing output:\",\n   561→            f\"  cat {step.output_file}\",\n   562→        ]\n   563→    )\n   564→\n   565→    return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n   566→\n   567→\n   568→def _get_all_step_ids(config: RoadmapConfig) -> list[str]:\n   569→    \"\"\"Get all step IDs in pipeline order.\"\"\"\n   570→    agent_a = config.agents[0]\n   571→    agent_b = config.agents[1] if len(config.agents) > 1 else config.agents[0]\n   572→    return [\n   573→        \"extract\",\n   574→        f\"generate-{agent_a.id}\",\n   575→        f\"generate-{agent_b.id}\",\n   576→        \"diff\",\n   577→        \"debate\",\n   578→        \"score\",\n   579→        \"merge\",\n   580→        \"test-strategy\",\n   581→        \"spec-fidelity\",\n   582→        \"remediate\",\n   583→        \"certify\",\n   584→    ]\n   585→\n   586→\n   587→def _print_terminal_halt(\n   588→    output_dir: Path,\n   589→    remaining_findings: list,\n   590→    attempt_count: int,\n   591→    spec_patch_budget_exhausted: bool = False,\n   592→    file=None,\n   593→) -> None:\n   594→    \"\"\"Print terminal halt message to stderr with finding details (SC-6).\n   595→\n   596→    Outputs:\n   597→    - Attempt count and remaining failing finding count\n   598→    - Per-finding details (ID, severity, description)\n   599→    - Manual-fix instructions including certification report path and resume command\n   600→\n   601→    Args:\n   602→        output_dir: Pipeline output directory (for certification report path)\n   603→        remaining_findings: List of Finding objects that failed remediation\n   604→        attempt_count: Number of remediation attempts made\n   605→        spec_patch_budget_exhausted: If True, note that spec-patch budget is also exhausted\n   606→        file: Output stream (defaults to sys.stderr)\n   607→    \"\"\"\n   608→    if file is None:\n   609→        file = sys.stderr\n   610→\n   611→    certify_path = output_dir / \"certify.md\"\n   612→\n   613→    lines = [\n   614→        \"\",\n   615→        \"=\" * 72,\n   616→        \"TERMINAL HALT: Remediation budget exhausted\",\n   617→        \"=\" * 72,\n   618→        f\"Attempt count: {attempt_count}\",\n   619→        f\"Remaining failing findings: {len(remaining_findings)}\",\n   620→        \"\",\n   621→    ]\n   622→\n   623→    if remaining_findings:\n   624→        lines.append(\"Failing findings:\")\n   625→        for finding in remaining_findings:\n   626→            severity = getattr(finding, \"severity\", \"UNKNOWN\")\n   627→            description = getattr(finding, \"description\", \"\")\n   628→            finding_id = getattr(finding, \"id\", \"UNKNOWN\")\n   629→            lines.append(f\"  - {finding_id} [{severity}]: {description}\")\n   630→        lines.append(\"\")\n   631→\n   632→    lines.extend(\n   633→        [\n   634→            \"Manual fix required:\",\n   635→            f\"  1. Review certification report: {certify_path}\",\n   636→            \"  2. Fix remaining findings manually\",\n   637→            \"  3. Resume pipeline with:\",\n   638→            f\"     superclaude roadmap run --resume\",\n   639→            \"\",\n   640→        ]\n   641→    )\n   642→\n   643→    if spec_patch_budget_exhausted:\n   644→        lines.extend(\n   645→            [\n   646→                \"Note: Both the remediation budget and the spec-patch cycle budget\",\n   647→                \"are exhausted. Full dual-budget recovery is deferred to v2.26.\",\n   648→                \"# TODO(v2.26): implement dual-budget-exhaustion recovery mechanism\",\n   649→                \"\",\n   650→            ]\n   651→        )\n   652→\n   653→    lines.append(\"=\" * 72)\n   654→\n   655→    print(\"\\n\".join(lines), file=file, flush=True)\n   656→\n   657→\n   658→def _check_annotate_deviations_freshness(\n   659→    output_dir: Path,\n   660→    roadmap_file: Path,\n   661→    gate_pass_state: dict[str, bool] | None = None,\n   662→) -> bool:\n   663→    \"\"\"Check if spec-deviations.md hash matches current roadmap.md (SC-8).\n   664→\n   665→    Reads roadmap_hash from spec-deviations.md frontmatter and compares\n   666→    against sha256(roadmap.md). Fail-closed for all error conditions.\n   667→\n   668→    On hash mismatch, resets spec-fidelity and deviation-analysis gate\n   669→    pass state to force re-run (FR-084).\n   670→\n   671→    Returns:\n   672→        True if hash matches (fresh), False for any failure or mismatch.\n   673→    \"\"\"\n   674→    spec_deviations = output_dir / \"spec-deviations.md\"\n   675→\n   676→    # Case 1: spec-deviations.md not found\n   677→    if not spec_deviations.exists():\n   678→        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: spec-deviations.md not found\")\n   679→        return False\n   680→\n   681→    # Case 2: Read error\n   682→    try:\n   683→        content = spec_deviations.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   684→    except OSError as e:\n   685→        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: read error for spec-deviations.md: %s\", e)\n   686→        return False\n   687→\n   688→    # Case 3: Empty file\n   689→    if not content.strip():\n   690→        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: spec-deviations.md is empty\")\n   691→        return False\n   692→\n   693→    # Case 4: Corrupt/missing frontmatter\n   694→    stripped = content.lstrip()\n   695→    if not stripped.startswith(\"---\"):\n   696→        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: spec-deviations.md has no frontmatter\")\n   697→        return False\n   698→\n   699→    rest = stripped[3:].lstrip(\"\\n\")\n   700→    end_idx = rest.find(\"\\n---\")\n   701→    if end_idx == -1:\n   702→        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: spec-deviations.md has corrupt frontmatter\")\n   703→        return False\n   704→\n   705→    # Parse roadmap_hash from frontmatter\n   706→    saved_hash: str | None = None\n   707→    for line in rest[:end_idx].splitlines():\n   708→        line = line.strip()\n   709→        if line.startswith(\"roadmap_hash:\"):\n   710→            saved_hash = line.split(\":\", 1)[1].strip()\n   711→            break\n   712→\n   713→    # Case 5: roadmap_hash field missing or empty\n   714→    if not saved_hash:\n   715→        _log.warning(\n   716→            \"Freshness check: roadmap_hash field missing or empty in spec-deviations.md\"\n   717→        )\n   718→        return False\n   719→\n   720→    # Case 6: roadmap.md not found\n   721→    if not roadmap_file.exists():\n   722→        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: roadmap.md not found at %s\", roadmap_file)\n   723→        return False\n   724→\n   725→    # Case 7: roadmap.md read error\n   726→    try:\n   727→        roadmap_content = roadmap_file.read_bytes()\n   728→    except OSError as e:\n   729→        _log.warning(\"Freshness check: read error for roadmap.md: %s\", e)\n   730→        return False\n   731→\n   732→    # Case 8: Hash comparison\n   733→    current_hash = hashlib.sha256(roadmap_content).hexdigest()\n   734→    if saved_hash != current_hash:\n   735→        _log.warning(\n   736→            \"Freshness check: hash mismatch -- saved=%s current=%s\",\n   737→            saved_hash[:12],\n   738→            current_hash[:12],\n   739→        )\n   740→        # FR-084: reset gate pass state for downstream steps\n   741→        if gate_pass_state is not None:\n   742→            for key in (\"spec-fidelity\", \"deviation-analysis\"):\n   743→                if key in gate_pass_state:\n   744→                    gate_pass_state[key] = False\n   745→        return False\n   746→\n   747→    return True\n   748→\n   749→\n   750→def _check_remediation_budget(\n   751→    output_dir: Path,\n   752→    max_attempts: int = 2,\n   753→    halt_fn: \"Callable[[Path, list, int], None] | None\" = None,\n   754→) -> bool:\n   755→    \"\"\"Check if remediation budget allows another attempt (SC-6).\n   756→\n   757→    Reads remediation_attempts from .roadmap-state.json.\n   758→    If attempts >= max_attempts, calls halt_fn (or _print_terminal_halt)\n   759→    and returns False.\n   760→\n   761→    Non-integer remediation_attempts is coerced to 0 with a WARNING log.\n   762→\n   763→    Args:\n   764→        output_dir: Pipeline output directory containing .roadmap-state.json\n   765→        max_attempts: Maximum allowed remediation attempts (default 2)\n   766→        halt_fn: Optional callable for terminal halt; defaults to _print_terminal_halt\n   767→\n   768→    Returns:\n   769→        True if budget allows another attempt, False if exhausted.\n   770→    \"\"\"\n   771→    state_file = output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n   772→    state = read_state(state_file)\n   773→\n   774→    attempts = 0\n   775→    if state is not None:\n   776→        raw = state.get(\"remediation_attempts\", 0)\n   777→        try:\n   778→            attempts = int(raw)\n   779→        except (ValueError, TypeError):\n   780→            _log.warning(\"Non-integer remediation_attempts %r coerced to 0\", raw)\n   781→            attempts = 0\n   782→\n   783→    if attempts >= max_attempts:\n   784→        if halt_fn is not None:\n   785→            halt_fn(output_dir, [], attempts + 1)\n   786→        else:\n   787→            _print_terminal_halt(\n   788→                output_dir=output_dir,\n   789→                remaining_findings=[],\n   790→                attempt_count=attempts + 1,\n   791→            )\n   792→        return False\n   793→\n   794→    return True\n   795→\n   796→\n   797→def _print_step_start(step: Step) -> None:\n   798→    \"\"\"Print progress output when a step starts.\"\"\"\n   799→    print(f\"[roadmap] Starting step: {step.id}\", flush=True)\n   800→\n   801→\n   802→def _print_step_complete(step: Step, result: StepResult) -> None:\n   803→    \"\"\"Print progress output when a step completes.\"\"\"\n   804→    duration = f\"{result.duration_seconds:.0f}s\"\n   805→    if result.status == StepStatus.PASS:\n   806→        print(\n   807→            f\"[roadmap] Step {step.id}  PASS (attempt {result.attempt}, {duration})\",\n   808→            flush=True,\n   809→        )\n   810→    else:\n   811→        print(\n   812→            f\"[roadmap] Step {step.id}  {result.status.value} (attempt {result.attempt}, {duration})\",\n   813→            flush=True,\n   814→        )\n   815→        if result.gate_failure_reason:\n   816→            print(f\"           Reason: {result.gate_failure_reason}\", flush=True)\n   817→\n   818→\n   819→def _dry_run_output(steps: list[Step | list[Step]]) -> None:\n   820→    \"\"\"Print step plan and gate criteria for --dry-run.\"\"\"\n   821→    step_num = 0\n   822→    for entry in steps:\n   823→        if isinstance(entry, list):\n   824→            for s in entry:\n   825→                step_num += 1\n   826→                _print_step_plan(step_num, s, parallel=True)\n   827→        else:\n   828→            step_num += 1\n   829→            _print_step_plan(step_num, entry)\n   830→\n   831→\n   832→def _print_step_plan(num: int, step: Step, parallel: bool = False) -> None:\n   833→    \"\"\"Print a single step's plan entry.\"\"\"\n   834→    par_label = \" (parallel)\" if parallel else \"\"\n   835→    print(f\"Step {num}{par_label}: {step.id}\")\n   836→    print(f\"  Output: {step.output_file}\")\n   837→    print(f\"  Timeout: {step.timeout_seconds}s\")\n   838→    if step.model:\n   839→        print(f\"  Model: {step.model}\")\n   840→    if step.gate:\n   841→        print(f\"  Gate tier: {step.gate.enforcement_tier}\")\n   842→        print(f\"  Gate min_lines: {step.gate.min_lines}\")\n   843→        if step.gate.required_frontmatter_fields:\n   844→            print(\n   845→                f\"  Gate frontmatter: {',          print(f\"  Semantic checks: {', '.join(checks)}\")\n   850→    print()\n   851→\n   852→\n   853→def _save_state(\n   854→    config: RoadmapConfig,\n   855→    results: list[StepResult],\n   856→    remediate_metadata: dict | None = None,\n   857→    certify_metadata: dict | None = None,\n   858→) -> None:\n   859→    \"\"\"Write .roadmap-state.json to output_dir via atomic tmp + os.replace().\n   860→\n   861→    Preserves existing ``validation``, ``fidelity_status``, ``remediate``,\n   862→    and ``certify`` keys if present. Accepts optional remediate/certify\n   863→    metadata dicts for Phase 6 state finalization.\n   864→\n   865→    Defense-in-depth guards:\n   866→    - No-progress guard: skip write when no steps passed (prevents corruption\n   867→      from broken resumes that fail immediately).\n   868→    - Agent-mismatch guard: preserve original agents in state when no generate\n   869→      steps ran (prevents overwriting correct agent config with wrong agents).\n   870→    \"\"\"\n   871→    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n   872→\n   873→    # No-progress guard: if the results list is empty (no steps were even\n   874→    # attempted), do not write state. This prevents corruption from broken\n   875→    # resumes that produce zero results. Any non-empty results list indicates\n   876→    # the pipeline made progress and state should be recorded.\n   877→    step_results = [r for r in results if r.step]\n   878→    if not step_results:\n   879→        _log.info(\"State not saved: no step results in this run\")\n   880→        return\n   881→\n   882→    # Preserve existing keys across state rewrites\n   883→    existing = read_state(state_file)\n   884→\n   885→    # Agent-mismatch guard: if agents differ from state and no generate steps\n   886→    # ran, preserve original agents to prevent corruption\n   887→    if existing is not None:\n   888→        saved_agents = existing.get(\"agents\", [])\n   889→        current_agents = [\n   890→            {\"model\": a.model, \"persona\": a.persona} for a in config.agents\n   891→        ]\n   892→        if saved_agents and saved_agents != current_agents:\n   893→            generate_ran = any(\n   894→                r.step\n   895→                and r.step.id.startswith(\"generate-\")\n   896→                and r.status == StepStatus.PASS\n   897→                for r in results\n   898→            )\n   899→            if not generate_ran:\n   900→                _log.warning(\n   901→                    \"Agents differ from state file and no generate steps ran; \"\n   902→                    \"preserving original agents in state\"\n   903→                )\n   904→                return\n   905→\n   906→    existing_validation = existing.get(\"validation\") if existing else None\n   907→    existing_fidelity = existing.get(\"fidelity_status\") if existing else None\n   908→    existing_remediate = existing.get(\"remediate\") if existing else None\n   909→    existing_certify = existing.get(\"certify\") if existing else None\n   910→\n   911→    spec_hash = hashlib.sha256(config.spec_file.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n   912→\n   913→    state = {\n   914→        \"schema_version\": 1,\n   915→        \"spec_file\": str(config.spec_file),\n   916→        \"spec_hash\": spec_hash,\n   917→        \"agents\": [{\"model\": a.model, \"persona\": a.persona} for a in config.agents],\n   918→        \"depth\": config.depth,\n   919→        \"last_run\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n   920→        \"steps\": {\n   921→            r.step.id: {\n   922→                \"status\": r.status.value,\n   923→                \"attempt\": r.attempt,\n   924→                \"output_file\": str(r.step.output_file),\n   925→                \"started_at\": r.started_at.isoformat(),\n   926→                \"completed_at\": r.finished_at.isoformat(),\n   927→            }\n   928→            for r in results\n   929→            if r.step\n   930→        },\n   931→    }\n   932→\n   933→    # Merge with existing state (preserve steps not in this run)\n   934→    if existing:\n   935→        existing_steps = existing.get(\"steps\", {})\n   936→        for step_id, step_data in existing_steps.items():\n   937→            if step_id not in state[\"steps\"]:\n   938→                state[\"steps\"][step_id] = step_data\n   939→\n   940→    if existing_validation is not None:\n   941→        state[\"validation\"] = existing_validation\n   942→\n   943→    # Derive fidelity_status from spec-fidelity step result\n   944→    fidelity_result = next(\n   945→        (r for r in results if r.step and r.step.id == \"spec-fidelity\"),\n   946→        None,\n   947→    )\n   948→    if fidelity_result is not None:\n   949→        state[\"fidelity_status\"] = _derive_fidelity_status(fidelity_result)\n   950→    elif existing_fidelity is not None:\n   951→        state[\"fidelity_status\"] = existing_fidelity\n   952→\n   953→    # Remediate metadata (spec §3.1)\n   954→    if remediate_metadata is not None:\n   955→        state[\"remediate\"] = remediate_metadata\n   956→    elif existing_remediate is not None:\n   957→        state[\"remediate\"] = existing_remediate\n   958→\n   959→    # Certify metadata (spec §3.1)\n   960→    if certify_metadata is not None:\n   961→        state[\"certify\"] = certify_metadata\n   962→    elif existing_certify is not None:\n   963→        state[\"certify\"] = existing_certify\n   964→\n   965→    write_state(state, state_file)\n   966→\n   967→\n   968→def _derive_fidelity_status(result: StepResult) -> str:\n   969→    \"\"\"Derive fidelity_status enum from a spec-fidelity StepResult.\n   970→\n   971→    Returns one of: 'pass', 'fail', 'skipped', 'degraded'.\n   972→    \"\"\"\n   973→    if result.status == StepStatus.PASS:\n   974→        # Check if the output indicates degraded mode\n   975→        if result.step and result.step.output_file.exists():\n   976→            content = result.step.output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   977→            if \"validation_complete: false\" in content:\n   978→                return \"degraded\"\n   979→        return \"pass\"\n   980→    if result.status == StepStatus.SKIPPED:\n   981→        return \"skipped\"\n   982→    if result.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT):\n   983→        return \"fail\"\n   984→    return \"skipped\"\n   985→\n   986→\n   987→def generate_degraded_report(\n   988→    output_file: Path,\n   989→    failed_agent: str,\n   990→    failure_reason: str,\n   991→) -> None:\n   992→    \"\"\"Generate a degraded fidelity report when the agent fails.\n   993→\n   994→    Produces a report with validation_complete=false and\n   995→    fidelity_check_attempted=true so the SPEC_FIDELITY_GATE\n   996→    can distinguish degraded from clean passes (NFR-007).\n   997→\n   998→    The degraded report names the failed agent and reason in the body.\n   999→    \"\"\"\n  1000→    report = (\n  1001→        \"---\\n\"\n  1002→        \"high_severity_count: 0\\n\"\n  1003→        \"medium_severity_count: 0\\n\"\n  1004→        \"low_severity_count: 0\\n\"\n  1005→        \"total_deviations: 0\\n\"\n  1006→        \"validation_complete: false\\n\"\n  1007→        \"fidelity_check_attempted: true\\n\"\n  1008→        \"tasklist_ready: false\\n\"\n  1009→ 1030→\n  1031→\n  1032→def build_remediate_metadata(\n  1033→    status: str,\n  1034→    scope: str,\n  1035→    findings_total: int,\n  1036→    findings_actionable: int,\n  1037→    findings_fixed: derive_pipeline_status(state: dict) -> str:\n  1085→    \"\"\"Derive the overall pipeline status from state transitions.\n  1086→\n  1087→    State transitions at each boundary:\n  1088→    - post-validate: validated | validated-with-issues\n  1089→    - post-remediate: remediated\n  1090→    - post-certify: certified | certified-with-caveats\n  1091→\n  1092→    Returns one of: 'pending', None:\n  1107→        if validation.get(\"status\") == \"pass\":\n  1108→            return \"validated\"\n  1109→        if validation.get(\"status\") == \"fail\":\n  1110→            return \"validated-with-issues\"\n  1111→\n  1112→    return \"pending\"\n  1113→\n  1114→\n  1115→def write_state(state: dict, path: Path) -> None:\n  1116→    \"\"\"Write state dict to path atomically via state from path with graceful recovery on missing/malformed files.\"\"\"\n  1127→    if not path.exists():\n  1128→        return None\n  1129→    try:\n  1130→        text = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n  1131→        if not text.strip():\n  1132→            return None\n  1133→        return json.loads(text)\n  1134→    except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n  1135→        return None\n  1136→\n  1137→\n  1138→def apply_decomposition_pass(deliverables: list[Deliverable]) -> list[Deliverable]:\n  1139→    \"\"\"Post-generation decomposition pass for the Idempotent: running twice produces identical results because\n  1145→    already-decomposed deliverables (IDs ending .a/.b) are skipped.\n  1146→\n  1147→    Preserves milestone-internal ordering: 1153→def _restore_from_state(\n  1154→    config: RoadmapConfig,\n  1155→    agents_explicit: bool,\n  1156→    depth_explicit: bool,\n  1157→) -> RoadmapConfig:\n  1158→    \"\"\"Restore agent/depth pipeline uses the same\n  1162→    configuration as the original run.\n  1163→    \"\"\"\n  1164→    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n  1165→    state =         return config\n  1191→\n  1192→            if restored != config.agents:\n  1193→                agent_str = \", \".join(f\"{a.model}:{a.persona}\" for a in restored)\n  1194→                print(\n  1195→                    f\"[roadmap] Restoring agents from state file: {agent_str}\",\n  1196→                    flush=True,\n  1197→                )\n  1198→            config.agents = --depth\n  1203→    if not depth_explicit:\n  1204→        saved_depth = state.get(\"depth\")\n  1205→        if saved_depth:\n  1206→            if saved_depth != config.depth:\n  1207→ {config.depth}\\n\"\n  1211→                    f\"  Using state file depth: {saved_depth}\\n\",\n  1212→                    file=sys.stderr,\n  1213→                    flush=True,\n  1214→                )\n  1215→            config.depth = saved_depth\n  1216→\n  1217→    return config\n  1218→\n  1219→\n  1220→def execute_roadmap(\n  1221→    config: RoadmapConfig,\n  1222→    resume: bool = False,\n  1223→    no_validate: bool = False,\n  1224→    auto_accept: bool = False,\n  1225→    agents_explicit: bool = True,\n  1226→    depth_explicit: bool = True,\n  1227→) -> None:\n  1228→    \"\"\"Execute the roadmap generation pipeline.\n  1229→\n  1230→    Builds the step list, handles --dry-run and --resume, then\n  1231→    delegates to execute_pipeline() with roadmap_run_step.\n  1232→\n  1233→    After all 9 steps pass, auto-invokes validation unless\n  1234→    --no-validate is set or --resume halted on a failed step.\n  1235→\n  1236→    Note: --no-validate does NOT skip the spec-fidelity step\n  1237→    (FR-010, AC-005). It only skips the post-pipeline validation\n  1238→    subsystem.\n  1239→\n  1240→    Args:\n  1241→        auto_accept: When True, the spec-patch resume cycle skips the\n  1242→            interactive prompt and proceeds automatically if evidence is\n  1243→            found. When False (default), the cycle prompts the user.\n  1244→            This is an internal parameter, not exposed on the CLI.\n  1245→        agents_explicit: Whether --agents was explicitly passed on the CLI.\n  1246→        depth_explicit: Whether --depth was explicitly passed on the CLI.\n  1247→    \"\"\"\n  1248→    config.output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n  1249→\n  1250→    # On --resume with defaulted agents/depth, restore from state file\n  1251→    if resume:\n  1252→        config = _restore_from_state(\n  1253→            config,\n  1254→            agents_explicit=agents_explicit,\n  1255→            depth_explicit=depth_explicit,\n  1256→        )\n  1257→\n  1258→    # Apply hardcoded defaults for any still-None fields (non-resume or no state)\n  1259→    if not config.agents:\n  1260→        config.agents = [AgentSpec(\"opus\", \"architect\"), AgentSpec(\"haiku\", \"architect\")]\n  1261→    if not config.depth:\n  1262→        config.depth = \"standard\"\n  1263→\n  1264→    # FR-2.24.1.11: Recursion guard — local variable, per-invocation\n  1265→    _spec_patch_cycle_count = 0\n  1266→\n  1267→    # FR-2.24.1.9 Condition 3: Capture spec hash at function entry\n  1268→    initial_spec_hash = hashlib.sha256(config.spec_file.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n  1269→\n  1270→    steps = _build_steps(config)\n  1271→\n  1272→    # --dry-run: print plan and exit\n  1273→    if config.dry_run:\n  1274→        _dry_run_output(steps)\n  1275→        return\n  1276→\n  1277→    # --resume: check which steps already pass their gates\n  1278→    if resume:\n  1279→        from ..pipeline.gates import gate_passed\n  1280→\n  1281→        steps = _apply_resume(steps, config, gate_passed)\n  1282→\n  1283→    # Execute pipeline\n  1284→    results = execute_pipeline(\n  1285→        steps=steps,\n  1286→        config=config,\n  1287→        run_step=roadmap_run_step,\n  1288→        on_step_start=_print_step_start,\n  1289→        on_step_complete=_print_step_complete,\n  1290→    )\n  1291→\n  1292→    # Save state\n  1293→    _save_state(config, results)\n  1294→\n  1295→    # Check for failures\n  1296→    failures = [r for r in results if r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)]\n  1297→    if failures:\n  1298→        # FR-2.24.1.9: Check if spec-fidelity failed and auto-resume is possible\n  1299→        spec_fidelity_failed = any(\n  1300→            r.step\n  1301→            and r.step.id == \"spec-fidelity\"\n  1302→            and r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)\n  1303→            for r in results\n  1304→        )\n  1305→\n  1306→        if spec_fidelity_failed:\n  1307→            resumed = _apply_resume_after_spec_patch(\n  1308→                config=config,\n  1309→                results=results,\n  1310→                auto_accept=auto_accept,\n  1311→                initial_spec_hash=initial_spec_hash,\n  1312→                cycle_count=_spec_patch_cycle_count,\n  1313→            )\n  1314→            if resumed:\n  1315→                _spec_patch_cycle_count += 1\n  1316→                # Cycle complete — resumed pipeline ran to completion or failure\n  1317→                # inside _apply_resume_after_spec_patch. If it returned True,\n  1318→                # we're done (either success or failure was handled internally).\n  1319→                return\n  1320→\n  1321→        halt_msg = _format_halt_output(results, config)\n  1322→        print(halt_msg, file=sys.stderr)\n  1323→        sys.exit(1)\n  1324→\n  1325→    print(f\"\\n[roadmap] Pipeline complete: {len(results)} steps passed\", flush=True)\n  1326→\n  1327→    # Auto-invoke validation after successful pipeline completion\n  1328→    if no_validate:\n  1329→        print(\"[roadmap] Validation skipped (--no-validate)\", flush=True)\n  1330→        _save_validation_status(config, \"skipped\")\n  1331→        return\n  1332→\n  1333→    # Check if validation already completed (--resume path)\n  1334→    if resume:\n  1335→        state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n  1336→        state = read_state(state_file)\n  1337→        if state and \"validation\" in state:\n  1338→            saved = state[\"validation\"]\n  1339→            if saved.get(\"status\") in (\"pass\", \"fail\"):\n  1340→                print(\n  1341→                    f\"[roadmap] Validation already completed ({saved['status']}), skipping\",\n  1342→                    flush=True,\n  1343→                )\n  1344→                return\n  1345→\n  1346→    _auto_invoke_validate(config)\n  1347→\n  1348→\n  1349→def _find_qualifying_deviation_files(\n  1350→    config: RoadmapConfig,\n  1351→    results: list[StepResult],\n  1352→) -> list:\n  1353→    \"\"\"Find deviation files written after spec-fidelity started.\n  1354→\n  1355→    Returns qualifying DeviationRecord objects, or empty list if\n  1356→    conditions are not met.\n  1357→\n  1358→    Implementation detail — not specified in the spec. Extracted for\n  1359→    testability of the three-condition detection gate.\n  1360→    \"\"\"\n  1361→    from .spec_patch import scan_accepted_deviation_records\n  1362→\n  1363→    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n  1364→    state = read_state(state_file)\n  1365→    if state is None:\n  1366→        return []\n  1367→\n  1368→    # Get spec-fidelity started_at timestamp\n  1369→    steps_state = state.get(\"steps\", {})\n  1370→    fidelity_state = steps_state.get(\"spec-fidelity\", {})\n  1371→    started_at_str = fidelity_state.get(\"started_at\")\n  1372→\n  1373→    # Fail-closed: if started_at is absent, condition not met\n  1374→    if not started_at_str:\n  1375→        return []\n  1376→\n  1377→    try:\n  1378→        started_at_ts = datetime.fromisoformat(started_at_str).timestamp()\n  1379→    except (ValueError, TypeError):\n  1380→        return []\n  1381→\n  1382→    # Scan all deviation records\n  1383→    all_records = scan_accepted_deviation_records(config.output_dir)\n  1384→    if not all_records:\n  1385→        return []\n  1386→\n  1387→    # Filter to records written AFTER spec-fidelity started.\n  1388→    # Strict > (not >=): a record with mtime == started_at was written before\n  1389→    # or exactly at step start, so it cannot be evidence of a subprocess fix.\n  1390→    # Caveat: HFS+ and some NFS mounts have 1-second mtime resolution, so\n  1391→    # records created within the same second as started_at may be missed.\n  1392→    qualifying = [r for r in all_records if r.mtime > started_at_ts]\n  1393→    return qualifying\n  1394→\n  1395→\n  1396→def _apply_resume_after_spec_patch(\n  1397→    config: RoadmapConfig,\n  1398→    results: list[StepResult],\n  1399→    auto_accept: bool,\n  1400→    initial_spec_hash: str,\n  1401→    cycle_count: int,\n  1402→) -> bool:\n  1403→    \"\"\"Attempt a single spec-patch auto-resume cycle after spec-fidelity FAIL.\n  1404→\n  1405→    Evaluates the three-condition detection gate (FR-2.24.1.9):\n  1406→      1. Recursion guard: cycle_count == 0\n  1407→      2. Qualifying deviation files exist with mtime > started_at\n  1408→      3. Spec file hash changed since run started (initial_spec_hash)\n  1409→\n  1410→    If all conditions pass, executes the six-step disk-reread sequence\n  1411→    (FR-2.24.1.10) and re-runs the pipeline via _apply_resume.\n  1412→\n  1413→    Single-writer assumption: no concurrent writer modifies\n  1414→    .roadmap-state.json between the reread and write steps.\n  1415→\n  1416→    Returns True if the cycle was attempted (regardless of outcome),\n  1417→    False if conditions were not met.\n  1418→    \"\"\"\n  1419→    # FR-2.24.1.11: Recursion guard\n  1420→    if cycle_count >= 1:\n  1421→        print(\n  1422→            \"[roadmap] Spec-patch cycle already exhausted \"\n  1423→            f\"(cycle_count={cycle_count}). Proceeding to normal failure.\",\n  1424→            flush=True,\n  1425→        )\n  1426→        return False\n  1427→\n  1428→    # FR-2.24.1.9 Condition 2: Qualifying deviation files\n  1429→    qualifying = _find_qualifying_deviation_files(config, results)\n  1430→    if not qualifying:\n  1431→        return False\n  1432→\n  1433→    # FR-2.24.1.9 Condition 3: Spec hash changed since run started\n  1434→    current_hash = hashlib.sha256(config.spec_file.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n  1435→    if current_hash == initial_spec_hash:\n  1436→        return False\n  1437→\n  1438→    # All three conditions met — enter the cycle\n  1439→    # FR-2.24.1.12: Cycle entry logging\n  1440→    print(\n  1441→        f\"[roadmap] Spec patched by subprocess. \"\n  1442→        f\"Found {len(qualifying)} accepted deviation record(s).\",\n  1443→        flush=True,\n  1444→    )\n  1445→    print(\n  1446→        \"[roadmap] Triggering spec-hash sync and resume (cycle 1/1).\",\n  1447→        flush=True,\n  1448→    )\n  1449→\n  1450→    # FR-2.24.1.10: Six-step disk-reread sequence\n  1451→    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n  1452→\n  1453→    # Step 1: Re-read state from disk\n  1454→    _fresh_state = read_state(state_file)  # noqa: F841\n  1455→\n  1456→    # Step 2: Recompute spec hash\n  1457→    new_hash = hashlib.sha256(config.spec_file.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n  1458→\n  1459→    # Step 3: Atomic write of new hash\n  1460→    try:\n  1461→        # Read again for fresh copy, update only spec_hash, write atomically\n  1462→        fresh_for_write = read_state(state_file) or {}\n  1463→        fresh_for_write[\"spec_hash\"] = new_hash\n  1464→        write_state(fresh_for_write, state_file)\n  1465→    except OSError as exc:\n  1466→        print(\n  1467→            f\"[roadmap] ERROR: Failed to update spec_hash during \"\n  1468→            f\"auto-resume cycle: {exc}. Falling through to normal failure.\",\n  1469→            file=sys.stderr,\n  1470→            flush=True,\n  1471→        )\n  1472→        return True  # Cycle was attempted but failed — don't retry\n  1473→\n  1474→    # Step 4: Re-read state from disk AGAIN (this is what _apply_resume gets)\n  1475→    post_write_state = read_state(state_file)\n  1476→    if post_write_state is None:\n  1477→        print(\n  1478→            \"[roadmap] ERROR: Could not re-read state after write. \"\n  1479→            \"Falling through to normal failure.\",\n  1480→            file=sys.stderr,\n  1481→            flush=True,\n  1482→        )\n  1483→        return True\n  1484→\n  1485→    # Step 5: Rebuild steps\n  1486→    steps = _build_steps(config)\n  1487→\n  1488→    # Step 6: Apply resume with post-write state\n  1489→    from ..pipeline.gates import gate_passed\n  1490→\n  1491→    steps = _apply_resume(steps, config, gate_passed)\n  1492→\n  1493→    # Re-execute pipeline from the resumed point\n  1494→    resumed_results = execute_pipeline(\n  1495→        steps=steps,\n  1496→        config=config,\n  1497→        run_step=roadmap_run_step,\n  1498→        on_step_start=_print_step_start,\n  1499→        on_step_complete=_print_step_complete,\n  1500→    )\n  1501→\n  1502→    # Save state from resumed run\n  1503→    _save_state(config, resumed_results)\n  1504→\n  1505→    # FR-2.24.1.12: Cycle completion logging\n  1506→    print(\"[roadmap] Spec-patch resume cycle complete.\", flush=True)\n  1507→\n  1508→    # Check if resumed pipeline also failed\n  1509→    resumed_failures = [\n  1510→        r for r in resumed_results if r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)\n  1511→    ]\n  1512→    if resumed_failures:\n  1513→        # FR-2.24.1.13: Normal failure on cycle exhaustion\n  1514→        # Use second-run results only\n  1515→        halt_msg = _format_halt_output(resumed_results, config)\n  1516→        print(halt_msg, file=sys.stderr)\n  1517→        sys.exit(1)\n  1518→\n  1519→    # Resumed pipeline succeeded\n  1520→    print(\n  1521→        f\"\\n[roadmap] Pipeline complete: {len(resumed_results)} steps passed\",\n  1522→        flush=True,\n  1523→    )\n  1524→    return True\n  1525→\n  1526→\n  1527→def _save_validation_status(\n  1528→    config: RoadmapConfig,\n  1529→    status: str,\n  1530→) -> None:\n  1531→    \"\"\"Update .roadmap-state.json with validation status.\n  1532→\n  1533→    Adds or updates the ``validation`` key without modifying existing state.\n  1534→\n  1535→    Parameters\n  1536→    ----------\n  1537→    config:\n  1538→        RoadmapConfig with output_dir.\n  1539→    status:\n  1540→        One of \"pass\", \"fail\", or \"skipped\".\n  1541→    \"\"\"\n  1542→    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n  1543→    state = read_state(state_file)\n  1544→    if state is None:\n  1545→        state = {}\n  1546→    state[\"validation\"] = {\n  1547→        \"status\": status,\n  1548→        \"timestamp\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\n  1549→    }\n  1550→    write_state(state, state_file)\n  1551→\n  1552→\n  1553→def _auto_invoke_validate(config: RoadmapConfig) -> None:\n  1554→    \"\"\"Auto-invoke validation after a successful roadmap pipeline run.\n  1555→\n  1556→    Inherits --model, --max-turns, --debug from the parent roadmap config.\n  1557→    Default agent count for roadmap-run invocation is 2 (dual-agent for rigor).\n  1558→    \"\"\"\n  1559→    from .models import AgentSpec, ValidateConfig\n  1560→    from .validate_executor import execute_validate\n  1561→\n  1562→    # Default to 2 agents for roadmap-run auto-invocation (dual-agent for rigor per OQ-1)\n  1563→    validate_agents = config.agents[:2] if len(config.agents) >= 2 else config.agents\n  1564→\n  1565→    validate_config = ValidateConfig(\n  1566→        output_dir=config.output_dir,\n  1567→        agents=validate_agents,\n  1568→        work_dir=config.output_dir,\n  1569→        max_turns=config.max_turns,\n  1570→        model=config.model,\n  1571→        debug=config.debug,\n  1572→    )\n  1573→\n  1574→    print(\"\\n[roadmap] Auto-invoking validation...\", flush=True)\n  1575→\n  1576→    try:\n  1577→        counts = execute_validate(validate_config)\n  1578→        blocking = counts.get(\"blocking_count\", 0)\n  1579→        warning = counts.get(\"warning_count\", 0)\n  1580→        info = counts.get(\"info_count\", 0)\n  1581→        print(\n  1582→            f\"[validate] Complete: {blocking} blocking, {warning} warning, {info} info\",\n  1583→            flush=True,\n  1584→        )\n  1585→        validation_status = \"fail\" if blocking > 0 else \"pass\"\n  1586→        _save_validation_status(config, validation_status)\n  1587→    except FileNotFoundError as exc:\n  1588→        _log.error(\"Validation skipped: %s\", exc)\n  1589→        print(f\"[validate] Skipped: {exc}\", file=sys.stderr, flush=True)\n  1590→        _save_validation_status(config, \"skipped\")\n  1591→\n  1592→\n  1593→def check_remediate_resume(\n  1594→    config: RoadmapConfig,\n  1595→    gate_fn: Callable,\n  1596→) -> bool:\n  1597→    \"\"\"Check if the remediate step can be skipped on --resume.\n  1598→\n  1599→    Returns True (skip) when:\n  1600→    1. remediation-tasklist.md exists\n  1601→    2. Passes REMEDIATE_GATE\n  1602→    3. source_report_hash matches current validation report SHA-256\n  1603→\n  1604→    Returns False (re-run needed) otherwise.\n  1605→    \"\"\"\n  1606→    from .gates import REMEDIATE_GATE\n  1607→\n  1608→    tasklist_file = config.output_dir / \"remediation-tasklist.md\"\n  1609→    if not tasklist_file.exists():\n  1610→        return False\n  1611→\n  1612→    passed, _reason = gate_fn(tasklist_file, REMEDIATE_GATE)\n  1613→    if not passed:\n  1614→        return False\n  1615→\n  1616→    # Hash check: verify tasklist was generated from current validation report\n  1617→    if not _check_tasklist_hash_current(tasklist_file, config.output_dir):\n  1618→        print(\n  1619→            \"[roadmap] Remediation tasklist stale (hash mismatch), will re-run remediate\",\n  1620→            flush=True,\n  1621→        )\n  1622→        return False\n  1623→\n  1624→    return True\n  1625→\n  1626→\n  1627→def check_certify_resume(\n  1628→    config: RoadmapConfig,\n  1629→    gate_fn: Callable,\n  1630→) -> bool:\n  1631→    \"\"\"Check if the certify step can be skipped on --resume.\n  1632→\n  1633→    Returns True (skip) when:\n  1634→    1. certification-report.md exists\n  1635→    2. Passes CERTIFY_GATE\n  1636→\n  1637→    Returns False (re-run needed) otherwise.\n  1638→    \"\"\"\n  1639→    from .gates import CERTIFY_GATE\n  1640→\n  1641→    report_file = config.output_dir / \"certification-report.md\"\n  1642→    if not report_file.exists():\n  1643→        return False\n  1644→\n  1645→    passed, _reason = gate_fn(report_file, CERTIFY_GATE)\n  1646→    return passed\n  1647→\n  1648→\n  1649→def _check_tasklist_hash_current(\n  1650→    tasklist_file: Path,\n  1651→    output_dir: Path,\n  1652→) -> bool:\n  1653→    \"\"\"Check if remediation tasklist's source_report_hash matches current report.\n  1654→\n  1655→    Reads the YAML frontmatter source_report_hash and compares against\n  1656→    SHA-256 of the validation report file. Returns False on mismatch\n  1657→    (fail closed).\n  1658→    \"\"\"\n  1659→    from .gates import _parse_frontmatter\n  1660→\n  1661→    content = tasklist_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n  1662→    fm = _parse_frontmatter(content)\n  1663→    if fm is None:\n  1664→        return False\n  1665→\n  1666→    saved_hash = fm.get(\"source_report_hash\", \"\")\n  1667→    if not saved_hash:\n  1668→        return False\n  1669→\n  1670→    # Find the validation report (source_report field)\n  1671→    source_report = fm.get(\"source_report\", \"\")\n  1672→    if source_report:\n  1673→        report_path = Path(source_report)\n  1674→        if not report_path.is_absolute():\n  1675→            report_path = output_dir / report_path\n  1676→    else:\n  1677→        # Default to reflect-merged.md or merged-validation-report.md\n  1678→        report_path = output_dir / \"reflect-merged.md\"\n  1679→        if not report_path.exists():\n  1680→            report_path = output_dir / \"merged-validation-report.md\"\n  1681→\n  1682→    if not report_path.exists():\n  1683→        return False\n  1684→\n  1685→    current_hash = hashlib.sha256(report_path.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n  1686→    return saved_hash == current_hash\n  1687→\n  1688→\n  1689→def _step_needs_rerun(\n  1690→    step: Step,\n  1691→    gate_fn: Callable,\n  1692→    dirty_outputs: set[Path],\n  1693→    force_extract: bool,\n  1694→    state_paths: dict[str, Path],\n  1695→) -> tuple[bool, str]:\n  1696→    \"\"\"Determine if a single step needs re-running.\n  1697→\n  1698→    A step needs re-run if:\n  1699→    1. force_extract and step is 'extract', OR\n  1700→    2. Any of its input files are in the dirty set, OR\n  1701→    3. Its own gate check fails (using state-recorded paths when available).\n  1702→\n  1703→    Returns:\n  1704→        (needs_rerun: bool, reason: str)\n  1705→    \"\"\"\n  1706→    if force_extract and step.id == \"extract\":\n  1707→        return True, \"spec file changed; forcing extract rerun\"\n  1708→\n  1709→    # If any input was re-generated, must re-run\n  1710→    if any(inp in dirty_outputs for inp in (step.inputs or [])):\n  1711→        deps = [str(inp) for inp in (step.inputs or []) if inp in dirty_outputs]\n  1712→        return True, f\"input dependency regenerated: {deps}\"\n  1713→\n  1714→    # Defense-in-depth: use state-recorded path for gate check\n  1715→    check_path = state_paths.get(step.id, step.output_file)\n  1716→    if check_path != step.output_file:\n  1717→        _log.info(\n  1718→            \"Resume: step '%s' using state-recorded path %s \"\n  1719→            \"(config-derived: %s)\",\n  1720→            step.id,\n  1721→            check_path,\n  1722→            step.output_file,\n  1723→        )\n  1724→\n  1725→    # Check own gate\n  1726→    if step.gate:\n  1727→        passed, reason = gate_fn(check_path, step.gate)\n  1728→        if passed:\n  1729→            return False, \"gate passes\"\n  1730→        # Log the failure reason with diagnostic hint\n  1731→        if reason and \"File not found\" in reason:\n  1732→            print(\n  1733→                f\"[roadmap] {step.id}: output missing ({check_path.name})\\n\"\n  1734→                f\"  Hint: Were different --agents used in the original run?\",\n  1735→                file=sys.stderr,\n  1736→                flush=True,\n  1737→            )\n  1738→        else:\n  1739→            print(f\"[roadmap] {step.id}: gate failed -- {reason}\", flush=True)\n  1740→        return True, f\"gate failed: {reason}\"\n  1741→\n  1742→    # No gate defined -- must run\n  1743→    return True, \"no gate defined\"\n  1744→\n  1745→\n  1746→def _apply_resume(\n  1747→    steps: list[Step | list[Step]],\n  1748→    config: RoadmapConfig,\n  1749→    gate_fn: Callable,\n  1750→) -> list[Step | list[Step]]:\n  1751→    \"\"\"Apply --resume logic: skip steps whose outputs pass gates.\n  1752→\n  1753→    Uses dependency tracking: if a step's inputs include the output of a\n  1754→    step that will be re-run, it must also re-run regardless of gate status.\n  1755→\n  1756→    Includes state-driven path resolution and parallel group semantics.\n  1757→    \"\"\"\n  1758→    state_file = config.output_dir / \".roadmap-state.json\"\n  1759→    state = read_state(state_file)\n  1760→    force_extract = False\n  1761→\n  1762→    # State-driven path resolution: build lookup step_id -> recorded output_file\n  1763→    state_paths: dict[str, Path] = {}\n  1764→    if state is not None:\n  1765→        saved_hash = state.get(\"spec_hash\", \"\")\n  1766→        current_hash = hashlib.sha256(config.spec_file.read_bytes()).hexdigest()\n  1767→        if saved_hash and saved_hash != current_hash:\n  1768→            print(\n  1769→                f\"WARNING: spec-file has changed since last run.\\n\"\n  1770→                f\"  Last hash: {saved_hash[:12]}...\\n\"\n  1771→                f\"  Current:   {current_hash[:12]}...\\n\"\n  1772→                f\"Forcing re-run of extract step.\",\n  1773→                file=sys.stderr,\n  1774→                flush=True,\n  1775→            )\n  1776→            force_extract = True\n  1777→\n  1778→        # files that will be regenerated\n  1787→\n  1788→    for entry in steps:\n  1789→        if isinstance(entry, list):\n  1790→            # Parallel group: check each step, re-run entire group if any needs it\n  1791→            group_needs_rerun = False\n  1792→            rerun_reasons: list[str] = []\n  1793→\n  1794→            for s in entry:\n  1795→                needs, reason = _step_needs_rerun(\n  1796→                    s, gate_fn, dirty_outputs, force_extract, state_paths,\n  1797→                )\n  1798→                if needs:\n  1799→                    group_needs_rerun = True\n  1800→                    rerun_reasons.append(f\"{s.id}: {reason}\")\n  1801→\n  1802→            if group_needs_rerun:\n  1803→                _log.info(\n  1804→                    \"Parallel group [%s] marked for rerun: %s\",\n  1805→                    \", \".join(s.id for s in entry),\n  1806→                    \"; \".join(rerun_reasons),\n  1807→                )\n  1808→                result.append(entry)\n  1809→                # Mark all group outputs as dirty (atomic group completion)\n  1810→                for s in entry:\n  1811→                    dirty_outputs.add(s.output_file)\n  1812→            else:\n  1813→                skipped += len(entry)\n  1814→                print(\n  1815→                    f\"[roadmap] Skipping {', '.join(s.id for s in entry)} (gates pass)\",\n  1816→                    flush=True,\n  1817→                )\n  1818→        else:\n  1819→            # Nothing to do.\", flush=True)\n  1837→\n  1838→    return result\n logging.getLogger(\"superclaude.roadmap.executor\")\n\n# Linux kernel compile-time constant (arch/arm64, x86_64, etc.)\n_MAX_ARG_STRLEN = 128 * 1024\n# 2.3x safety factor; measured template peak ~3.4 KB\n_PROMPT_TEMPLATE_OVERHEAD = 8 * 1024\n# Derivation: MAX_ARG_STRLEN - PROMPT_TEMPLATE_OVERHEAD = 120 KB = 122,880 bytes\n_EMBED_SIZE_LIMIT = _MAX_ARG_STRLEN - _PROMPT_TEMPLATE_OVERHEAD\nassert <path>`` header.\n    Returns an empty string when *input_paths* is empty (no-op).\n    \"\"\"\n    if not input_paths:\n        return \"\"\n\n    blocks: list[str] = []\n    for p in input_paths:\n        content = Path(p).read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        blocks.append(f\"# {p}\\n```\\n{content}\\n```\")\n    return \"\\n\\n\".join(blocks)\n\n\ndef _sanitize_output(output_file: Path) -> int:\n    \"\"\"Strip conversational preamble and leading blank lines before YAML\n    frontmatter in a step output file.\n\n    Reads the file, strips leading blank lines, finds the first ``^---``\n    line, and removes everything before it.  Uses atomic write (write to\n    ``.tmp`` then ``os.replace()``) to prevent partial file states.\n\n    Returns the byte count of the stripped content (0 when file already\n    starts with ``---`` and has no preamble).\n    \"\"\"\n    import os\n    import re\n\n    try:\n        raw = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    except FileNotFoundError:\n        return 0\n\n    # Strip leading blank lines (LLMs sometimes emit \\n\\n before ---)\n    content = raw.lstrip(\"\\n\\r\\t \")\n\n    # Already starts with frontmatter delimiter -- no preamble to strip\n    if content.startswith(\"---\"):\n        if content == raw:\n            return 0\n        # Leading whitespace only -- write back the stripped version\n        preamble_bytes = len(raw.encode(\"utf-8\")) - len(content.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n        tmp_file = output_file.with_suffix(output_file.suffix + \".tmp\")\n        tmp_file.write_text(content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        os.replace(tmp_file, output_file)\n        _log.info(\n            \"Stripped %d-byte leading whitespace from %s\",\n            preamble_bytes,\n            output_file,\n        )\n        return preamble_bytes\n\n    # Search for the first ^--- line (conversational preamble case)\n    match = re.search(r\"^---[ \\t]*$\", content, re.MULTILINE)\n    if match is None:\n        # No frontmatter found at all -- leave file unchanged\n        return 0\n\n    preamble = content[: match.start()]\n    cleaned = content[match.start():]\n    # Total bytes stripped = leading whitespace + conversational preamble\n    preamble_bytes = len(raw.encode(\"utf-8\")) - len(cleaned.encode(\"utf-8\"))\n\n    # Atomic write: tmp file + os.replace\n    tmp_file = output_file.with_suffix(output_file.suffix + \".tmp\")\n    tmp_file.write_text(cleaned, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    os.replace(tmp_file, output_file)\n\n    _log.info(\"Stripped %d-byte preamble from %s\", preamble_bytes, output_file)\n    return preamble_bytes\n\n\ndef _inject_pipeline_diagnostics(\n    output_file: Path,\n    started_at: datetime,\n    finished_at: datetime,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Inject executor-populated pipeline_diagnostics into extraction frontmatter.\n\n    The LLM cannot reliably produce execution timing or environment metadata,\n    so the executor injects these fields post-subprocess (FR-033).\n    \"\"\"\n    content = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").lstrip(\"\\n\\r\\t \")\n    if not content.startswith(\"---\"):\n        return\n\n    # Find end of frontmatter\n    end_idx = content.find(\"\\n---\", 3)\n    if end_idx == -1:\n        return\n\n    # Idempotency: skip if already injected\n    frontmatter = content[3:end_idx]\n    if \"pipeline_diagnostics:\" in frontmatter:\n        return\n\n    elapsed = (finished_at - started_at).total_seconds()\n    diagnostics_line = (\n        f\"pipeline_diagnostics: \"\n        f\"{{elapsed_seconds: {elapsed:.1f}, \"\n        f'started_at: \"{started_at.isoformat()}\", '\n        f'finished_at: \"{finished_at.isoformat()}\"}}'\n    )\n\n    # Insert before the closing ---\n    new_content = content[:end_idx] + \"\\n\" + diagnostics_line + content[end_idx:]\n    output_file.write_text(new_content, encoding=\"utf-8\")\n\n\ndef _inject_provenance_fields(\n    output_file: Path,\n    spec_source: str,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Inject provenance fields into test-strategy frontmatter.\n\n    The test-strategy step's LLM output may omit provenance metadata\n    (spec_source, generated, generator) since the prompt focuses on\n    test strategy content. The executor injects these fields post-subprocess\n    to satisfy TEST_STRATEGY_GATE required_frontmatter_fields.\n    \"\"\"\n    content = output_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").lstrip(\"\\n\\r\\t \")\n    if not content.startswith(\"---\"):\n        return\n\n    # Find end of frontmatter\n    end_idx = content.find(\"\\n---\", 3)\n    if end_idx == -1:\n        return\n\n    # Idempotency: only inject fields that are missing\n    frontmatter = content[3:end_idx]\n\n    fields_to_inject = []\n    if \"spec_source:\" not in frontmatter:\n        fields_to_inject.append(f\"spec_source: {spec_source}\")\n    if \"generated:\" not in frontmatter:\n        generated = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\n        fields_to_inject.append(f'generated: \"{generated}\"')\n    if \"generator:\" not in frontmatter:\n        fields_to_inject.append(\"generator: superclaude-roadmap-executor\")\n\n    if not fields_to_inject:\n        return  # \"\"\"Format HALT diagnostic output per spec section 6.2.\"\"\"\n    failed = [r for r in results if r.status in (StepStatus.FAIL, StepStatus.TIMEOUT)]\n    passed = [r for r in results if r.status == StepStatus.PASS]\n    cancelled = [r for r in results if r.status == StepStatus.CANCELLED]\n\n    if not failed:\n        return \"\"\n\n    fail = failed[-1]\n    step = fail.step\n\n    # Calculate
