len(self.all_findings)\n   102→\n   103→    @property\n   104→    def unsuppressed_findings(self) -> list[WiringFinding]:\n   105→        \"\"\"Findings not suppressed by whitelist.\"\"\"\n   106→        return [f for f in self.all_findings if not f.suppressed]\n   107→\n   108→    @property\n   109→    def clean(self) -> bool:\n   110→        \"\"\"True if zero unsuppressed findings regardless of rollout mode.\"\"\"\n   111→        return len(self.unsuppressed_findings) == 0\n   112→\n   113→    def blocking_count(self, mode: str | None = None) -> int:\n   114→        \"\"\"Count of findings that block the gate for the given rollout mode.\n   115→\n   116→        Args:\n   117→            mode: Override rollout mode. Uses self.rollout_mode if None.\n   118→\n   119→        Returns:\n   120→            Number of blocking findings per OQ-6:\n   121→            - shadow: 0 (never blocks)\n   122→            - soft: critical unsuppressed only\n   123→            - full: critical + major unsuppressed\n   124→        \"\"\"\n   125→        effective_mode = mode or self.rollout_mode\n   126→        unsuppressed = self.unsuppressed_findings\n   127→\n   128→        if effective_mode == \"shadow\":\n   129→            return 0\n   130→        elif effective_mode == \"soft\":\n   131→            return sum(1 for f in unsuppressed if f.severity == \"critical\")\n   132→        else:  # full\n   133→            return sum(\n   134→                1 for f in unsuppressed if f.severity in (\"critical\", \"major\")\n   135→            )\n   136→\n   137→\n   138→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   139→# Analyzers (sections 5.2.1, 5.2.2, 5.2.3)\n   140→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   141→\n   142→\n   143→def _collect_python_files(\n   144→    source_dir: Path,\n   145→    exclude_patterns: list[str],\n   146→) -> tuple[list[Path], int]:\n   147→    \"\"\"Collect Python files from source_dir, applying exclude patterns.\n   148→\n   149→    Returns:\n   150→        Tuple of (included files, skipped count).\n   151→    \"\"\"\n   152→    included: list[Path] = []\n   153→    skipped = 0\n   154→\n   155→    for py_file in sorted(source_dir.rglob(\"*.py\")):\n   156→        name = py_file.name\n   157→        if any(fnmatch(name, pat) for pat in exclude_patterns):\n   158→            skipped += 1\n   159→            continue\n   160→        included.append(py_file)\n   161→\n   162→    return included, skipped\n   163→\n   164→\n   165→def _safe_parse(file_path: Path) -> ast.Module | None:\n   166→    \"\"\"Parse a Python file, returning None on SyntaxError (R2 mitigation).\"\"\"\n   167→    try:\n   168→        source = file_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n   169→        return ast.parse(source, filename=str(file_path))\n   170→    except SyntaxError as exc:\n   171→        logger.warning(\"SyntaxError parsing %s: %s — skipping\", file_path, exc)\n   172→        return None\n   173→    except (OSError, UnicodeDecodeError) as exc:\n   174→        logger.warning(\"Cannot read %s: %s — skipping\", file_path, exc)\n   175→        return None\n   176→\n   177→\n   178→def _apply_whitelist(\n   179→    findings: list[WiringFinding],\n   180→    whitelist: list[WhitelistEntry],\n   181→) -> list[WiringFinding]:\n   182→    \"\"\"Mark findings as suppressed if they match a whitelist entry.\"\"\"\n   183→    suppressed_symbols = {\n   184→        (e.symbol, e.finding_type) for e in whitelist\n   185→    }\n   186→    for finding in findings:\n   187→        if (finding.symbol_name, finding.finding_type) in suppressed_symbols:\n   188→            finding.suppressed = True\n   189→            finding.severity = \"info\"\n   190→    return findings\n   191→\n   192→\n   193→# --- G-001: Unwired Callable Analysis (section 5.2.1) ---\n   194→\n   195→\n   196→def _extract_injectable_params(\n   197→    tree: ast.Module, file_path: str\n   198→) -> list[tuple[str, str, int]]:\n   199→    \"\"\"Extract Optional[Callable] constructor parameters.\n   200→\n   201→    Returns list of (class_name.param_name, param_name, line_number).\n   202→    \"\"\"\n   203→    injectables: list[tuple[str, str, int]] = []\n   204→\n   205→    for node in ast.walk(tree):\n   206→        if not isinstance(node, ast.ClassDef):\n   207→            continue\n   208→\n   209→        for item in node.body:\n   210→            if not isinstance(item, ast.FunctionDef) or item.name != \"__init__\":\n   211→                continue\n   212→\n   213→            for arg in item.args.args:\n   214→                if arg.arg == \"self\":\n   215→                    continue\n   216→                annotation = arg.annotation\n   217→                if annotation is None:\n   218→                    continue\n   219→\n   220→                # Check for Optional[Callable] or Callable | None\n   221→                if _is_optional_callable(annotation):\n   222→                    # Verify default is None\n   223→                    if _has_none_default(item, arg):\n   224→                        symbol = f\"{node.name}.{arg.arg}\"\n   225→                        injectables.append(\n   226→                            (symbol, arg.arg, arg.lineno)\n   227→                        )\n   228→\n   229→    return injectables\n   230→\n   231→\n   232→def _is_optional_callable(annotation: ast.expr) -> bool:\n   233→    \"\"\"Check if an annotation represents Optional[Callable] or Callable | None.\"\"\"\n   234→    # Optional[Callable] -> Subscript(Name('Optional'), Name('Callable'))\n   235→    if isinstance(annotation, ast.Subscript):\n   236→        if isinstance(annotation.value, ast.Name) and annotation.value.id == \"Optional\":\n   237→            slice_val = annotation.slice\n   238→            if isinstance(slice_val, ast.Name) and slice_val.id == \"Callable\":\n   239→                return True\n   240→            # Optional[Callable[...]]\n   241→            if isinstance(slice_val, ast.Subscript):\n   242→                if isinstance(slice_val.value, ast.Name) and slice_val.value.id == \"Callable\":\n   243→                    return True\n   244→        # Also check Attribute form: typing.Optional\n   245→        if isinstance(annotation.value, ast.Attribute):\n   246→            if annotation.value.attr == \"Optional\":\n   247→                slice_val = annotation.slice\n   248→                if isinstance(slice_val, ast.Name) and slice_val.id == \"Callable\":\n   249→                    return True\n   250→\n   251→    # Callable | None -> BinOp(Name('Callable'), BitOr, Constant(None))\n   252→    if isinstance(annotation, ast.BinOp) and isinstance(annotation.op, ast.BitOr):\n   253→        left, right = annotation.left, annotation.right\n   254→        if (isinstance(left, ast.Name) and left.id == \"Callable\" and\n   255→                isinstance(right, ast.Constant) and right.value is None):\n   256→            return True\n   257→        if (isinstance(right, ast.Name) and right.id == \"Callable\" and\n   258→                isinstance(left, ast.Constant) and left.value is None):\n   259→            return True\n   260→\n   261→    return False\n   262→\n   263→\n   264→def _has_none_default(func_def: ast.FunctionDef, target_arg: ast.arg) -> bool:\n   265→    \"\"\"Check if the given arg has a default value of None.\"\"\"\n   266→    args = func_def.args\n   267→    # defaults apply to the last N positional args\n   268→    all_args = args.args\n   269→    defaults = args.defaults\n   270→\n   271→    if not defaults:\n   272→        return False\n   273→\n   274→    # Map defaults to args (right-aligned)\n   275→    offset = len(all_args) - len(defaults)\n   276→    for i, arg in enumerate(all_args):\n   277→        if arg is target_arg:\n   278→            default_idx = i - offset\n   279→            if 0 <= default_idx < len(defaults):\n   280→                default = defaults[default_idx]\n   281→                return isinstance(default, ast.Constant) and default.value is None\n   282→    return False\n   283→\n   284→\n   285→def _find_wiring_call_sites(\n   286→    param_name: str,\n   287→    class_name: str,\n   288→    trees: dict[Path, ast.Module],\n   289→) -> bool:\n   290→    \"\"\"Search for call sites that explicitly provide the parameter by keyword.\"\"\"\n   291→    for tree in trees.values():\n   292→        for node in ast.walk(tree):\n   293→            if isinstance(node, ast.Call):\n   294→                # Check keyword arguments\n   295→                for kw in node.keywords:\n   296→                    if kw.arg == param_name:\n   297→                        # Verify it's calling the right class\n   298→                        if _is_class_call(node, class_name):\n   299→                            return True\n   300→    return False\n   301→\n   302→\n   303→def _is_class_call(call: ast.Call, class_name: str) -> bool:\n   304→    \"\"\"Check if a Call node is calling the specified class.\"\"\"\n   305→    func = call.func\n   306→    if isinstance(func, ast.Name) and func.id == class_name:\n   307→        return True\n   308→    if isinstance(func, ast.Attribute) and func.attr == class_name:\n   309→        return True\n   310→    return False\n   311→\n   312→\n   313→def analyze_unwired_callables(\n   314→    config: WiringConfig,\n   315→    source_dir: Path,\n   316→) -> list[WiringFinding]:\n   317→    \"\"\"Detect injectable callables (Optional[Callable]) that are never wired.\n   318→\n   319→    Algorithm (section 5.2.1):\n   320→    1. Parse all Python files, extract Optional[Callable] constructor params\n   321→    2. For each injectable, search all files for keyword call sites\n   322→    3. If zero providers, emit unwired_callable finding\n   323→    4. Apply whitelist suppression\n   324→\n   325→    Args:\n   326→        config: Wiring analysis configuration.\n   327→        source_dir: Root directory to scan.\n   328→\n   329→    Returns:\n   330→        List of WiringFinding with finding_type=\"unwired_callable\".\n   331→    \"\"\"\n   332→    files, _ = _collect_python_files(source_dir, config.exclude_patterns)\n   333→    trees: dict[Path, ast.Module] = {}\n   334→\n   335→    for f in files:\n   336→        tree = _safe_parse(f)\n   337→        if tree is not None:\n   338→            trees[f] = tree\n   339→\n   340→    findings: list[WiringFinding] = []\n   341→\n   342→    for file_path, tree in trees.items():\n   343→        injectables = _extract_injectable_params(tree, str(file_path))\n   344→        for symbol, param_name, lineno in injectables:\n   345→            class_name = symbol.split(\".\")[0]\n   346→            if not _find_wiring_call_sites(param_name, class_name, trees):\n   347→                findings.append(\n   348→                    WiringFinding(\n   349→                        finding_type=\"unwired_callable\",\n   350→                        file_path=str(file_path),\n   351→                        symbol_name=symbol,\n   352→                        line_number=lineno,\n   353→                        detail=(\n   354→                            f\"Parameter '{param_name}' typed Optional[Callable] \"\n   355→                            f\"with default None is never wired by any call site\"\n   356→                        ),\n   357→                        severity=\"critical\",\n   358→                    )\n   359→                )\n   360→\n   361→    # Apply whitelist\n   362→    whitelist = load_whitelist(config.whitelist_path, config.rollout_mode)\n   363→    return _apply_whitelist(findings, whitelist)\n   364→\n   365→\n   366→# --- G-002: Orphan Module Analysis (section 5.2.2) ---\n   367→\n   368→\n   369→def _find_provider_dirs(source_dir: Path, provider_names: frozenset[str]) -> list[Path]:\n   370→    \"\"\"Find directories matching provider_dir_names convention.\"\"\"\n   371→    providers: list[Path] = []\n   372→    for d in sorted(source_dir.rglob(\"*\")):\n   373→        if d.is_dir() and d.name in provider_names:\n   374→            providers.append(d)\n   375→    return providers\n   376→\n   377→\n   378→def _extract_imports(tree: ast.Module) -> set[str]:\n   379→    \"\"\"Extract all imported module/symbol names from an AST.\"\"\"\n   380→    imports: set[str] = set()\n   381→    for node in ast.walk(tree):\n   382→        if isinstance(node, ast.Import):\n   383→            for alias in node.names:\n   384→                imports.add(alias.name)\n   385→        elif isinstance(node, ast.ImportFrom):\n   386→            if node.module:\n   387→                imports.add(node.module)\n   388→                for alias in node.names:\n   389→                    imports.add(f\"{node.module}.{alias.name}\")\n   390→    return imports\n   391→\n   392→\n   393→def analyze_orphan_modules(\n   394→    config: WiringConfig,\n   395→    source_dir: Path,\n   396→) -> list[WiringFinding]:\n   397→    \"\"\"Detect Python modules in provider directories with zero inbound imports.\n   398→\n   399→    Algorithm (section 5.2.2):\n   400→    1. Find provider directories by config.provider_dir_names convention\n   401→    2. For each Python file in provider dirs, check if any file outside\n   402→       the provider dir imports it\n   403→    3. If zero importers, emit orphan_module finding\n   404→    4. Apply exclude_patterns and whitelist suppression\n   405→\n   406→    Args:\n   407→        config: Wiring analysis configuration.\n   408→        source_dir: Root directory to scan.\n   409→\n   410→    Returns:\n   411→        List of WiringFinding with finding_type=\"orphan_module\".\n   412→    \"\"\"\n   413→    provider_dirs = _find_provider_dirs(source_dir, config.provider_dir_names)\n   414→    if not provider_dirs:\n   415→        return []\n   416→\n   417→    # Collect all Python files and parse them for imports\n   418→    all_files, _ = _collect_python_files(source_dir, [])\n   419→    all_imports: set[str] = set()\n   420→    trees: dict[Path, ast.Module] = {}\n   421→\n   422→    for f in all_files:\n   423→        tree = _safe_parse(f)\n   424→        if tree is not None:\n   425→            trees[f] = tree\n   426→\n   427→    # Gather imports from files OUTSIDE provider directories\n   428→    for f, tree in trees.items():\n   429→        is_in_provider = any(\n   430→            f.resolve().is_relative_to(pd.resolve()) for pd in provider_dirs\n   431→        )\n   432→        if not is_in_provider:\n   433→            all_imports.update(_extract_imports(tree))\n   434→\n   435→    findings: list[WiringFinding] = []\n   436→\n   437→    for provider_dir in provider_dirs:\n   438→        for py_file in sorted(provider_dir.glob(\"*.py\")):\n   439→            # Apply exclude patterns\n   440→            if any(fnmatch(py_file.name, pat) for pat in config.exclude_patterns):\n   441→                continue\n   442→\n   443→            # Derive the module path components\n   444→            try:\n   445→                rel = py_file.relative_to(source_dir)\n   446→            except ValueError:\n   447→                continue\n   448→\n   449→            # Build possible import names\n   450→            module_stem = py_file.stem\n   451→            module_dotpath = str(rel.with_suffix(\"\")).replace(\"/\", \".\").replace(\"\\\\\", \".\")\n   452→\n   453→            # Check if any external file imports this module\n   454→            is_imported = False\n   455→            for imp in all_imports:\n   456→                if module_dotpath in imp or module_stem in imp:\n   457→                    is_imported = True\n   458→                    break\n   459→\n   460→            if not is_imported:\n   461→                findings.append(\n   462→                    WiringFinding(\n   463→                        finding_type=\"orphan_module\",\n   464→                        file_path=str(py_file),\n   465→                        symbol_name=module_dotpath,\n   466→                        line_number=1,\n   467→                        detail=(\n   468→                            f\"Module '{module_dotpath}' in provider directory \"\n   469→                            f\"'{provider_dir.name}' has zero inbound imports\"\n   470→                        ),\n   471→                        severity=\"major\",\n   472→                    )\n   473→                )\n   474→\n   475→    # Apply whitelist\n   476→    whitelist = load_whitelist(config.whitelist_path, config.rollout_mode)\n   477→    return _apply_whitelist(findings, whitelist)\n   478→\n   479→\n   480→# --- G-003: Registry Analysis (section 5.2.3) ---\n   481→\n   482→\n   483→def _matches_registry_pattern(\n   484→    name: str, patterns: tuple[re.Pattern[str], ...]\n   485→) -> bool:\n   486→    \"\"\"Check if a variable name matches any registry detection pattern.\"\"\"\n   487→    return any(p.match(name) for p in patterns)\n   488→\n   489→\n   490→def _resolve_name_in_scope(\n   491→    name: str, tree: ast.Module, all_imports: set[str]\n   492→) -> bool:\n   493→    \"\"\"Check if a name is resolvable: defined in module or importable.\"\"\"\n   494→    # Check module-level definitions\n   495→    for node in ast.iter_child_nodes(tree):\n   496→        if isinstance(node, (ast.FunctionDef, ast.AsyncFunctionDef)):\n   497→            if node.name == name:\n   498→                return True\n   499→        elif isinstance(node, ast.ClassDef):\n   500→            if node.name == name:\n   501→                return True\n   502→        elif isinstance(node, ast.Assign):\n   503→            for target in node.targets:\n   504→                if isinstance(target, ast.Name) and target.id == name:\n   505→                    return True\n   506→\n   507→    # Check if importable\n   508→    if name in all_imports:\n   509→        return True\n   510→\n   511→    return False\n   512→\n   513→\n   514→def analyze_registries(\n   515→    config: WiringConfig,\n   516→    source_dir: Path,\n   517→) -> list[WiringFinding]:\n   518→    \"\"\"Detect broken dispatch registry entries referencing nonexistent callables.\n   519→\n   520→    Algorithm (section 5.2.3):\n   521→    1. Scan files for module-level dict assignments matching registry patterns\n   522→    2. For each dict entry, verify the value references an importable callable\n   523→    3. Unresolved references emit unwired_registry findings\n   524→\n   525→    Args:\n   526→        config: Wiring analysis configuration.\n   527→        source_dir: Root directory to scan.\n   528→\n   529→    Returns:\n   530→        List of WiringFinding with finding_type=\"unwired_registry\".\n   531→    \"\"\"\n   532→    files, _ = _collect_python_files(source_dir, config.exclude_patterns)\n   533→    trees: dict[Path, ast.Module] = {}\n   534→    all_imports_by_file: dict[Path, set[str]] = {}\n   535→\n   536→    for f in files:\n   537→        tree = _safe_parse(f)\n   538→        if tree is not None:\n   539→            trees[f] = tree\n   540→            all_imports_by_file[f] = _extract_imports(tree)\n   541→\n   542→    findings: list[WiringFinding] = []\n   543→\n   544→    for file_path, tree in trees.items():\n   545→        file_imports = all_imports_by_file.get(file_path, set())\n   546→\n   547→        for node in ast.iter_child_nodes(tree):\n   548→            if not isinstance(node, ast.Assign):\n   549→                continue\n   550→\n   551→            for target in node.targets:\n   552→                if not isinstance(target, ast.Name):\n   553→                    continue\n   554→                if not _matches_registry_pattern(target.id, config.registry_patterns):\n   555→                    continue\n   556→\n   557→                # This is a registry assignment — check if value is a dict\n   558→                if not isinstance(node.value, ast.Dict):\n   559→                    continue\n   560→\n   561→                for key_node, val_node in zip(node.value.keys, node.value.values):\n   562→                    if val_node is None:\n   563→                        continue\n   564→\n   565→                    # Explicit None is allowed\n   566→                    if isinstance(val_node, ast.Constant) and val_node.value is None:\n   567→                        continue\n   568→\n   569→                    # Name reference — must be resolvable\n   570→                    if isinstance(val_node, ast.Name):\n   571→                        if not _resolve_name_in_scope(\n   572→                            val_node.id, tree, file_imports\n   573→                        ):\n   574→                            key_repr = (\n   575→                                ast.literal_eval(key_node)\n   576→                                if isinstance(key_node, ast.Constant)\n   577→                                else \"<dynamic>\"\n   578→                            )\n   579→                            findings.append(\n   580→                                WiringFinding(\n   581→                                    finding_type=\"unwired_registry\",\n   582→                                    file_path=str(file_path),\n   583→                                    symbol_name=f\"{target.id}[{key_repr!r}]\",\n   584→                                    line_number=val_node.lineno,\n   585→                                    detail=(\n   586→                                        f\"Registry '{target.id}' entry \"\n   587→                                        f\"'{key_repr}' references unresolvable \"\n   588→                                        f\"callable '{val_node.id}'\"\n   589→                                    ),\n   590→                                    severity=\"critical\",\n   591→                                )\n   592→                            )\n   593→\n   594→                    # String reference — try to resolve as dotted path\n   595→                    elif isinstance(val_node, ast.Constant) and isinstance(\n   596→                        val_node.value, str\n   597→                    ):\n   598→                        ref = val_node.value\n   599→                        parts = ref.rsplit(\".\", 1)\n   600→                        func_name = parts[-1] if len(parts) > 1 else ref\n   601→                        if not _resolve_name_in_scope(\n   602→                            func_name, tree, file_imports\n   603→                        ):\n   604→                            key_repr = (\n   605→                                ast.literal_eval(key_node)\n   606→                                if isinstance(key_node, ast.Constant)\n   607→                                else \"<dynamic>\"\n   608→                            )\n   609→                            findings.append(\n   610→                                WiringFinding(\n   611→                                    finding_type=\"unwired_registry\",\n   612→                                    file_path=str(file_path),\n   613→                                    symbol_name=f\"{target.id}[{key_repr!r}]\",\n   614→                                    line_number=val_node.lineno,\n   615→                                    detail=(\n   616→                                        f\"Registry '{target.id}' entry \"\n   617→                                        f\"'{key_repr}' references unresolvable \"\n   618→                                        f\"string path '{ref}'\"\n   619→                                    ),\n   620→                                    severity=\"critical\",\n   621→                                )\n   622→                            )\n   623→\n   624→    # Apply whitelist\n   625→    whitelist = load_whitelist(config.whitelist_path, config.rollout_mode)\n   626→    return _apply_whitelist(findings, whitelist)\n   627→\n   628→\n   629→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   630→# Top-level entry point\n   631→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   632→\n   633→\n   634→def run_wiring_analysis(\n   635→    config: WiringConfig,\n   636→    source_dir: Path,\n   637→) -> WiringReport:\n   638→    \"\"\"Run all three wiring analyzers and produce a WiringReport.\n   639→\n   640→    Args:\n   641→        config: Wiring analysis configuration.\n   642→        source_dir: Root directory to scan.\n   643→\n   644→    Returns:\n   645→        WiringReport aggregating findings from all analyzers.\n   646→    \"\"\"\n   647→    start = time.monotonic()\n   648→\n   649→    _, skipped = _collect_python_files(source_dir, config.exclude_patterns)\n   650→    files, _ = _collect_python_files(source_dir, config.exclude_patterns)\n   651→    files_analyzed = len(files)\n   652→\n   653→    unwired = analyze_unwired_callables(config, source_dir)\n   654→    orphans = analyze_orphan_modules(config, source_dir)\n   655→    registries = analyze_registries(config, source_dir)\n   656→\n   657→    duration = time.monotonic() - start\n   658→\n   659→    return WiringReport(\n   660→        target_dir=str(source_dir),\n   661→        files_analyzed=files_analyzed,\n   662→        files_skipped=skipped,\n   663→        unwired_callables=unwired,\n   664→        orphan_modules=orphans,\n   665→        unwired_registries=registries,\n   666→        scan_duration_seconds=round(duration, 4),\n   667→        rollout_mode=config.rollout_mode,\n   668→    )\n   669→\n   670→\n   671→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   672→# Report emission (section 5.4)\n   673→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   674→\n   675→\n   676→def emit_report(report: WiringReport, output_path: Path) -> Path:\n   677→    \"\"\"Write a wiring verification report with YAML frontmatter and 7 Markdown sections.\n   678→\n   679→    Frontmatter contains 16 fields per section 5.6. String fields are serialized\n   680→    with yaml.safe_dump() to prevent YAML injection (section 5.4).\n   681→\n   682→    Args:\n   683→        report: Aggregated wiring analysis report.\n   684→        output_path: Destination file path.\n   685→\n   686→    Returns:\n   687→        The output_path after writing.\n   688→    \"\"\"\n   689→    # Compute severity counts from unsuppressed findings\n   690→    unsuppressed = report.unsuppressed_findings\n   691→    critical_count = sum(1 for f in unsuppressed if f.severity == \"critical\")\n   692→    major_count = sum(1 for f in unsuppressed if f.severity == \"major\")\n   693→    info_count = sum(1 for f in unsuppressed if f.severity == \"info\")\n   694→    suppressed_count = sum(1 for f in report.all_findings if f.suppressed)\n   695→\n   696→    # TestCrossRefsResolve:\n    def test_cross_refs_resolve_valid(self):\n        \"\"\"Valid cross-references resolve to existing headings.\"\"\"\n        content = (\n            \"# Document\\n\"\n            \"## 1.0 Introduction\\n\"\n            \"### 1.1 Overview\\n\"\n            \"See section 1.1 for details.\\n\"\n        )\n        assert _cross_refs_resolve(content) is True\n\n    def test_cross_refs_resolve_invalid(self):\n        \"\"\"Dangling cross-references emit warnings but return True (warning-only mode).\"\"\"\n        content = \"# Document\\n## 1.0 Introduction\\nSee section 9.9 for details.\\n\"\n        import warnings\n\n        with warnings.catch_warnings(record=True) as w:\n            warnings.simplefilter(\"always\")\n            result = _cross_refs_resolve(content)\n            # Warning-only mode: returns True despite dangling ref\n            assert result is True\n            # But a warning was emitted\n            assert len(w) == 1\n            assert \"9.9\" in str(w[0].message)\n\n    def test_cross_refs_resolve_no_refs(self):\n        \"\"\"Documents with no cross-references pass without warnings.\"\"\"\n        content = (\n            \"# Document\\n## Introduction\\nThis document has no cross-references.\\n\"\n        )\n        import warnings\n\n        with warnings.catch_warnings(record=True) as w:\n
